game_name: The NOexistenceN of you AND me
review_text:
i think the target audience is people younger than 20 years old who are just beginning to form their own concepts of self-identity and how they relate with the outside world. if you're that audience, this might be a good pick.

sadly the game does not respect you if you've already done your fair share of introspection and have already resolved your early-20s struggles with existentialism. it fails the basic rule of "show, don't tell", as it spoonfeeds you the ideas that the author wants you to take away. like, it literally lists them at the end: "oh, this scene was about this idea, that scene was about that concept, and this is should be your takeaway from the game". 

as it had 96% positive reviews, i had a lot of expectations. i really tried to immerse myself while reading it. zero distractions, zero alttabbing, maximum focus in reading and understanding every single line of dialogue. even took my time to reflect in-between the chapters. it just falls flat if you're not the target audience.

but game is cheap and the art is good and lilith looks cute, so it's not really a bad game to pick up. i just dont think it really deserves the overwhelmingly positive rating.

game_name: The NOexistenceN of you AND me
review_text:
This visual novel is less of a story and more of a philosophical thesis that the author had tried to convey. Unfortunately, the ideas it puts forward are fairly banal to anyone who's gone through at least one existential crisis and done a bit of self-reflection, and it isn't even presented in a compelling way. It feels like the author was so desperate to subvert your immersion that in their rush they have utterly forgotten to get you immersed to begin with. Nothing that's supposed to be happening diegetically is ever properly contextualized: not the world, not the character of Lilith, not even you, the player. At first the game wants you to play the role of a protagonist, then at the end it "breaks the 4th wall" by referring to the player directly, yet the distinguishing line between the protagonist and the player is not drawn at any point. You're thrown into interacting with Lilith and given options for strong sympathetic or antagonistic choices without establishing any motivation to lean either way, other than "it's a visual novel so you're supposed to get the girl", I guess (and no, I don't believe that knowing the full story sufficiently recontextualizes the early narrative). The majority of the scenes are disjointed and barely justified, which isn't inherently a bad thing, but here it just feels like badly structured writing, existing only as a means to deliver a poorly disguised message. To add insult to injury, the game beats you over the head with its symbolism by literally explaining it all to you scene by scene in the last chapter. I was not interested in repeated playthroughs to see the other endings because it's pretty obvious what the other possibilities are.

To be honest, at first I was intrigued and amused by the game's apparent quirkiness and self-awareness, but unfortunately it just doesn't go anywhere worth exploring. It's too short and not nearly as smart as it wants to be. I feel like in some respects it does a disservice to the underlying questions it circles around by providing a very shallow, singular interpretation of them with no subtlety or room for nuance. And the annoying part is, I can totally see the vision for what this narrative could have been in the right hands, but then it would've needed to be a 30 hour read, not a 3 hour one.

As for some positives, the art and the voice acting are generally quite good. But I find the inclusion of an AI-generated song in such a short title baffling, especially considering they also use what seems to be a fully original song later on. I'm not against generative AI in principle, but they could've used any royalty-free track for this particular use case instead.

game_name: The NOexistenceN of you AND me
review_text:
There is an interesting story here. It's just that it's being explained to you rather than narrated.
There's a difference between games having their own unique narrative tools, frame and language and outright discarding narration.
The Nonexistence of you and me is a philosophical essay that is more concerned about the reader understanding its thesis than leading them into it organically: "that was chapter 1", the cute anime girl says, "that chapter was about talking about the self. But in chapter 2, we talked about individuals" and so on, and so forth.
Cut away half the lines of dialogue so that the actual interactions between you and the girl take center focus, and you get a novel that makes you ponder on its themes. As it is, it's a one-way monologue accompanied by a nice soundtrack and an ending that takes control away from you and ends up contraddicting the very fundaments of its philosophy

game_name: The NOexistenceN of you AND me
review_text:
Minor spoilers for the games theme's below.

The noexistencen of you and me is a visual novel that tries to have a deep message but to me comes across as shallow. The game wants to sell me truths like "people often have secrets", "in fictional stories you choose to immerse yourself in a world that isnt real" and "people often have their own subjective experiences about events" as somehow being profound never before heard of observations, but they're not. A child knows of these things. Additionally, whilst playing you get constantly reminded you are playing a game and that I should always be thinking about the fact that I am playing a game, but that just ruins my interest in learning about the characters and in caring about what happens next. Does the game want me to care about Lilith or not? Odd choice for a visual novel, if I don't see this world as real or these characters as alive, then I am not invested, then I feel nothing as things happen. If I am reminded that this is purely a work of fiction that satirises dating sims where nothing important will happen whether I continue reading or I don't, then I think you have royally messed up as a story teller because eventually the player will just believe it and stop playing. 

I should also note that this game was clearly made for a cn audience, and the english localization is pretty clunky at times. It's fine when there's simple back and forth dialogue but as soon as the themes of the game are being explained to you I found some of the dialogue to be very difficult to understand. Here's an except from the first 5 mins of the game "[spoiler]The existence of certain cakes is not known to people in a literal sense, yet their very being points to the existence of other cakes in a symbolic way—that is, the belief in the existence of ovens serves as faith or a hypothesis for the existence of time machines (and an inexplicable motive at that) experienced by people.[/spoiler]". This is meaningless. I have zero guesses as to what this paragraph means. It could be a moot point as to whether or not it matters that I understand the words being put in front of me in a different game, but I don't think it is in this case because I think in a visual novel that's trying to explore how we perceive stories you should be presented with legible sentences.

game_name: The NOexistenceN of you AND me
review_text:
I would ask for a refund, but it feels silly to do that after already completing the game.
Good part: Lilith’s design and her dialogue when she’s not in teacher mode are a magnificent part of the game. But…
Boring part: the whole point of the game of [Spoilers department] is dull and not really appealing. I ended up skipping most of the ending once I fully understood the plot, which made it feel even more boring than before - there’s simply no reason to continue when you already know everything Lilith is going to tell you anyway.

Once again, a Chinese VN with good visuals and interesting characters, but without truly fascinating storytelling. I only completed one of the three endings, and I have no interest in replaying it.

game_name: The NOexistenceN of you AND me
review_text:
[h1][b]EXISTENCIAL CRISIS: THE GAME[/b][/h1]

Really cool concept, but not so good of an execution.
It explains [i]everything[/i] to you, not letting you ponder on the information given to you.

Nonetheless, it's a pretty cheap game, so you can just buy it and see for yourself whether you like it or not and come to your own conclusions.

game_name: The NOexistenceN of you AND me
review_text: bought it for like 50 cents which is about what its worth. Pretty boring and doesn't really make a whole lot of sense

game_name: The NOexistenceN of you AND me
review_text:
This game tries to explore what it means to exist, asking whether a fictional character you connect with can feel more real than a stranger. It touches on themes of self, relationships and perception, showing that existence is tied to being seen and cared for. 
And Lilith DOES feel personal, with dialogue choices that land exactly the way the game intends. The game uses visuals, like the broken mirror in the main menu to reinforce the fragility of existence and connection.

And I've got to say that despite these ideas, I didn’t enjoy it, it felt like some a cheap knock-off, boring and repetitive.

game_name: The NOexistenceN of you AND me
review_text:
I expected more.

Honestly, most of the negative reviews already describe the issues pretty well, so I’ll keep this short.

I love the art style… but that’s about it. The rest of the game was either boring or frustrating.

[spoiler] And the bad ending is just awful. I went through the game trying to be supportive and positive, and somehow the result is that I’m forced to strangle this girl. After looking up explanations, it turns out a couple of completely harmless early choices can shove you straight into the bad ending [/spoiler]

And here’s the real issue: the game acts like choices matter, but most of them don’t change anything in the moment. On a lot of questions you can pick all four answers and get the exact same response. The only real difference shows up at the very end, and the good ending apparently even involves leaving the game open for 20 minutes doing nothing. No thank you.

So… should YOU play this game? Sure, if you’re curious. It’s two bucks, and you can refund it if you’re under two hours. I personally can’t recommend it, but clearly there are people out there who love this game and especially the main girl. I’m just not one of them — the writing and choice design completely killed it for me.

Edit ; I Just want to make very clear that i really, really tried to love this game. Even now i am sitting here thinking " man maybe i should just give it another try " but as soon as i think about what i would have to do to get the good or even the middle of the road ending... i cant bring myself to start it up again because its just simply frustrating.

game_name: The NOexistenceN of you AND me
review_text:
degenerate pseudo intellectual slop 
it tries to be more than it is, which i guess is the point of the game, but holy shit is it just hard to sit down and take seriously.

i guess if you enjoy pretending to talk to a kawaii anime girl then you could have more fun with it, but i cant really engage with it because the game is unplayable unless you humor it

in other words, its a visual novel that attempts to be something deep or introspective but just comes off as random, edgy at times, or needlessly cryptic in order to appear thought provoking, when all the ideas it espouses are really surface level and frankly stupid

you could have a similar thought provoking experience by visiting the friedrich nietzsche page on wikipedia. its totally philosophical and mind breaking, bro!

shits buns man
i will keep playing it to see if my opinion changes but its hard to even play because of how it wants you to humor it when its so obviously trying to push you in a way that is unsatisfactory and makes the game seem more cryptic or interesting than it really is.

im always open to being wrong, but i was very disappointed on initial playthrough, and i dont know if i will be satisfied in future playthroughs. 

2/10 for 2 dollars

review_text:
This VISUAL NOVEL have an issues with Motor Function Accessibility!

Like in the first chapter there's a minigame where you need to click fast on disappearing words all over the screen. My hand trauma was never an issue in SOULSLIKES but this game is just won't let me get more than 50%. It requires like 5 clicks and about 10cm of zigzag mouse movement per second. Impossible on controller or touchpad even for people with healthy hands. I only managed by using both touchpad and mouse simultaneously (so I can move and click more)... and I won't be playing it anymore, not with how many tears I have shred.

Who the heck adds a DPS check in a VISUAL NOVEL? The devs are certainly sadistic.

game_name: The NOexistenceN of you AND me
review_text:
The art style and VAs are great considering the price, but the story/text felt so philosophical that I had no clue what it was trying to tell me and it failed to draw me in enough. It's also quite short (~2 hours), so there's very little build-up and it felt rushed at times.

It's also worth noting some accessibliltiy issues with a few scenes that have white text on white backgrounds that can be difficult to read for some.

game_name: The NOexistenceN of you AND me
review_text:
Fun for a one time run, but...

While it's still one of the first attempts at visual novels of the dev, and the game is kind of cheap, I just want to point out what I don't like, that could be avoided in next games:

- linear story, no impactful changes during the story, except for the ending
- story is (possibly unintended) designed around one-time consumption and then you're done, while there are several endings, by the time you get your last achievements, you really want to uninstall the game because of the monotony you need to get through
- main character essentially hanging on your neck since the very beginning, especially without any explained story behind it, is nauseating. Lack of "common sense" in human interactions makes it more annoying than attention grabbing in the long run.

game_name: The NOexistenceN of you AND me
review_text:
I can see what this game was trying to go for, especially in the first trailer and I was honestly excited to try it out hoping for a good think piece. But like many other people in the review I just honestly could not connect with Lilith at all.

 I understand the back and forth of her existence is the premise of the story. But even when trying to immerse myself into it im unsure if it was just the clunky dialogue that took me out of it or that we didnt have more time with her before thrown into that premise and philosophy. A character that does this well is Monkia from DDLC, before we had time to really dig into her we saw her as a real person and have a connection with her; a drive and reason to care and romance. Also despite doing what I thought was a neutral or good choices I ended up getting the bad ending first which really confused me and took me out of the game even more so. (It turns out not wanting to jump from a ferris wheel makes you an evil person) 

I do like two things from this VN however, stylistically and when going outside and having a conversation with someone. The idea the game was trying to portray (social anxiety, agoraphobia, etc.) was nice and the fact you are "saved" by your sweet anime girl Lilith is a good hint at her true purpose in the end. Other than that any part of trying to be thought provoking or deep is a really poor attempt as most of this is something youd end up thinking yourself when trying to fall asleep at 3 am. 

 (Also the fact that AI was used in this, even if it was just for a short song its really disappointing. If I double checked before putting this on my wishlist I wouldntve have put it on there to begin with.)

Is not as deep as i try to be, but can be a good introduction?

It's ok for the price, but the steam scores are binary, not third option.

The game itself is very interesting... But that's about it. It gives a nice atmosphere and an interesting world to explore... But the game falls flat at the very end because of its ending. As one would expect, this is a character driven story. The main conflict revolves around the character in questions, the MC you play as and the girl you talk to. If you play through the story, you'll see that there is some depth given to these two characters, but ultimately, the depth given to them is promptly ripped away as their character arcs is cut short. The problem surrounding the endings mainly stems from the fact that the MC completely disregards the female lead. 

Spoiler for the Ending [spoiler] The bad endings where MC dies is whatever. Those are bad endings so I am not expecting resolution there. But, for the "True Ending" where you uncover all the mysteries around the game and ultimately confront Peony with the truth... Moss just cures her and peaces out. There's no dialogue exchange between them. At best, I could attribute Moss dialogue to Peony as a villain who's monologueing to the protagonist. He just tells her that he figured out her plan and decides its simply not worth killing her anymore. He doesn't sympathize with her, talk through her frustrations, hear out her anger toward him for killing her sister. He did the bare minimum of apologizing (one sided, mind you) and just... leaves. Doesn't even look back. He just leaves. Now, I could see an argument being made that what he's doing is simply him being in character as someone who was a sociopath who tortured and murdered multiple innocent lives. The only problem with that is that the dialogue between Moss and Peony has been anything BUT sociopathic. [/spoiler]

[spoiler]Throughout the entire story, we see Peony completely open up to Moss. She tells him so much information like the mundane things in her life like her being a violinist and liking classical music to the sacred things in her life such as her little sister and her family trauma. Peony is practically trusting her life story to Moss and with it, I can't help but empathize with Peony. That's why, it's great that Moss actually responds to Peony good will with a same level of good faith. He responds to her with genuine empathy and shows compassion for her cause. In story, when Peony is breaking down from the shock of the password ending on -18 not being the answer, Moss levels with her and calmly talks her through her panic. He shows himself reasonable and faces Peony's troubles head on. He tells her that they'll get through this just like all the other clues. Later on, when Peony's sister's portrait is revealed from the locked drawer, Peony has a genuine meltdown and lashes out at Moss. She rightfully lashes out at him for being the cause of her sister death and is genuinely revealing how she truly feels about him. Yet, even when faced by a such a vehement hatred toward him, Moss shows immense levels of compassion. He doesn't dismiss Peony's hatred toward him and shows her complete sympathy for her cause. So much so that he expresses to Peony that he's not gonna leave her and will, in fact, help her find her sister. This level of compassion dismantled what little anger Peony had for Moss and for it, she places her full trust in him and tells him that sentiment. This whole interaction was so genuine and heartfelt that I couldn't help but scoff when I found out it amounted to nothing. [/spoiler]

[spoiler]The direction the story was going for was really good up till this point. Everything after this point invalidates every interaction you ever had with Peony in favor of relegated backstory that has zero semblance to the time you've spent with Peony. You get pulled into a plotline where Peony is actually a bioweapon and her casual flower selling parents.... were actually scientist concocting a super virus that stems from flowers, with the flower shop being their cover up. Its then hinted that Peony's sister was a valuable test subject for them so it's implied that they want to capture her... Only to then find out via a cutscene that Moss apparently killed her and burned Peony's house down...? Which then means Peony is the last living test subject that has the anti-flower virus so the cult wants to capture her...? And it only gets worst because apparently, there's also a conspiracy with trying to spread the flower plague and only let the few cultist who has the antivirus to live? Like, at this point, the game is losing sight on what it's trying to tell. And to make it worst, as you learn more about these expositions, you get the background for the MC. The background for the MC is as edgy as you can get where MC is a killer out for vengeance amidst a conspiracy where the government was actually involved in the killing of his sister and he's still on the hunt for the cause of his sister's death. But he's also a very loving brother as seen by the letter in the violin case Peony got. It's such a stark contrast that at the very end where we are finally witnessing the climax between Moss and Peony... He just defaults to an edgelord and monologues to Peony and leaves. He shows no compassion for her or her cause, doesn't face her outburst head-on, and tells her nothing but a one-sided apology where she doesn't even get a word in. It's the most self centered way or dealing with a problem that I'm just baffled how they could fumble this hard on the ending. This entire story revolved around both Moss and Peony, yet Peony receives zero resolutions and Moss... Moss gets the achievement of "Nirvana" by one-sidedly telling Peony to keep struggling, that he's sorry, and forcefully curing her as if what he just did was saintly. This resolution is just so disrespectful to the dialogue between Peony and Moss up till the final parts of the story. The ending truly left a sour taste in my mouth as the emotions I invested into these characters were thoroughly robbed, leaving zero desire of wanting to re-experience this game. [/spoiler]

Also, the fact that there is a feature in this game that is locked behind a "second playthrough" wall is utterly obnoxious. I spent around 30 minutes just wondering what other sources of information I can scavenge to figure out what to do in my first playthrough, only to realize that the hint isnt even in the game itself. The only way to unlock the feature in the game is to go through the "Load Chapter" or start a new game. Once you do so, that's when you can actually get extra dialogue options. Sadly, the game doesnt make that clear to you and I only accidentally stumbled upon it because at that point, after wasting 30 minutes just going circles around the entire level, checking something like the new game option was the only solution. Personal grievance that this was so convoluted and really unnecessary for what its worth.
Nonsensical story and too much backtracking to redo puzzles I've already done

I tried liking this game but the plot is incoherent at its very core. While this game tries to portray a valuable moral message, the absurdity of certain character actions are just incomprehensible.

I am very disappointed with this from Laplacian. Ono Wasabi is currently either my favorite or second favorite scenario writer which was why I was willing to give this a chance, but the English translation by AI with likely zero editing and some proofreading is unnatural. That isn't to say the translation itself is bad, it seems mostly technically correct, but it uses weird phrases and has odd word choices that a native English speaker, or likely even an ESL speaker would not choose / use.

Ultimately it's up to you if you want to support this or not, and considering the page stating it was only "assisted with English translation" I was willing to give it a chance, but it's highly likely that this was 100% AI translation with very little editing, if any, and only some light proofreading.

Laplacian, I am saddened by this as I have loved every visual novel you've put out so far.

Edited review:

It was worth the read, but ultimately it's a bit disappointing, likely due to the large feeling of the plot but it being so short.

I won't go into a lot of details because there's extremely little that can be talked about in this visual novel without spoilers. The two heroines are cute, but arguably in one of the routes (Eri's) the issues aren't really solved, leading to a feeling that Sari is the "true route". I have not read the novel that this is based off of, but I feel like that was the story in the novel and Eri's route was only added to appease VN fans. Past that the story spends a large amount of time on the setting and buildup of everything but very little time on resolving anything, and it doesn't feel like the plot is resolved in a satisfactory manner either.

Ultimately it feels like it's a story that to be told in a good manner it would have needed another 2 hours of runtime or so (it was 3:29 at my reading speed). However, it wasn't all bad, the music is atmospheric and adds to the experience, and the OP and ED are especially good (composed by Muu Dogg). The story itself is still solid, but is unremarkable due to the above issues. 

I still enjoyed it and found that it wasn't a waste of time, but at the same point I'm unsure if I'd really recommend this VN to anyone because it's so middle of the road in every manner.

2025/10/21 Update: It seems the game has been updated to replace AI translation with translation by human so kudos to Laplacian and the translators for that. 

--

The Chinese version was human translation, but the English version is AI-generated slop.
That tells you everything you need to know about this company and its practices.

Edit: I tried to give it another try, hoping the issue was with just one heroine. No, this issue is with every heroine. Every heroine has a randomly forced-in story that promotes arrogant and ignorant nationalism. In one story, saying "foreign food isn't worse than Chinese food" is seen as the villain of the story. The "villains" doesn't say foreign food are better, just not worse. To make it worse, it tries to insult the concept of "menuless" restaurants as some foreign concept ruining Chinese food. However, this concept is not a new foreign concept, it has been around for over a century in certain parts of China.

What is with Chinese games and their obsessive need to bash foreign cultures/countries? It's incredibly hard to find a Chinese game that doesn't repeatedly bash foreign cultures and promote their own as superior. They often cherry pick the worse examples from foreign cultures while cherry picking the good parts of theirs.

Office life is filled with this. It's not as common at first but increasingly becomes more common over-time. For example, the entire storyline of the Uncle is that he tries to immigrate but finds that western nations are filled with crime, unfair policies (to rich people), etc. This isn't a one time thing, many of the side stories are filled with similar trends and even the main story of some of the heroines are about one-upping foreign nations.

If they were at least subtle about it, it would be more tolerable. Every nation has some degree of nationalism but modern Chinese games are by far the most unapologetically blunt about it. At least the games from other nations tries to show both sides, Chinese games don't even bother.

我非常讨厌AI图像生成，而我买这款游戏之前并不知道这一点。

I did not know there were AI generated images before buying this game. Absolutely disappointed.
I heard a lot of bad news around this generated thing. Could this game uses AI images without stealing people's artwork? I doubt that. You hired artists and made them work for those BS images instead of creating your own art style for your game? What a smart decision to make, wow.

For the game play, I don't think this game worth replaying since there are too many repetitive parts here. For each game play you have to record the similar sound track over and over again, which is very boring. For the writing part, it is not satisfying as well. If this game did not use AI, I am still willing to support it without leaving this negative review, but well...

At the end of this review, I start to think how many video games with AI generated images or songs will I encounter in the future. Is it going to be fighting invisible enemies in the dark? Teammates truing into enemies? I still don't know.

If you find any grammar mistake in this review, don't worry. It must be me who made those mistakes instead of AI.

I did not expect to leave a negative review of this game, but here we are. If you do not want spoilers, I do not recommend reading my review. 

The biggest turn off for me is that this game uses AI art. This info isn't exactly hidden, but its not super obvious either. Instead of paying their already hired artists to make the icons and drama covers, the developers either got lazy or cheap and used AI art instead. For as much as I enjoyed this game, I wouldn't have bought it if I knew this.

Initially, I enjoyed playing and getting to know the characters. I decided to aim for Yuki's route first, which was a mistake. I should have read other reviews first. You can only go so far with him your first playthrough before his route gets locked. They bury this info in Yuki's info sheet where you have to search ahead in the route status bar to find his route gets locked after a certain drama gets recorded. If the developer didn't want you to aim for Yuki the first time, he should have been locked for the entire first playthrough. I understand this is a way for them to get you to the Solo Aki achievement, but to spend all that time working towards Yuki, only to have his route get locked so abruptly was upsetting. Then, you have to basically play a 2nd time the exact same way if you still want Yuki's ending, which seems tedious and it feels like the developers just want to waste player's time.

After a certain point, the game feels slow and repetitive, with very little happening to advance the story. You end up stuck in monotonous gameplay and interactions with other characters that are short, and repetitive in nature. The main plot also wasn't developed a lot, which is a shame because it had potential to be an interesting story. 

Overall, the game was enjoyable up until I got locked out of Yuki's route. Idk if I'd spend more time playing it over and over to unlock each character's routes though and the plot itself is pretty thin.

*Almost* spoiler free review!

The premise is simple: You meet four (main) characters who become voice acters at your studio, learn how to produce bl (and other) audio dramas, get to know and/or romance one of them and live happily ever after. 
The graphics are HQ, the voice acting is obviously the main focus. So what's wrong with Voice Love On Air?

Firstly, next to the very pretty anime artworks, the game also features AI art for the drama CD covers. Do with this info wahtever you want to. I do not support this and would like to know beforehand next time.

The gameplay loop is boring and repetitive and the main management part is a game based on luck rather than skill or good preperation. 
To produce dramas, you have to reach a certain amount of points in 3 different skills. These skills are leveled by taking courses for 5 days in-game time (the player is looking at the screen, hands off of the keyboard), after you passed the threshhold you appoint 1 or 2 VA's and then you have to execute the acting by picking cards to support the actors in the voice booths. Your stats have no influence on which cards you can pull and if you pull bad cards you can't meet certain requirements, deliver a worse product and get less cash as well as affection from your actors. But none of this really matters, because you will swim in money after Y1. Or rather I did, but I also only produced S rank dramas because of lucky card draws.

I normally do not gravitate to this kind of game, but I know there are a ton of good VN's out there. This one is just boring. The story is bland and has no depth beyond '[spoiler] my sister died [/spoiler]' and now the MC has a special emotion based power that's not getting explained in any detail at all. 
The love interests are uninspired BL tropes I just can't take serious anymore at this day and age. 
We have the ditzy cute one who's also abrasive at times when he feels inadequat but cries easily. 
The cool and mysterious smart guy wearing glasses and being rich. His MO? He likes [spoiler] building bricks[/spoiler]. (Thats kinda cute ngl, he's also my favorite)
The other cool but strong one who does not know how to show softness, because he has to be strong for his little sick sister. 
And your childhood best friend. Who is obviously down bad for you (Aki) since minute one.
Is there nothing more original than this? 

Before I end this review I'd like to open up another unpleaseant conversation. Please bare with me. I do not consider myself a fujoshi. I actually dislike this term a lot because of the community that surrounds a lot of BL works and makes me feel uncomfortable to sometimes even play or read anything like this openly, because of the blatant fetishization in the genre. I don't know anything about the studio behind this game, but I hope they will at least try better next time. 
80% of the explicit dramas we can produce in this game are r* fantasies, non-con or hurting one character in the process. Why. WHY? 
The very first audio drama is one. I actually thought about getting a refund, but I thought it might be random at first. It is not. The romances with the cast are 'normal' but at the same time, very same-y, like you can clearly feel that their focus is in a very specialized corner of BL. Now if you like this, please go on and pay 30€ (don't it's overprized, there are typos and bad translations everywhere), perfect game for you. I do not and that's why I won't recommend.

the game itself isnt that bad, the story is interesting but it can drag on and feel repetitive, but why the ai art...?!?!?! why hire great artists only to use their art in cgs and sprites only? im really disappointed cause i thought this game would be really cute,,, call me dramatic, sure, but i m not exactly thrilled that i paid almost 30 dollars for a game with ai art .

The translation in this game is terrible, I don't understand why nobody has mentioned it yet. I had to skip all of the audio dramas which is such a shame because the voice acting was superb, but the translation just ruined all the fun. Same with dates, I had a feeling that some parts were just translated by a machine because no human would make such basic mistakes.  I'm doubtful if there even was any beta testing before the game's release... 

There is so little story that we get considering the time we need to put into this game because of all the time managment stuff, I can't say it's really worth it. The first route took me about 15 hours because I had to replay some segments (missing requirements) and then I found out that Rousei's story happened again and again in every single route, the only difference being who saves Aki and a few other different conversations happening almost at the end of the game. 

Oh, and one more thing. On Wesley's route, you can't record his drama before some fixed points in the story play out, which happen at the end of the game. In my case, I wasted about 9-10 hours of playing just to find out on October of the 2nd year (2 months before the end) that I need to record Wesley's last drama having the max recognition stat (which never happened before) AND Aki's personal drama, where both need 2 full months to complete. So yes, if at this point you're lacking in any stat, that's a game over. 

I'm so done with this game, I can't fulfill those requirements even when I go back a few months now and there's no way I'm replaying the whole thing. I'm so dissapointed, the game could have been so much fun and it's all ruined for me now because of the terrible translation and all the frustration from not being able to finish what I started.

The English translation is in desperate need of proofreading as there are a ton of mistranslated sentences and words, making a lot of the scenes awkward to read.  The main plot is also pretty much nonexistent, which is a shame since there are a few interesting parts to it.  (The MC's past, for example)  As for the stat raising aspect to the gameplay, I ended up finding that a bit of chore after a while.  This was mostly, because of the time constraints, which I think could've been balanced better.  On a more positive note, the art is pretty and the characters are interesting.  Although, I would've liked to know a bit more about them with more events, etc, through the story.  So, if your looking for an engaging story with lots of romance, I can't really recommend this.  Maybe I've spoiled myself with Nitro Chiral BL games, because this just feels lacking in comparison. Such a shame, since it does have some good points to it.

I am bummed to leave a not-recommend review for this, cause a lot of work was put into this game and it was clear, but I spent hours grinding to get Yuki's route, only to find out that you can't get it the first time (which I didn't know until I saw a review here). I got my bar all the way up to max and then was blocked out. It was disheartening to say the least. Why not tell the player sooner that they can't get that route to start? N+C and other BL games block routes but it's not time you could've spent developing other routes, they just put you in routes not for that character. After I was blocked out, I didn't even want to finish playing cause it was that frustrating to get to where I'd been. If they change the routes I'd consider playing again, but for now, I'm pretty upset that I spent so much time for nothing.

The requirements of recording are too high. For the last drama it requires 800... when it told me to record, I only got 700 on average, and it is impossible for me to improve 100 in a month... I would not recommend this game if the developers want to make it this hard.

Concept is fun and the story is decently interesting but the mechanics of a dating sim are just not fun, needing to go into a new game just to be able to go after one of the characters route is annoying.

Spent 13 hours only to be locked out of an ending because the game didn't trigger a time specific event. All motivation lost, since I'd have to start all over again.

Might try again in the future.

Brilliant concept ruined by a horrible translation job, and a stat builder with gameplay that relies on RNG.

I cannot recommend this game in the state that it is in. The interesting part of the game - the part where you direct voice actors and hear the final result - is repetitive and RNG dependant. It totally removes the most satisfying parts of a stat builder, while crushing any sense creative freedom you thought you might have when directing audio dramas. Audio dramas that you will hardly feel anything from, because the translation appears to have washed all feeling, nuance and subtext from every single line of dialogue in the game. I do not know Japanese by a long shot, but I'm familiar enough to know that there is a LOT being said in the voice over with subtext and tone that is totally removed from the English translation. I feel like I'm reading a 6 word summary for lines of dialogue at times.

I couldn't play more of this game because it feels translated in the most bare bones way possible. There is no subtext to what is being said, and the grammar is questionable in so many ways, to the point that I ran into these issues within 10 minutes of opening the game. The documents that you get with the jobs are hard to read because they are written so horribly, which ruins the immersion. Aki, the protagonist, possesses the ability to recognise when someone is lying, which feels very cheap when the translation removes practically all subtext for the viewer to take in for themselves.

I went back and forth on if I should refund or not, and decided that while I could absolutely see myself sitting through the rest, as the CGs are very pretty, I can't imagine the game making me feel anything at all for the characters.

Some extra bits I want to mention 
- Unnecessary use of AI images for Icons and Backgrounds when they could have had the artists draw stylistically consistent simple icons as used in the rest of the UI. It's a pointless stain on the game as they look like crap. The school background contains AI words off to the right, the shop items are covered in AI art writing, the drama covers look like shit.
- The tutorial writing is just as bad as the story writing, its a good thing the UI is intuitive.
- A plain soundtrack that feels very out of place.

So I've just finished my first playthrough which took about 15 hours or so of actual play and I'm pretty disappointed.

Pros:
+ looks good
+ the voice acting, of course (the real one, not ingame one)

Cons
- the "romances" / ending; so I've pursued a guy (Yuki), pretty sure I've done all his recordings because I've used him in every single one; filled his bar to 930 pretty fast; at some point I get a locked X on the last heart, thought it would unlock eventually; it didn't; apparently I missed something somewhere, god knows where. You spend 15 hours and then you don't even get a kiss with your guy because of some hidden crap? This is completely asinine design. Any game that requires you to either use a guide or stumble by sheer luck through a hidden scene is bad; not only that, but those scenes completely decide if you get ANY type of ending with the dude you chose - let me repeat that, you don't get ANYTHING, it's like it never happened. 15 hours for literally nothing.
When you design a game, you have to ask yourself - if the player doesn't find all our hidden encounters, does it have a satisfying ending? If the answer is NO, you go back to the drawing board, because it was not satisfying, it was "wait, what? really? I wasted all that time clicking for this one line ending that doesn't even mention anything about any character, let alone my romance option?"
- in that regard, what is the point of the affection with the other non romance characters since there doesn't seem to be an epilogue? I guess there's a bit of mundane dialogue, but not really worth spending a day on them.
- the gameplay is affected by randomness; what's the point? At the end you have many options so every recording works fine in one go, but at the start it's completely frustrating; like you're all good and then some char says something and gets -2 and you can't fix it next turn because you've got cards that don't match at all; I completed all the recordings with S but I loaded the first one about 10 times (out of which 2 were because I didn't get the system);
- the weird name translation and honorific adaptation; you can hear "Miki" and in writing they use some name that doesn't even come close; you can hear them say JunJun and they adapted it to JoJo; Mone as well, no clue what they're even saying but they're not saying Mone; the idol wannabe guy calls you "Aki" and they use "recording guy" (probably because it uses no honorifics? but it's so jarring for him to still address you "recording guy" after helping him so no clue what they were going for); and the list is actually pretty long here
- the idol wannabe guy was pretty annoying and they start the story with him; even after doing his whole arc, I find his reactions unjustified and offputting;
- there's an inventory that's almost useless; that wouldn't be a problem in itself but there's so much clicking, my fingers hurt from pointless clicking; for example, to use an item from the shop to raise a stat, you click the shop, scroll to 2nd page, click on the item, can't recall if there's a confirmation here, do the same once more because you want a second item and can't buy in batch, then get out of the shop app, go to the studio app, click the inventory, click the item, click use, click confirmation that you really want to use it, click to make the message with what stat was raised disappear and do these last 3 actions again for the 2nd item. Why? Could've raised the stat directly when you bought it, not like you can gift the stat raising item or do anything else with it.
- I don't know if I missed anything, but nothing happens on Christmas, New Year or other events; the most you're gonna get a text and once you get to gift someone something on White Day, but that's it. Which I don't get because no one gifted the MC anything on Valentines, so...?
*Edit: I managed to get an event on Christmas by scheduling a date with a guy in the weekend before. It was sheer accident I stumbled upon it
- started a sort of new game plus but have no clue what it reuses because it doesn't seem to reuse any; said "Yuki story updated" and not much else; they don't keep your stats, don't keep your money so what's the point? *Edit: I had all the cards and the actors had the unlocked proficiency - you probably unlock stats and other stuff if you replay the game several times...
*Edit 2: Nope, no stats and money. 3rd playthrough and doesn't seem to carry anything else over compared to the the 2nd.

That being said, I'll replay it once more. I'll update the review if I manage to get one romance done, otherwise I'll let this stay not recommended because nobody wants to waste 15 hours or more and get nothing. And the whole stat raising game is not that entertaining that I'd spend 15 hours just for that.

+++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++I've played the game now so far except the Ryousei route. The updates have clearly made the game easier and more playable, but unfortunately it doesn't change the fact that the story remains completely the same. You get 2 new CG's per route and that's it. Unfortunately is just not enough story for this price. Because games like Slow Damage or Hashimie of the old book town offer you more for less money.
+++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++


!!!DON'T PLAY YUKI FIRST !!!


It really hurts to give a bad review here, but the system of this game is terrible.

I bought the game as soon as it was released. Big mistake because no one knew that Yuki's route was blocked and could only be played in the second round. Nice, 10 hours for nothing .-.  Afterwards I played Junichi and I have to say that for the effort it takes to play the game it really isn't worth. I like playing VN with stats systems like Jack Jeanne, but this system is absolutely unreasonable in terms of regulations. I just failed again on a route because I had to finish Wesley's albums (his third) and the main album in 2 months. Unfortunately, the stats are sometimes so bad that you have almost no chance of getting it done (have you even played the game yourself??).

As it is now I'm going to drop it because I really don't want to be disappointed again. I understand that the creators wanted to do something special here, but the stats system is too hard and replaying for hours every time ..no.. A pity. I liked the characters and art...

我非常讨厌AI图像生成，而我买这款游戏之前并不知道这一点。

I did not know there were AI generated images before buying this game. Absolutely disappointed.
I heard a lot of bad news around this generated thing. Could this game uses AI images without stealing people's artwork? I doubt that. You hired artists and made them work for those BS images instead of creating your own art style for your game? What a smart decision to make, wow.

For the game play, I don't think this game worth replaying since there are too many repetitive parts here. For each game play you have to record the similar sound track over and over again, which is very boring. For the writing part, it is not satisfying as well. If this game did not use AI, I am still willing to support it without leaving this negative review, but well...

At the end of this review, I start to think how many video games with AI generated images or songs will I encounter in the future. Is it going to be fighting invisible enemies in the dark? Teammates truing into enemies? I still don't know.

If you find any grammar mistake in this review, don't worry. It must be me who made those mistakes instead of AI.

I did not expect to leave a negative review of this game, but here we are. If you do not want spoilers, I do not recommend reading my review. 

The biggest turn off for me is that this game uses AI art. This info isn't exactly hidden, but its not super obvious either. Instead of paying their already hired artists to make the icons and drama covers, the developers either got lazy or cheap and used AI art instead. For as much as I enjoyed this game, I wouldn't have bought it if I knew this.

Initially, I enjoyed playing and getting to know the characters. I decided to aim for Yuki's route first, which was a mistake. I should have read other reviews first. You can only go so far with him your first playthrough before his route gets locked. They bury this info in Yuki's info sheet where you have to search ahead in the route status bar to find his route gets locked after a certain drama gets recorded. If the developer didn't want you to aim for Yuki the first time, he should have been locked for the entire first playthrough. I understand this is a way for them to get you to the Solo Aki achievement, but to spend all that time working towards Yuki, only to have his route get locked so abruptly was upsetting. Then, you have to basically play a 2nd time the exact same way if you still want Yuki's ending, which seems tedious and it feels like the developers just want to waste player's time.

After a certain point, the game feels slow and repetitive, with very little happening to advance the story. You end up stuck in monotonous gameplay and interactions with other characters that are short, and repetitive in nature. The main plot also wasn't developed a lot, which is a shame because it had potential to be an interesting story. 

Overall, the game was enjoyable up until I got locked out of Yuki's route. Idk if I'd spend more time playing it over and over to unlock each character's routes though and the plot itself is pretty thin.

*Almost* spoiler free review!

The premise is simple: You meet four (main) characters who become voice acters at your studio, learn how to produce bl (and other) audio dramas, get to know and/or romance one of them and live happily ever after. 
The graphics are HQ, the voice acting is obviously the main focus. So what's wrong with Voice Love On Air?

Firstly, next to the very pretty anime artworks, the game also features AI art for the drama CD covers. Do with this info wahtever you want to. I do not support this and would like to know beforehand next time.

The gameplay loop is boring and repetitive and the main management part is a game based on luck rather than skill or good preperation. 
To produce dramas, you have to reach a certain amount of points in 3 different skills. These skills are leveled by taking courses for 5 days in-game time (the player is looking at the screen, hands off of the keyboard), after you passed the threshhold you appoint 1 or 2 VA's and then you have to execute the acting by picking cards to support the actors in the voice booths. Your stats have no influence on which cards you can pull and if you pull bad cards you can't meet certain requirements, deliver a worse product and get less cash as well as affection from your actors. But none of this really matters, because you will swim in money after Y1. Or rather I did, but I also only produced S rank dramas because of lucky card draws.

I normally do not gravitate to this kind of game, but I know there are a ton of good VN's out there. This one is just boring. The story is bland and has no depth beyond '[spoiler] my sister died [/spoiler]' and now the MC has a special emotion based power that's not getting explained in any detail at all. 
The love interests are uninspired BL tropes I just can't take serious anymore at this day and age. 
We have the ditzy cute one who's also abrasive at times when he feels inadequat but cries easily. 
The cool and mysterious smart guy wearing glasses and being rich. His MO? He likes [spoiler] building bricks[/spoiler]. (Thats kinda cute ngl, he's also my favorite)
The other cool but strong one who does not know how to show softness, because he has to be strong for his little sick sister. 
And your childhood best friend. Who is obviously down bad for you (Aki) since minute one.
Is there nothing more original than this? 

Before I end this review I'd like to open up another unpleaseant conversation. Please bare with me. I do not consider myself a fujoshi. I actually dislike this term a lot because of the community that surrounds a lot of BL works and makes me feel uncomfortable to sometimes even play or read anything like this openly, because of the blatant fetishization in the genre. I don't know anything about the studio behind this game, but I hope they will at least try better next time. 
80% of the explicit dramas we can produce in this game are r* fantasies, non-con or hurting one character in the process. Why. WHY? 
The very first audio drama is one. I actually thought about getting a refund, but I thought it might be random at first. It is not. The romances with the cast are 'normal' but at the same time, very same-y, like you can clearly feel that their focus is in a very specialized corner of BL. Now if you like this, please go on and pay 30€ (don't it's overprized, there are typos and bad translations everywhere), perfect game for you. I do not and that's why I won't recommend.

the game itself isnt that bad, the story is interesting but it can drag on and feel repetitive, but why the ai art...?!?!?! why hire great artists only to use their art in cgs and sprites only? im really disappointed cause i thought this game would be really cute,,, call me dramatic, sure, but i m not exactly thrilled that i paid almost 30 dollars for a game with ai art .

The translation in this game is terrible, I don't understand why nobody has mentioned it yet. I had to skip all of the audio dramas which is such a shame because the voice acting was superb, but the translation just ruined all the fun. Same with dates, I had a feeling that some parts were just translated by a machine because no human would make such basic mistakes.  I'm doubtful if there even was any beta testing before the game's release... 

There is so little story that we get considering the time we need to put into this game because of all the time managment stuff, I can't say it's really worth it. The first route took me about 15 hours because I had to replay some segments (missing requirements) and then I found out that Rousei's story happened again and again in every single route, the only difference being who saves Aki and a few other different conversations happening almost at the end of the game. 

Oh, and one more thing. On Wesley's route, you can't record his drama before some fixed points in the story play out, which happen at the end of the game. In my case, I wasted about 9-10 hours of playing just to find out on October of the 2nd year (2 months before the end) that I need to record Wesley's last drama having the max recognition stat (which never happened before) AND Aki's personal drama, where both need 2 full months to complete. So yes, if at this point you're lacking in any stat, that's a game over. 

I'm so done with this game, I can't fulfill those requirements even when I go back a few months now and there's no way I'm replaying the whole thing. I'm so dissapointed, the game could have been so much fun and it's all ruined for me now because of the terrible translation and all the frustration from not being able to finish what I started.

The English translation is in desperate need of proofreading as there are a ton of mistranslated sentences and words, making a lot of the scenes awkward to read.  The main plot is also pretty much nonexistent, which is a shame since there are a few interesting parts to it.  (The MC's past, for example)  As for the stat raising aspect to the gameplay, I ended up finding that a bit of chore after a while.  This was mostly, because of the time constraints, which I think could've been balanced better.  On a more positive note, the art is pretty and the characters are interesting.  Although, I would've liked to know a bit more about them with more events, etc, through the story.  So, if your looking for an engaging story with lots of romance, I can't really recommend this.  Maybe I've spoiled myself with Nitro Chiral BL games, because this just feels lacking in comparison. Such a shame, since it does have some good points to it.

I am bummed to leave a not-recommend review for this, cause a lot of work was put into this game and it was clear, but I spent hours grinding to get Yuki's route, only to find out that you can't get it the first time (which I didn't know until I saw a review here). I got my bar all the way up to max and then was blocked out. It was disheartening to say the least. Why not tell the player sooner that they can't get that route to start? N+C and other BL games block routes but it's not time you could've spent developing other routes, they just put you in routes not for that character. After I was blocked out, I didn't even want to finish playing cause it was that frustrating to get to where I'd been. If they change the routes I'd consider playing again, but for now, I'm pretty upset that I spent so much time for nothing.

The requirements of recording are too high. For the last drama it requires 800... when it told me to record, I only got 700 on average, and it is impossible for me to improve 100 in a month... I would not recommend this game if the developers want to make it this hard.

Concept is fun and the story is decently interesting but the mechanics of a dating sim are just not fun, needing to go into a new game just to be able to go after one of the characters route is annoying.

Spent 13 hours only to be locked out of an ending because the game didn't trigger a time specific event. All motivation lost, since I'd have to start all over again.

Might try again in the future.

Brilliant concept ruined by a horrible translation job, and a stat builder with gameplay that relies on RNG.

I cannot recommend this game in the state that it is in. The interesting part of the game - the part where you direct voice actors and hear the final result - is repetitive and RNG dependant. It totally removes the most satisfying parts of a stat builder, while crushing any sense creative freedom you thought you might have when directing audio dramas. Audio dramas that you will hardly feel anything from, because the translation appears to have washed all feeling, nuance and subtext from every single line of dialogue in the game. I do not know Japanese by a long shot, but I'm familiar enough to know that there is a LOT being said in the voice over with subtext and tone that is totally removed from the English translation. I feel like I'm reading a 6 word summary for lines of dialogue at times.

I couldn't play more of this game because it feels translated in the most bare bones way possible. There is no subtext to what is being said, and the grammar is questionable in so many ways, to the point that I ran into these issues within 10 minutes of opening the game. The documents that you get with the jobs are hard to read because they are written so horribly, which ruins the immersion. Aki, the protagonist, possesses the ability to recognise when someone is lying, which feels very cheap when the translation removes practically all subtext for the viewer to take in for themselves.

I went back and forth on if I should refund or not, and decided that while I could absolutely see myself sitting through the rest, as the CGs are very pretty, I can't imagine the game making me feel anything at all for the characters.

Some extra bits I want to mention 
- Unnecessary use of AI images for Icons and Backgrounds when they could have had the artists draw stylistically consistent simple icons as used in the rest of the UI. It's a pointless stain on the game as they look like crap. The school background contains AI words off to the right, the shop items are covered in AI art writing, the drama covers look like shit.
- The tutorial writing is just as bad as the story writing, its a good thing the UI is intuitive.
- A plain soundtrack that feels very out of place.

So I've just finished my first playthrough which took about 15 hours or so of actual play and I'm pretty disappointed.

Terrible acting, the audio is nearly broken, constant mistakes in their lines, hearing the production cast in the background, lines cutting out, probably the dumbest plot I have ever seen in any game, and definitely the worst attempt at an FMV experience. This game might have been worth a couple bucks at most but over $10 for something the developers clearly didn't care about is absurd.

This game is not so bad it is good. It is just bad. It is interesting that so many people could attach their names to a project with such obvious flaws, but the only enjoyment to be had with this is wondering how it got made.
Insane how lazy the creators of this game are. Bugs everywhere, the scrips is random af, acting is trash, and the minigames not only are reapted but are also easy. Insane.

Holy crap this game is terrible. The actors cannot act except for two of the female characters and one of them dies within the first few scenes. The written text and the spoken dialogue do not match at all. The mini games are simplistic flash games from 20 years ago. The main character talks tough, but gets knocked out in one hit almost every time. Don't worry, the ladies love him anyway! The audio mixing is trash and some scenes you can't even hear them speaking, but even if you could some of them talk so fast you can't understand a word they say and the text doesn't pick up on it either.

Where to begin. No offense to anyone involved in this production. It seemed like a classroom project for beginner actors 101 where then someone got their kid brothers AV club to do the editing. Not the greatest.

However, I was still willing to see how the game unfolded. The trailer has a lot of things that piqued my interest. 

Unfortunately, due to the fact that every scene seemed to start out of focus before it was in focus was a deal breaker. My eyes just couldnt take the constant feeling  of wearing glasses with the wrong prescription.

Good luck to anyone who still decides to venture on.

Anna getting mad at me for being in love but the only reason she's there is for her husband???

Genuinely one worst made FMV I've ever played.
Actors messing up lines is kept in, you can hear the director yell "cut", scenes cut off the actor before they finish their line, the main game of chapter 3 doesn't even work properly (I bring it up in the game's discussion page), and the rules of the game are inconsistant (no violence allowed, but later characters fight without getting punished). The acting is terrible, but that's to be expected and is part of the fun in these things, but some characters English is so bad you sometimes cannot understand them. Funnily enough, for a game that tries to push the female characters onto the male lead, you'd think they'd be the best actors in the group, but nope. Excluding the ones who die early, the male cast members are the best actors lol.

It was somewhat fun though, I got it on sale so I don't feel like I waisted my money too bad lol.

Edit:
One actor even messes up his line, stops to say "I'm going to take the line from the top." and then starts the scene over. All of that should have been edited out, LOL.

This was in the "so bad you can't help but laugh'. We seriously wondered if the videos were AI generated. The tonal shifts between characters and the horrible script made the plot almost impossible to follow. Scene to Scene there were tons of continuity errors. You can even find one scene where the actor literally says "I'm going to take that line again from the top". Get it if you want a great time laughing at the campy acting by some of the individuals (including the main lead), I get what they were trying to go for here but I've slogged through this game once and have no desire to explore the other paths in the game. It sucks too because I'm a big fan of D-game anime and series like Alice in Borderland, Btoom, and others. Given a bit more spit and polish this game could have been great. I'm looking forward to the "Directors Cut" of this game if it ever comes out. Just be aware there is also obviously some AI generated voices in this game.

Terrible acting, the audio is nearly broken, constant mistakes in their lines, hearing the production cast in the background, lines cutting out, probably the dumbest plot I have ever seen in any game, and definitely the worst attempt at an FMV experience. This game might have been worth a couple bucks at most but over $10 for something the developers clearly didn't care about is absurd.

This is legitimately one of the most poorly written and least well designed FMV's that I've ever played. if you love a trainwreck this can be worth it, but don't expect a well put together gaming or narrative experience.

This game is not so bad it is good. It is just bad. It is interesting that so many people could attach their names to a project with such obvious flaws, but the only enjoyment to be had with this is wondering how it got made.
Insane how lazy the creators of this game are. Bugs everywhere, the scrips is random af, acting is trash, and the minigames not only are reapted but are also easy. Insane.

So I'm going to try and be honest be with everyone. Is the game bad? Oh ya you can say that. But was it a good first try at this sort of concept? Debatable but I will say yes. The game will leave you frustrated, confused, and wondering if you got your money's worth. With that I'll try to answer as detailed as I can.

The story. So you start off with your main character whom is a game developer that owns a failing company. You get dragged into the Joker games because you owe a lot of money and was invited somehow via a phone app. From there you meet your cast of colorful characters whom have their motivation to win the game which offers a lot of money. But there is more to this game of death than it seems but it's up to you how you want to play things out. The story path branches in a manner of whether you want to be righteous or selfish which can also change out the type of people whom you end up teaming or competing with at the end of the game. Very cool concept to take a game tournament style of a story that can be shaped based on whom you interact positively with and choose to support. This however is where the game begins to fall into a linear path because depending on who you save then you get one of three destined endings, with some variations in character changes. If they were to continue this concept I hope they can branch this out more with character interactions with each other, more in depth character stories with whom we choose to be teams with, and heck more romance options as well since they offer one in this game. The endings for the game was very lackluster and can explain a lot of the disappointments that player have when they reach their own conclusions. For future games I would hope that they would think more about how they end these stories as books are written with an ending an mind first, but that's just me. Choices don't seem to also really matter often as the game feels really linear story progression wise. This could be addressed easily with more story paths and branches. Lastly, holy missing context. I was left confused often cuz the story would refer to certain events happening or characters backstory and I'm just like where the heck did all this come from. Cuz the game doesn't do any job to provide you context you may feel unsure of how to choose at times. So tldr, future installments need more branching paths, more interactions between each character to make it feel like like, more options if they are presenting anything at all, and please add some form of context so that players can understand what the heck is going on so that the story makes more sense when jumping into a game.

The characters. So having a diverse crew of characters to work with is all fine and dandy. Heck they even interact with each other during certain games which makes em feel somewhat alive. My issue lies with the actors and actresses themselves. Don't get me wrong I'm sure they're great but my Lord does the acting get kinda awkward. Some times they would slip up on their words but the game kept these mistakes for some reason instead of having a perfect version recorded. Wouldn't hurt to record again as much as you need to make the game feel flawless and not break immersion. Continuity of the characters don't make sense, at one moment I made some decision that would anger a character. The next either they or the main character would switch up their tone and suddenly become all buddy Buddy with each other. Like I said before with the branching paths and interactions. Record different versions if you have to and show the characters grateful or hated to us for our choices and not just some weird hey how you doing crap. Also, you could just tell when some of the actors forget their lines and have this awkward pause or stutter. Again they couldn't just recorded again too have a perfect shot which I'm not sure what they didn't. Just something for the devs to keep in mind next time. Also, please consider adding like filler story or something at the start of the story cuz when home boy meets our Asian lady friend Lin, he busted out the "ni hao" out of nowhere and just got me laughing my ass off. Like where did our character assume she was Chinese and why was that the first thing that comes out of his mouth meeting her. Wouldn't been good to know that he was raised by a Chinese dad or something you know. My favorite actors were the guy playing Rick and Leon, they felt the most natural and didn't feel too forced compared to most of the cast. I'm sure from this experience the whole crew will learn and can only grow so can't wait to see how everyone improved on their next projects.

Game mechanics. Alright this is where it gets really funky. Most of the mini games are pretty much phone ad games that you could probably find ads for on tiktok or YouTube. Not all of them are too difficult but lordy you have to play some of these on a PC cuz otherwise you'll have a bad time on the steam deck. The audio for the game also acts weird at times, cutting out when some people are talking and then you hear the mic static in the background. Not sure what they were using to record but please have the audio fix for next time or at least prerecord something. Then the game has these weird transitions where they fade to black but the actors are still saying stuff like it's all sentimental. Yes that's just more awkward if anything, just finish recording the characters saying what they need to say. The game also crashes on load screens for me too but I managed to fix these by reloading the scene and fast forward to where I was. Minor inconvenience that the devs could fix really easy but probably should have tested that before releasing. I'm the beginning of the game dialogue are displayed on the screen which supposed to symbolize the main characters thoughts. For a fmv game this is a bad move, if the character has thoughts just have them speak it out loud with a filter to let us know they're thinking this in their head. I did not like having to randomly read some text and just assume it is the main character. Did they not have time to record these lines or no budget to pay em?

Big warning, the game DOES use AI art in this game. The use of AI in this game involves a scene trying to explain some backstory for the main character. It's about as ugly and horrendous as you expect. The devs should have instead just have actors act the scene out and used CGI, so this really puts a big negative for me.

In summary. I give this game a good ol 4/10. I see the potential but I can not give the greenlight for this game and expect other people to enjoy a story about a game of death. I can hope that from this game that future releases will be higher quality, have more thought out mechanics, and provide better moments for the actors and actresses to shine their talent.

Holy crap this game is terrible. The actors cannot act except for two of the female characters and one of them dies within the first few scenes. The written text and the spoken dialogue do not match at all. The mini games are simplistic flash games from 20 years ago. The main character talks tough, but gets knocked out in one hit almost every time. Don't worry, the ladies love him anyway! The audio mixing is trash and some scenes you can't even hear them speaking, but even if you could some of them talk so fast you can't understand a word they say and the text doesn't pick up on it either.

Where to begin. No offense to anyone involved in this production. It seemed like a classroom project for beginner actors 101 where then someone got their kid brothers AV club to do the editing. Not the greatest.

However, I was still willing to see how the game unfolded. The trailer has a lot of things that piqued my interest. 

Unfortunately, due to the fact that every scene seemed to start out of focus before it was in focus was a deal breaker. My eyes just couldnt take the constant feeling  of wearing glasses with the wrong prescription.

Good luck to anyone who still decides to venture on.

Genuinely one worst made FMV I've ever played.
Actors messing up lines is kept in, you can hear the director yell "cut", scenes cut off the actor before they finish their line, the main game of chapter 3 doesn't even work properly (I bring it up in the game's discussion page), and the rules of the game are inconsistant (no violence allowed, but later characters fight without getting punished). The acting is terrible, but that's to be expected and is part of the fun in these things, but some characters English is so bad you sometimes cannot understand them. Funnily enough, for a game that tries to push the female characters onto the male lead, you'd think they'd be the best actors in the group, but nope. Excluding the ones who die early, the male cast members are the best actors lol.

It was somewhat fun though, I got it on sale so I don't feel like I waisted my money too bad lol.

Edit:
One actor even messes up his line, stops to say "I'm going to take the line from the top." and then starts the scene over. All of that should have been edited out, LOL.

I love FMV games and so I'm very forgiving if the acting isn't great or it's a bit campy or the game is janky. But this is on another level, way down. I hate to slate it because there are so few FMV games and I love to see new ones, but it is bad. After playing for a while I genuinely thought all the FMVs were fully AI generated. There are huge issues with the acting and the dialogue, there are sentences that don't make any sense, sentences with words missing and actors making mistakes that aren't edited out. There are serious issues with the story and the writing, as others have said, continuity issues and areas where what is happening in the moment, or even the overall story, don't make any sense.

If the game had gotten a lot more polish it probably would be a passable FMV entry, but as it stands it's a mess.

Don't be like me. Don't waste your money.
