I've been holding off on giving this game a review because I really wanted to give it a fair chance and put it through its paces.
But unfortunately after ~33 hours (at time of review) I've concluded that the game is simply too broken to be able to recommend in any good faith. In spite of whatever positives it may have, or what few there are anyway.

The elevator pitch for this game is something like 'STALKER but Fallout 76'
But they also decided to keep all the horrible game breaking bugs of Fallout 76 too.

Without going on and on about every single bug I encountered, it really boils down to the game and server simply refusing to function as expected or even within reason. This coupled with an already clunky game itself aided by similarly clunky design all culminates in what is unfortunately an incredibly underwhelming and disappointing experience.

Though some credit where credit is due, the game does have most if not all dialogue fully voiced, which is great.
But if you don't really care about the story or the dialogue, that doesn't leave much else.

The gunplay is barely functional, the weapon customization is fairly inconsequential, and the weapon variety is all but absent. While there are a solid handful available, there really is precious little to distinguish any weapon from another of the same class, or even iterations of the same weapon. While higher tier weapons do get very minor perks, most do precious little to really affect gameplay in any meaningful or noticeable manner.
On top of this is how rare most weapons actually end up being. Though there are crates around the world that always spawn and typically contain a random weapon, they frequently contain a similar and small variety of weapons in my experience. Meaning you can loot the same chest maybe a dozen times and get the same two or three weapons. And while that may just be bad RNG, it certainly begs the question of how many weapons even [i]are[/i] really in the game to be able to keep getting the same ones by dumb luck alone.
And as mentioned before, any two iterations of the same weapon are going to be statistically and functionally identical, as in, no randomized stats. Which for some might be a good thing because at least when you get a gun, you know what you're getting in terms of stats/functionality. But it does make looting the same items over and over excruciatingly repetitive and boring. A little randomization would definitely make acquiring multiples of the same weapon a little more interesting because the varying stats could affect how they're used. But that's just my personal taste more than anything.

The quests can be pretty hit or miss. Again, credit where credit is due, it does seem a fair amount of thought and effort [i]has[/i] gone in to designing storylines. And even though I admittedly haven't paid much attention to most of them, the little bits I've gleamed [i]do[/i] seem reasonably well thought out. I just personally don't care for any of that. But the real let down of quests is that most yield pretty useless or meaningless rewards.
A majority of the time all you get is a fistful of currency, a few consumable items, and usually a card item for the games in-game card game battler. Which if you never interact with that minigame (which there isn't much point to outside of boredom), these cards will be completely useless to you as a player.

And just to round out the review because it's already getting pretty long in the tooth, I just want to quickly mention that the anomalies in the game are annoying more than anything. Though not horrifically frequent, they are often littered all over the roads between POI's and in later area's of the game make certain sections of the map nearly impassable and for no real reason other than to justify their existence.
Also the item durability mechanic is just plain irritating. While not as bad as some games, personally I'm just sick of item durability in games, especially online games. Especially considering each death degrades all equipped items ~10% or so, which the game never even explains, you just suddenly notice when all your equipment is about to break. Of which this durability requires resources to repair (higher tier resources for higher tier items).
Even if the argument is to try to encourage players to use lower tier gear normally and only take out higher tier gear when strictly necessary, the game still does a very poor job of this. At best, it's poorly executed, at worst, it's poorly designed. In either case it makes for a very unenjoyable experience. And while gathering resources to repair isn't enormously difficult, it's just an extra layer of tedium that simply doesn't need to be there.

Were this a free-to-play game, it might at least be worth a try.
But for $30, absolutely stay as far away as you can.
I do hope the game improves over time, but it is not worth playing in its current state.

As much as I am thoroughly enjoying this game its next to impossible to recommend it in its current state. I have an AMD Ryzen 7 7800X3D, Gigabyte GeForce RTX 4070 Ti Super, Corsair Vengeance 32gb 5200 MHz DDR5, ASUS TUF Gaming B650 - Plus AM5 and it is installed on an M.2. The bugs and issues make it so difficult to play. Ive tried too play this on max, medium and low graphics reaching 600fps then also setting a cap at 180 incase the game couldnt handle the frames with no change.

I have multiple bugs where my gun breaks mid fight but stays out and stops me from being able to switch weapon or remove it in my inventory until I die, I have died because on occasions enemies shoot through multiple walls with 0 line of sight, I have died to invisible enemies that show up in the damage log, I have randomly died in safe zones with the damage log saying i have dealt damage to myself.

The looting system is extremely buggy where instead of being able to open containers they just glow a faint yellow and does not let you interact until you reboot the game and even leaving to the main menu and rejoining does not work. I have opened multiple trial chests where it randomly either drops the loot for you, the loot goes into your inventory or does not drop any loot at all. 

The fast travel system via the guide also has multiple issues where it seems like the servers cannot load what is happening so instead it boots you back to the main menu over and over until the game is rebooted completely but then using the fast travel spots on the edges of the maps do not bug so its either completely rebooting the game or needing to run the entire length of the area to fast travel.

The actual areas also have multiple bug where the visual of the objects such as stairs do not align with the object until you reboot the game so in order to walk up stairs it makes you walk through a building as it is rendered in the incorrect place.

Their are multiple quests (People with arrow pointing down) where no matter how many times you speak to them they do not give you the quest but just repeat the same thing over and over. There are multiple bugs in quests stopping you from progressing until (You guessed it) you reboot the game and the quest works perfectly fine, notably the Old Friends Lair quest which I am still unable to complete as it will not let the message be listened too no matter how long I wait for it to finish, there is also multiple quests that do not update no matter if you completed the objective or not.

Finally the enemies are so f****ng random, I have died in one shot to an even with a max health buff where on another occasion im taking 5-10 damage per shot. The spawns of the enemies are also extremely random. I have cleared areas and gone back 20 minutes later and the enemies have not respawned and on other occasions ive cleared an area, started looting and have been in the area 5 minutes maximum and they have spawned back in all around me. I have been mid mission and had a random shotgunner spawn behind me and one shot me instantly.  A few times where no matter how much i shoot an enemy they do not take damage and there is no hit marker showing and also enemies who will just randomly fully heal during a fight out of no where but mainly mini bosses such as the swamp thing.

Also random plants just spawn and despawn infront of you randomly while needing to loot them which has also stopped me from completing bounties. I have also not been able to return in bounties at other times so I was just stuck with the trophies.

Finally the design of the quests can be infuriating, upon entering a location no matter how deep in the mission you are if you die it throws you back to the complete start of the mission so if you die on the final boss you have to do the entire place again. The design of the public events are even worse as theres not even an area checkpoint you spawn too, if you die at the very end of the public event you will need to respawn at a near settlement meaning you dont complete the event and miss out on the rewards.

The game is not well optimised at all and a few of the design choices are honestly the worst ive ever seen. However this game has extreme potential, scratches that Stalker itch and is overall an enjoyable and brilliant game. With some bug fixes, some updates and some small changes this game can easily be a top game with endless fun and fantastic content. Unfortunately though I cannot recommend this in its current state.

The game has potential, but if you're a solo player I'd wait for it to be 50% or more off or much further along in development.  Spamming reload 100 times in the middle of a heavy fight to get your weapon to reload is a constant issue that can quickly result in death if you're fighting a melee mob that walks 10 times faster then you sprint.  

Quick travel seemingly involves completely disconnecting from the game and reconnecting in a new location, even if you've only traveled 20 feet to a QT point.  This means you can also get stuck in an infinite loop load screen traveling between two points as well as trying to connect to the game.  

No Global chat, only party chat, so if you're running solo you will always be running solo (aside from rando pvp brawls) until they change that.  The eating portion of survivability seems more like a tacked on after thought that's annoying more then anything useful, especially for those OCD inclined when the "hungry" icon pops up on your screen at 90% full, and the most basic food restores 15%.

It's not a bad game, it is early release, but it can be extremely frustrating with the above issues as the player TTK in many instances can be super quick at melee range and you can't reload.  This is made worse by the nonsensical respawn timer that increases with each death.  Check points for respawn outside of an instanced event/quest zone related situation tend to be 200 to 600 meters from where ever you died so the run time and load screen to get back to the area you died is already a lengthy time gate. The respawn timer as such feels more like an opportunity for the dev to mock the person who died despite it being a lack of functionality on the games part that caused the death.

Aside from that, the story isn't to bad, voice acting is cringe in some spots but that might be intentional as well. I'd honestly probably recommend it if they could fix the load screen and inability to reload your weapons issues sometime soon.  Unless I'm never able to get in again in which case f**k this game lol.
Really bad endgame design, there's no point in playing it and is not worth your time at this stage.

(release review)
I'd love for this game to be actually working, because when it does. Its very enjoyable. Gunplay is nice too. And the guns feel nice and powerful. (At times.) Looting at times feels meh though. But i'll expand on that later. Leveling up is a little bit of a grind.

But the constant state of lag, rubber banding, causing you to die, miss shots. PVP is unplayable because of this. But regular gameplay as well, since you'll at times get random damages to something you don't see? (Because the enemy warped behind you due to lag). They seriously need to fix this ASAP. Its frustrating. There's also some serious backrolls on your progress as of late as well. There's literally little on what the devs do right now. 

There's also some needless features. Such as trading loading screens. When you finish a trade, it needs to load. (Of course.) But. This takes way too long. Same for dying. You need to wait 10 seconds, but there's an additional few extra 5 seconds because of loading into the map (Due to lag of course.) This also counts for crafting, way too many processing loading screens that slow down gameplay. Its something you'll encounter A LOT. Lots of loading.

Gameplay features such as world events? Useless. If you die during an event and you rejoin. You might not get any rewards. And if you do, they are literally junk rewards. Nothing of value. Daily quests? Pointless loot. Something you can easily find in the world itself. Challanges? Pointless, same worthless unsatisfying loot. I recommend avoiding these features until they actually make them worth while. Traders don't sell any worth while loot either.

Doing quests are simple, sometimes fetch quests, kill quests. Etc. The usual mmo shtick. Issue though. If you do quests for NPC's that have a sh*tty attitude to you, finish those quests. You'd be expecting that'll change their behaviour towards you, nope. NPC's don't seem to react to the things you've done for them.

This game needs some serious work, and i don't usually say this, just check my other reviews. Gotta put that elbow grease on ya' and get to work. Until then, don't buy this game until they've fixed these issues. Its not worth it.

Edit: Its been a while. Still no fixes or changes. Don't buy this game. I wished I could've refunded it. Again, dont buy this.

I really wanted to enjoy this game but couldn't. I can live with general jank, poor English localization, and I even put a ton of effort to find/create a controller layout that worked (outside of menus, that is).

The game has no precompilation for shaders which leads to constant stuttering until you've seen every asset and effect, and it is much more negatively impactful here than in similar games I've played (usually, i can live with one-time stutter). Once you make your way out of the city into the first "true" open world zone, you start teleporting around/rubber banding - it seems as if the server authorizes position rather than client. I connected to a fairly close server and actively play other online games without any such issue, so no connection issues on my end. The rubber-banding is not an "every movement" type of issue (in fact it may be crossing certain boundaries/objects), but it is enough to make the game unenjoyable.

I may come back and check the game out in the future when things like shaders, netcode, and controller support are up to par. But for now, the game isn't worth playing.

As a stalker fan I was excited to try this game, but I can't recommend it until the developers address ongoing security warnings.

Since launch, Bitdefender and other antivirus software flag the game executable (pioner-win64-shipping.exe) for attempting phishing by connecting to a suspicious endpoint. The devs acknowledged this months ago, calling it a "false positive" and saying they'd fix it….you surrre about that?

The advice given by some in the community is concerning just whitelisting it when even the devs haven't explained why it keeps happening is crazy.

I'm not saying the game is malware. I'm saying the developers have had plenty of time to resolve what they claim is a simple false positive, and they haven't. That raises questions.

If you choose to play keep this in mind, you don't get hacked or exploited right away. Don’t trust people just saying it's fine they will not help you when you're screwed. And will just say it's your fault. Some of these collection types are stored for months or years with your data until you think it didn't come from them before attacking.

I’ll keep an eye out for updates and will update once it's fixed to focus on the game itself.

I don’t usually leave reviews because most of the time I just play a game, shrug, and move on. But this one annoyed me enough that I actually felt the need to write something.

What state is this game even in? Early Access? Nah, this feels more like a pre alpha test build that somehow escaped into the wild. I played the beta, and anyone who did knew straight away this wasn’t even close to being ready. The amount of bugs is unreal, and not the funny kind, the frustrating, progress breaking kind.

Some of the game design choices genuinely make you stop and go “wtf?”. For example, you’re on a solo mission, you die, and boom - back to the start. No checkpoint, no mercy. I completed a side quest, moved on to another one, died during that… and for some reason the game decided I needed to redo the first side quest again? You start a world event, die, the game gives you an option to spawn on the nearest checkpoint and it decides to spawn you on the furthest one? Makes absolutely no sense and just wastes your time.

The devs have said PvP “isn’t necessary” yet one of the main quests is placed inside a PvP zone. Yeah, that area definitely won’t be camped by players at all… great design choice there.

It’s also advertised as an MMO, which is honestly laughable. The server issues alone make it barely functional, if this was a single-player game it would still be unacceptable, let alone something that relies on online stability.

Right now? It’s just shit. Plain and simple. Which is a shame, because there are bits I like - the setting is cool, and on paper this could’ve been a STALKER style multiplayer experience, which sounds amazing. Instead, we’ve ended up with this mess.

Maybe one day it’ll get there. But in its current state, this game shouldn’t have been released at all.
The gameplay is fun, the graphics are decent, and the story is interesting, but when your servers can't support 1k players and you are advertising this as an mmo... thats not good.

This game is a masterclass in how to squander potential at every conceivable level. From the moment it launches, it signals its lack of respect for the player’s time, intelligence, and money.
The gameplay is painfully repetitive and mechanically shallow. Core systems feel unfinished, unbalanced, or outright broken, as if they were stitched together hours before release. Controls are unresponsive, animations are stiff, and combat devolves into mindless button-mashing with no tactical depth or sense of progression. Any challenge comes not from clever design, but from cheap difficulty spikes and poorly tuned enemies.
The technical state is indefensible. Bugs are constant and immersion-breaking: frequent crashes, clipping issues, AI that fails to function, and quests that simply do not complete. Performance is abysmal, with severe frame drops and loading times that belong to a different decade. It feels less like a finished product and more like a rushed beta that was pushed out the door prematurely.
The narrative, if it can be called that, is incoherent and lazily written. Characters are one-dimensional, dialogue is wooden, and plot points are introduced and abandoned with no payoff. There is no emotional engagement, no world-building worth caring about, and no justification for the hours the game demands from the player.
Visually, the game is equally disappointing. Environments are bland and repetitive, asset reuse is obvious, and the art direction lacks any cohesive identity. The audio design fares no better: forgettable music, poor sound mixing, and voice acting that ranges from disinterested to outright embarrassing.
Ultimately, this game feels like a cynical cash grab—an exercise in mediocrity wrapped in marketing promises it fails to deliver on. There is no redeeming feature substantial enough to justify playing it, let alone purchasing it. It is not just disappointing; it is a waste of time.

I wanted to love it. People mentioned the likes of Stalker and Metro as its influences. That was music to my ears. There are obvious superficial similarities. Anomalies, scavenging, etc. But where Metro and Stalker excelled, an engaging loop of exploration, danger, and discovery, this game struggles. 

The world feels empty, but not in a mystical sense like Stalker. It just feels devoid of things to do or feel. 
The enemies are linear and offer little excitement, at least at this point. The map lacks distinction. It feels like it was generated from some mmo template.

Maybe one day it will be ready for the Stalker comparison on a more meaningful level but it’s not there yet.

Despite my negative review I wish this game well and hope it improves over time. I’ll check back in a year.

I really love the overall concept of the game and the diversity of its landscapes. The weapons feel satisfying to use, and the attachments genuinely add depth and utility. The story is there and easy to follow, which is great — though that’s not where my main concerns lie.

Issue 1: Performance and FPS Stability
For context, I’m running a GTX 4070 Ti, AMD Ryzen 9 5950X, and 64GB of DDR4 3600MHz RAM — so performance shouldn’t be an issue. However, FPS stability is very inconsistent. Frame drops happen frequently enough to be noticeable. Some optimization would be great before the developers focus on adding more content.

Issue 2: Reload Animation Bugs
Possibly related to performance, but I’ve had to tap reload seven to nine times before the game registers the action. Sometimes the animation completes, yet the magazine doesn’t actually reload. This becomes especially frustrating during large fights (15+ enemies) or in PVP encounters where timing is everything.

Issue 3: Falling Physics
Falling feels off — almost like your character has their own personal gravity well. You hit terminal velocity instantly, and even small steps off a ledge can send you flying forward at high speed. I once crouch-walked off a platform and was launched about 20 feet ahead within a second.

Issue 4: Objective Marker Visibility
The objective markers tend to blend too much into the environment. During my first world event with the Raven Cultists sacrificing Brigade members in pyres, the objective was to “put out the fires” — but there was no indication of how or where. Being the only player there, with enemies constantly respawning, it took me almost the entire event to notice the marker hidden behind all the on-screen chaos.

Final Thoughts
The game has a ton of potential, and I genuinely enjoy the ideas and atmosphere. But improving the gameplay consistency, performance, and clarity of objectives would make the experience much smoother and more enjoyable overall. I will continue to play this game, but for now I cannot recommend until some improvements are made to smooth out the moment to moment gameplay. 

If anyone has any tips on improving the performance, I left the comments open for suggestions.

I've been holding off on giving this game a review because I really wanted to give it a fair chance and put it through its paces.
But unfortunately after ~33 hours (at time of review) I've concluded that the game is simply too broken to be able to recommend in any good faith. In spite of whatever positives it may have, or what few there are anyway.

The elevator pitch for this game is something like 'STALKER but Fallout 76'
But they also decided to keep all the horrible game breaking bugs of Fallout 76 too.

Without going on and on about every single bug I encountered, it really boils down to the game and server simply refusing to function as expected or even within reason. This coupled with an already clunky game itself aided by similarly clunky design all culminates in what is unfortunately an incredibly underwhelming and disappointing experience.

Though some credit where credit is due, the game does have most if not all dialogue fully voiced, which is great.
But if you don't really care about the story or the dialogue, that doesn't leave much else.

The gunplay is barely functional, the weapon customization is fairly inconsequential, and the weapon variety is all but absent. While there are a solid handful available, there really is precious little to distinguish any weapon from another of the same class, or even iterations of the same weapon. While higher tier weapons do get very minor perks, most do precious little to really affect gameplay in any meaningful or noticeable manner.
On top of this is how rare most weapons actually end up being. Though there are crates around the world that always spawn and typically contain a random weapon, they frequently contain a similar and small variety of weapons in my experience. Meaning you can loot the same chest maybe a dozen times and get the same two or three weapons. And while that may just be bad RNG, it certainly begs the question of how many weapons even [i]are[/i] really in the game to be able to keep getting the same ones by dumb luck alone.
And as mentioned before, any two iterations of the same weapon are going to be statistically and functionally identical, as in, no randomized stats. Which for some might be a good thing because at least when you get a gun, you know what you're getting in terms of stats/functionality. But it does make looting the same items over and over excruciatingly repetitive and boring. A little randomization would definitely make acquiring multiples of the same weapon a little more interesting because the varying stats could affect how they're used. But that's just my personal taste more than anything.

The quests can be pretty hit or miss. Again, credit where credit is due, it does seem a fair amount of thought and effort [i]has[/i] gone in to designing storylines. And even though I admittedly haven't paid much attention to most of them, the little bits I've gleamed [i]do[/i] seem reasonably well thought out. I just personally don't care for any of that. But the real let down of quests is that most yield pretty useless or meaningless rewards.
A majority of the time all you get is a fistful of currency, a few consumable items, and usually a card item for the games in-game card game battler. Which if you never interact with that minigame (which there isn't much point to outside of boredom), these cards will be completely useless to you as a player.

And just to round out the review because it's already getting pretty long in the tooth, I just want to quickly mention that the anomalies in the game are annoying more than anything. Though not horrifically frequent, they are often littered all over the roads between POI's and in later area's of the game make certain sections of the map nearly impassable and for no real reason other than to justify their existence.
Also the item durability mechanic is just plain irritating. While not as bad as some games, personally I'm just sick of item durability in games, especially online games. Especially considering each death degrades all equipped items ~10% or so, which the game never even explains, you just suddenly notice when all your equipment is about to break. Of which this durability requires resources to repair (higher tier resources for higher tier items).
Even if the argument is to try to encourage players to use lower tier gear normally and only take out higher tier gear when strictly necessary, the game still does a very poor job of this. At best, it's poorly executed, at worst, it's poorly designed. In either case it makes for a very unenjoyable experience. And while gathering resources to repair isn't enormously difficult, it's just an extra layer of tedium that simply doesn't need to be there.

Were this a free-to-play game, it might at least be worth a try.
But for $30, absolutely stay as far away as you can.
I do hope the game improves over time, but it is not worth playing in its current state.

As much as I am thoroughly enjoying this game its next to impossible to recommend it in its current state. I have an AMD Ryzen 7 7800X3D, Gigabyte GeForce RTX 4070 Ti Super, Corsair Vengeance 32gb 5200 MHz DDR5, ASUS TUF Gaming B650 - Plus AM5 and it is installed on an M.2. The bugs and issues make it so difficult to play. Ive tried too play this on max, medium and low graphics reaching 600fps then also setting a cap at 180 incase the game couldnt handle the frames with no change.

I have multiple bugs where my gun breaks mid fight but stays out and stops me from being able to switch weapon or remove it in my inventory until I die, I have died because on occasions enemies shoot through multiple walls with 0 line of sight, I have died to invisible enemies that show up in the damage log, I have randomly died in safe zones with the damage log saying i have dealt damage to myself.

The looting system is extremely buggy where instead of being able to open containers they just glow a faint yellow and does not let you interact until you reboot the game and even leaving to the main menu and rejoining does not work. I have opened multiple trial chests where it randomly either drops the loot for you, the loot goes into your inventory or does not drop any loot at all. 

The fast travel system via the guide also has multiple issues where it seems like the servers cannot load what is happening so instead it boots you back to the main menu over and over until the game is rebooted completely but then using the fast travel spots on the edges of the maps do not bug so its either completely rebooting the game or needing to run the entire length of the area to fast travel.

The actual areas also have multiple bug where the visual of the objects such as stairs do not align with the object until you reboot the game so in order to walk up stairs it makes you walk through a building as it is rendered in the incorrect place.

Their are multiple quests (People with arrow pointing down) where no matter how many times you speak to them they do not give you the quest but just repeat the same thing over and over. There are multiple bugs in quests stopping you from progressing until (You guessed it) you reboot the game and the quest works perfectly fine, notably the Old Friends Lair quest which I am still unable to complete as it will not let the message be listened too no matter how long I wait for it to finish, there is also multiple quests that do not update no matter if you completed the objective or not.

Finally the enemies are so f****ng random, I have died in one shot to an even with a max health buff where on another occasion im taking 5-10 damage per shot. The spawns of the enemies are also extremely random. I have cleared areas and gone back 20 minutes later and the enemies have not respawned and on other occasions ive cleared an area, started looting and have been in the area 5 minutes maximum and they have spawned back in all around me. I have been mid mission and had a random shotgunner spawn behind me and one shot me instantly.  A few times where no matter how much i shoot an enemy they do not take damage and there is no hit marker showing and also enemies who will just randomly fully heal during a fight out of no where but mainly mini bosses such as the swamp thing.

Also random plants just spawn and despawn infront of you randomly while needing to loot them which has also stopped me from completing bounties. I have also not been able to return in bounties at other times so I was just stuck with the trophies.

Finally the design of the quests can be infuriating, upon entering a location no matter how deep in the mission you are if you die it throws you back to the complete start of the mission so if you die on the final boss you have to do the entire place again. The design of the public events are even worse as theres not even an area checkpoint you spawn too, if you die at the very end of the public event you will need to respawn at a near settlement meaning you dont complete the event and miss out on the rewards.

The game is not well optimised at all and a few of the design choices are honestly the worst ive ever seen. However this game has extreme potential, scratches that Stalker itch and is overall an enjoyable and brilliant game. With some bug fixes, some updates and some small changes this game can easily be a top game with endless fun and fantastic content. Unfortunately though I cannot recommend this in its current state.

Потенциально отличная игра подыхает в пучине безалаберности своих же разработчиков. Стабильность серверов оставляет желать лучшего, сюжет вообще ни о чем, пвп-зона просто сломана ВСЯ. Отсутствие внятных чекпоинтов: умер - проходи задание заново. Спасибо что в сюжетке есть хоть какие-то чекпоинты (хотя это не всегда помогает, ведь баги и вылеты никто не отменял). Если ты умер в пвп-зоне - респавн в жопе мира, но только не у края этой самой зоны. Невероятно раздражают аномалии, которые *буквально* на каждом шагу и спокойно могут вынести тебя за пару тычек. Про спавн мобов за пару минут, как только что ты зачистил локацию, вообще промолчу. 
При всём этом имеется неплохой геймплей, сильно напоминающий мне F76, только в сеттинге русской зоны отчуждения. В игре просто приятно исследовать локации, чем я и занимался большую часть времени. Как убивалка времени игра шикарно справляется с этой задачей. А если вчитываться в диалоги квестов, то заметно как разрабы старались над игрой, юмора и интересных решений хватает. Лор всей этой катавасии очень занятный, хоть и намешано всё подряд. Но спорные решения и шероховатость игры сказывается на общем впечатлении. Если разрабы прислушаются к сообществу и начнут исправлять не только баги, но и менять неудачные решения в геймдизайне, то в пионера станет играть куда приятнее.

Boy, I was so hyped for this game! Finally, an apocalyptic MMO game that will scratch the biggest itch. Oh how wrong I was! How do I put this... A game that tries to be similar to games like S.T.A.L.K.E.R., Fallout, Escape from Tarkov(?), but often fails to do so.
Just like S.T.A.L.K.E.R.(of which I'm hardcore fan since the series came out), PIONER has all kinds of anomalies that require very strategic approach and slow gameplay that cannot be executed in a fast pace MMO games. It just doesn't work. On top of it some anomalies are so densely packed in some places that it's just a nightmare to navigate. Especially annoying are those anomalies that almost always damage you no matter how careful you are (like those violet spitting flowers with AoE damage). 
Healing in this game is beyond useless. When you need a quick fix just to have a chance for a quick escape but animation takes ages, slows you down and makes you even more of an easy target to your enemies. Healing can be done only in a safe spot... Ironically, 90% of the times I'm unable to reach a safe spot, see? And once you die in a mission you'll be thrown back to the start of it. Or if you die during event  - you'll most likely fail the event.
AI is terrible and clunky. Instant dashes, no cooldown on attacks, sometimes irresponsive. 
Stash capacity, frankly, is a joke. Crafting materials should have a separate storage! Game takes half of your time to solve dilemma on what to keep and what to scrap or sell. Few moment later you get a quest and realise you just sold quest items FFS!.. It's tedious and exhausting. I get Fallout 76 stash management vibes without 1st subscription - absolute nightmare. Also, some item stacks like fish (lootable from the barrel) or fish bite (that drops from worm mobs) has disappeared from my stash. *Palmface*
Worst part is that game has no dodge or block mechanics. Seriously? No way to protect yourself in a heat of the battle when you're being mashed by a bunch of melee mobs or got under a bullet shower? Front slide won't help often, if at all! The amount of times I died because of this and failed an event! Speaking of event you'll most likely fail them if you're playing solo or don't know what to do exactly and the game doesn't indicate where and what to do, go figure. Attempted like 6-7 event and couldn't finish a single one of them! Such mechanics are not newbie friendly and was a big turn off for me personally.
Skill system is, technically, non-existent. There are few nice passive skills, but no active skills. And maybe it's not a bad idea for a first person MMO shooter... but no HP/Stamina pool increase with level up? Just to rely on two pity food buffs and shabby gear? Yeah, right, good luck with that.

There are a few nice things about this game: the story is engaging, locations are interesting, it's graphically gorgeous and pretty well optimized game even for older machines. But poor game design choices take all the fun from it. I kept pushing hoping it will get better but I just can't get over the mess of this game. Wide as an ocean, deep as a puddle.

Highly doubt I'll ever come back to this game.

The game has potential, but if you're a solo player I'd wait for it to be 50% or more off or much further along in development.  Spamming reload 100 times in the middle of a heavy fight to get your weapon to reload is a constant issue that can quickly result in death if you're fighting a melee mob that walks 10 times faster then you sprint.  

Quick travel seemingly involves completely disconnecting from the game and reconnecting in a new location, even if you've only traveled 20 feet to a QT point.  This means you can also get stuck in an infinite loop load screen traveling between two points as well as trying to connect to the game.  

No Global chat, only party chat, so if you're running solo you will always be running solo (aside from rando pvp brawls) until they change that.  The eating portion of survivability seems more like a tacked on after thought that's annoying more then anything useful, especially for those OCD inclined when the "hungry" icon pops up on your screen at 90% full, and the most basic food restores 15%.

It's not a bad game, it is early release, but it can be extremely frustrating with the above issues as the player TTK in many instances can be super quick at melee range and you can't reload.  This is made worse by the nonsensical respawn timer that increases with each death.  Check points for respawn outside of an instanced event/quest zone related situation tend to be 200 to 600 meters from where ever you died so the run time and load screen to get back to the area you died is already a lengthy time gate. The respawn timer as such feels more like an opportunity for the dev to mock the person who died despite it being a lack of functionality on the games part that caused the death.

Aside from that, the story isn't to bad, voice acting is cringe in some spots but that might be intentional as well. I'd honestly probably recommend it if they could fix the load screen and inability to reload your weapons issues sometime soon.  Unless I'm never able to get in again in which case f**k this game lol.

This game feels like Fallout, STALKER and Gothic 3 had a wild night out and this is what came of it. Why this is good and bad i will share:

First the Good:

- The Atmosphere is great. Just like in the STALKER games, the environment is hostile, everything wants to kill you and anomalies will make your life hard
- Graphics are good, not mindblowing by any means but its pretty
- The Core Gameplay loop is fun
- The World is big and rewards exploration on every corner with challanges, enemy camps, secrets and more.
- The Russian Voice acting is good. I dont know if this also uses AI like other languages but it sounds awesome (i am german, i have no idea what they say and if its AI, but i like it)
- Despite the fact, that this is supposed to be an MMO, i have been a solo Player for over 70 hours now and can say: If you wanna play this game strictly solo, you can. Nothing prevents you from it.
- PVP is optional and limited to specific small Areas in various Zones. And you dont loose everything you have when you die there
- Music is suttle and atmospheric

Now lets get to the bad

- Its buggy (as expected of an Early Access Game) but sometmes has gamebreaking bugs (like you cannot continue the Main Story wich locks you out of many endgame Activities) "UPDATE": This has been fixed since i posted this review)
- Matchmaking for the 2 Raids is mostly fast, but it does not matter wich Raid you are looking for or what difficulty. It seems the game puts you into one at random every time. And you apparently can only matchmake for Extreme Raids, when at least 1 other player is in your group, you CAN NOT solo queue for Extreme Raids, wich is quite the bummer because they are not really that difficult
- The game is labeled as an MMO and as such, it is always online. This hurts the game in almost every single aspect of it: Long loading times, performance issues, connection issuse with sudden disconnects at random times etc. 
And the worst part of this: Its not even an MMO. There are exactly 2 Things in the Game, you can do in groups (limited to 3 ppl): 

1.) Raids (imo wrong name, its just 3 Player dungeons) of wich there are 2 right now in the game (Normal and Hardcore Version for each). 
2.) World Quests (not limited to 3 ppl, everyone can join in. 
     (Quests are mostly SP so i will not list questing here)

- The Game looks A LOT better in the Trailers, even with everything maxed out you will not manage to get this type of Visuals, no chance
-Clans are completely broken right now. Tried to create one, it failed, next day i suddenly had my own Clan that i created the Day before and since the last patch, my Clan has completely vanished and i cant create a new one
- HUD Information is terrible. No buff indication, no weapon degredation UI (Weapons and Armor will break often and you dont get any info on it.
- The game has a bad respawn system. When you die, most of the time, you wil be sent back to the last outpost you visited, NOT the last spot where your quest was updated or anything. This mean after (most) deaths, you have to walk quite a bit to get back where you started) Really annoying 
- Quest Descriptions are bad most of the times. You could not do most quests, if you would just read their description. This needs to be fixed, since not all quests have map markers to show you where you have to go (actually a good thing, makes you explorer the world more)
- No realtime Shadows or Muzzle Flashes. Makes seeing enemies at Night a horrible experience. The only way to do it, is using the ingame Flashlight Attachement for your Gun.
- Invisible Walls at every corner. You think you can get across here or cutoff here, but NO, you cant. Makes exploration a little tedious.
- Sounds often dont match the environment you are in.
- Entering Raids in only possible in Wandering Pass. No Searching for you while you do quests etc, no, you just have to stand there and wait while you do nothing
- Only one Character for all Servers. And you cant delete this one so you better make sure you dont screw up.
- No Respec Option (again, make sure you dont blow it)
- No Option to restart or cancel Quests. Really annoying if you have a quest that is stuck and wont let you progress.
- Crafting is annoying. Especially salvaging items, because you have to salvage every item on its own and have to go back into the menu every single time. Let us mark Items and salvage them all at once
- No Map markers to Mark Points of Intrest. Better print out the Map and start taking notes.
- HUB Areas and Camps are way to big for what they are. A lot of times this game feels like a running simulator

I love this game very much and dont mind the flaws it has right now,  but it needs a lot of work. It has the Atmospheric Style and Visuals of STALKER, the interesting Places on every corner of the map from Fallout and sadly the buggy, unpolished and sometimes gamebreaking Problems, Gothic 3 had in its days.

After 30 hours of gameplay I will comment on - the open world, questing, progression, developers, bugs, QOL features, NPC.

Open world

There are currently 4 maps in this game, they are not connected seamlessly. Traveling between them requires either speaking to a fast travel NPC or going to a transit area.

Due to the large map size, traversal is very tedious, like a running simulator even with teleports unlocked. If you're going on foot, expect frequent encounters with an excessive amount of anomalies.

Many POI marked with (?) are copy paste NPC camps that you can't interact with other than trade (they all sell the same thing so theres no incentives to explore much) Most buildings are not enterable. There are occasional puzzles, but several are currently bugged.

The maps are large but content wise empty.

Questing

Quest rewards are extremely underwhelming relative to progression needs.

- Main, side, and daily quests gives very little XP, item rewards are rare or trash.

- Side quests are mostly "go there, kill that, return"

As a result, quests do very little to boost your characters, especially compared to other MMOs, making it quite pointless to talk to random NPC.

Progression

Progression is heavily locked behind raids, an instanced game mode.

If you don't participate in raids, you'll struggle significantly in later zones like Midlands. Story and side quests do not provide viable armor or weapons, so you're forced into raids to obtain better gear.

The raids themselves are fine, but progression should not rely almost entirely on instanced content in an MMO game whose selling point is the open world and NPC interaction.

Additionally due to low player counts, matchmaking for raids generally takes very long, you'll be lucky if you even find one, and solo play is not an option in the game.

QOL feaetures

This game lacks many basic QOL features

- Selling items requires clicking each individually, with ~1 second loading per sale

- Trader requires full dialogue interaction just to access menus, including when exiting.

- World events does not have a teleport to option, causing most events to be empty (despite patch note claiming this was added, it is not in game)

- No checkpoints in missions, dying 10 minutes in resets progress

- Healing requires a 2 second animation, which option leads to death in combat.

- Very few teleport points despite massively empty map.

- Player hubs are oversized with horrendous layout. Traders are spread out with no clear indicators where they're at, stash access is only via physical chests, placed far from traders, with no menu access.

NPC

Quest related NPC interactions are ok. AI voice is weird but tolerable. However functional NPCs (traders, teleporters) forces full dialogue interaction before you can access them, which is annoying.

Enemy NPC behaviour is inconsistent

- Erractic movement

- Spawning randomely around you, especially dogs and worms, causing cheap deaths.

Enemies are also pure bullet sponges, with bosses simply having more health.

Bugs

Surprisingly there's not alot of bugs, but that doesn't make the gameplay any better. Note that many players who called bugs just experienced week 1 server instability.

Developers

The game is being developed by GFAgames a studio formed in 2021, Pioner is their debut project and a very ambitious one, MMO especially requires lots of funding and developer skills, currently the team has no track records, and have already shown several major red flags that are considered amateur for game developers.

- States an updated feature has been implemented -> Update is not in game

- Certain parts of UI elements and buttons are misaligned or inconsistent (small issue on paper but major red flag, UI polish is one of the easiest things to get right)

- Majority of playtest complaints are present in early access.

I did enjoy what little content Expedition to the Backrooms currently offers. The atmosphere feels genuinely unsettling, and the environments capture the eerie realism that fans of the Backrooms are looking for. That said, while the foundation is promising, there are far more negatives than positives in the game’s current state. I’m honestly unsure whether more content and fixes are planned, because right now it feels unfinished and unstable.
Here are the main issues I ran into:
Crashes & Respawning
I can’t properly respawn or retry sections because the game crashes instead. This makes dying incredibly frustrating and discourages experimentation or exploration.
Performance Issues
The game is very laggy. Lowering the graphics does help somewhat, but performance still feels inconsistent and unpolished.
Co-op Problems
Co-op frequently kicks players out of the session, which completely breaks immersion and makes multiplayer unreliable.
Entity Toggle Not Working
Even when selecting the option to disable entities, they still appear in-game. This defeats the purpose of having the option in the first place.
Severe Lack of Content
There are only about three actual levels plus the hub, and most of the hub doors can’t even be opened—only two are usable.
What is the actual purpose of the “research center” in the middle of the yellow rooms? There’s no meaningful follow-up. You just find a few random pieces of paper that don’t actually do anything. It feels pointless and half-baked.
Poorly Explained & Underdeveloped Mechanics
The mechanics need significant work, especially in Level 2. It took an absurd amount of time to figure out that tasks require your hands to be empty.
There should be:
A proper inventory system to equip or store items
Clear prompts or tutorials
A stamina bar
Nutritional items or supplements to restore stamina
On top of that, tools are only really used in Level 2, which makes them feel underutilized.
Map Design Issues
While the yellow rooms/offices feel large at first, each area is actually quite small when explored individually. This makes exploration repetitive and boring rather than tense or mysterious.
Endings Feel Like a Mistake
The game really shouldn’t have two “endings.” One of the biggest appeals of the Backrooms is the idea that it’s endless, with countless levels and no real escape. Including endings just feels like lazy writing—or like the project was abandoned halfway through and released anyway.
Final Thoughts
There’s potential here. The atmosphere works, and the concept is strong. But right now, Expedition to the Backrooms feels more like an early prototype than a complete game. Until major fixes, expanded content, and mechanical improvements are made, it’s hard to recommend beyond curiosity alone.

The only thing that will keep you playing this game are the people you meet. However, it will only keep you playing for so long. You can have an enjoyable time playing Fate War as a F2P player but don't expect to contend with the whales because they will absolutely crush you. I was a Top 30 player and an R4 of the second ranked tribe on my server and I have to admit that I had a lot of fun playing Fate War. I'd say give it a try and don't let FOMO take over your mind if your deciding to go in as a F2P or low spender. Like I said before, If your not going all out by throwing thousands into this game you won't be able to contend with those who are, plain and simple. Overall, I had a blast engaging with everyone on my server and in my tribe; however, everything tends to become dull after a king is chosen on the server. After that, I lasted maybe a month or so and then decided to quit.

I tried this game, hoping 2025 mobile-MMOs had finally improved. They haven’t, just gotten prettier. This game is very popular, and I understand why people may want to play it --- it's a waifu girl on your screen... but it's not worth your $1.  

This is just a desktop-pet you drag to a location on your desktop and they exist there. You can set a certain pose, but you need to go through the dialog constantly to keep re-setting them into that certain pose.  You can download a custom model for your girl, but when you choose 'costume 2' or 'costume 3', it will revert back to it's original model.  The 'costume' dialog menu is completely meaningless in this game if you want to use a non-enormous boobed-character.  That leaves you with only the 'pose' dialog menu, and although I found a nice character to use, I found myself having to go back into the dialog menu and choose the same pose over and over.  If you touch this thing, it will revert back to whatever default previous pose.

Why are people even bothering with this program.  DON'T waste your money on this app.  It doesn't talk to you, you can't do anything with it, and it's ugly as sin.  

In my opinion, the best part about this whole experience is that I only wasted $1 on it.  That's your selling feature -- you will only waste $1 if you buy this app.  It has nothing to offer of any value or substance.  If they were to update this with costumes or items in some way (like other apps in this space), I would be all-over it, if properly implemented.  Especially with workshop 3D VRM support, the custom-models and costume-world could go a LONG way, this is a billions-of-dollars market potential and "Your Mom" is a bit of a market leader in user-count, but in feature-count they are at the bottom.  

I am hopeful that "Your Mom" lives up to it's current popularity spike and evolves this genre to the next level, if otherwise I would need some other understanding or circumstance to explain to me why it makes sense that 2000-4000 people are playing a game of a basic model avatar on their desktop.   https://steamdb.info/app/4147820/charts/

It's really just a static voluptuous female figure that you can zoom in and out, dress however you want, and pose whatever you want. It doesn't move from point A to B and you just drag her wherever you want.

Waiting for 8 hours to get all achievements was also a chore.

Not for me. I hope I can still refund it. Thanks for the game anyway,

"[Nightmare Files] Clap Clap" is at least mildly amusing in terms of the premise, even if the gameplay is a bit weak.  This Itchio game marries a joke premise to your basic Unity kitbashed first person horror adventure minigame (there's thousands of these on Steam).  This one makes fun of the old 1980's clapper sound sensitive light switches where you clap your hands to turn on and off a light.  

What do you do in the game?  The idea of this Russian shovelware from Itch is that you're house sitting and you do a few first person adventure game chores like watering flowers etc, talk to some guys in the neighbourhood who give you hints there's something wrong.  As your day progesses, the expected strange noises and things happen in the house and you end up wandering around, walking into dark rooms and clapping... with scripted events making something run out of the corner of your eyes, rooms suddenly being overturned, scratches on the walls and rubber ducks appearing around the place.  Spooky, right.

Itch has one job, and it's not a hard job, but they failed again... to keep amateur stuff in their weird hugbox where everyone claps and says "Good job!!!" at every bit of amateur drivel (which nobody actually plays) and away from gamers.  Unfortunately this has leaked out onto Steam platforms and now gamers have to put up with yet another bit of poorly made amateur shovelware.

I'm not sure if this is an asset flip, the assets are pretty poor quality, but the game quality is worse.  The muddy textures are pretty awful and it makes this feel a bit like it was made as a joke game. 

Now on to pricing and value for gamers.

This is "free" enough (not every "free" game in the "free" section is truly free, there's usually some hidden price tag), at least you won't be expected to spend any more money after you download this, but just because something is free doesn't mean it's good, and the cost of a game is more than just financial, your time is valuable too.

Given that this free title won't increase your game collector count, is it worth your time to add it to your library?

I think you should give this one a miss. Although it's free, your time and storage are valuable resources, and the market is filled with superior options.

I was such a big fan of MSC, I have a total of 300+ hours in it. I'm not shy to the fact that the game punishes you. I love that. I love that the game doesn't hold your hand at all and you are just free to go get yourself killed in pursuit of the plot. I always play permadeath, and at times, the janky deaths and problems add charm. It really feels like a finnish dude made a game about a year he had in a weird little hometown. 

But to this game. MWC is so obviously thrown together with the framework of the first that you cannot convince me in the slightest that Topless didn't start making this anymore than a year ago. I'm so beyond certain that this game was scraped together to make money when the money from MSC had been gone through and he thought another game might top him back off. People keep giving the same tired excuse of early access without ever mentioning that MSC was in EA until LAST YEAR. So patronize me. Did he start making the sequel before he finished the first one? or did it take less than a year to finish this one? EA is a sorry excuse to capitalize on a semi finished game anymore. In any case, this means nothing to the facts.  Minor problems exist in 1:1 scale in this, proving that it IS the same game. .Just a some small examples. Temo originally stood in the left most counterspace when it was separated in MSC. The character model still sits in the exact same spot he did, except now the desk no longer is there. This copied code also exists in the death credit scene. You can pick strawberries in MSC as a (shitty) side job, yet no strawberries in grow in winter, and there is still an indicator for how many strawberries you picked. I'm pretty sure he mentioned fixing it in the last log but that doesn't change the fact that it is a copy paste of the old game. The magazine parts are all exactly the same with slightly modified visuals, but still in the exact same positions. the exact same font. I waited for this game for YEARS and got a complete imitation game with snow. It serves it purpose. There are some elements that change it up just enough to potentially be the starting point for someone looking for a MSC experience, but id never play this as a sequel. Its quirky. Its got the charm. It screws with you in similar ways the MSC did. Yet I cant fully support the game with the lack of effort that was so obviously put into it. [h1]The matter of replayability[/h1]

Well, here it is. The long-awaited sequel to one of the more unique and interesting games of fairly recent times. My Winter Car provides a refreshing experience for those who have played My Summer Car a lot up to this point. The new project car is fun to build and drive, provided that it remains together once it is assembled. The new additions such as the new jobs, the new convenience store, and the change in season provide changes to the way the game is played, which help distinguish it from it's predecessor. But within all of the good, the bad, and occasional ugly changes to the game, I found one thing that bothers me more than the rest. I also haven't noticed people talking about it a whole lot yet. So here's the issue. My Summer Car is replayable, while My Winter Car isn't.

[h2]The good[/h2]

My Winter Car isn't all bad when it comes to replayability. In fact, there is one new addition that could be really good for the replayability aspect of the game. The addition in question is the VIN plate system, and the randomization of the project car's starting state for every save to have this system work. 

If you have ever played My Summer Car, and have gotten far enough in the game to have that game's project car be in a driveable state, then you may have considered restoring it to it's factory condition. Then again, you may have completely ignored this, because the idea of restoring the car to it's factory condition in that game was rather vague, and unrewarding. The main issues with factory restoration of that game's project car is that there was no indication what was fitted to it when it rolled off the factory, and that there was no form of objective reward for achieving that goal. While the lack of part variety did help indicate what was, and what wasn't installed to the car, the general issue was that there was not a whole lot of reasons to go for it. 

With the VIN plate system, the player can now decode the VIN plate on their car to figure out what kind of parts should be fitted on it, if they want to restore it to it's factory condition. On top of there being a way to figure out what was exactly fitted to the car from the factory, there's now the possibility to have the car go through an historical inspection, where the car is inspected to see if it has been restored to it's factory condition. For passing this inspection, you get an special register plate, and a longer interval between the next inspection. It's not much, but it's an improvement over the previous game. 

What really makes this shine is the large amount of different variables that affect the state of the project car you're buying. It could be something small, like a difference between having hubcaps for your wheels and not having them, but it could be something much bigger, like having a different type of transmission, axle lock, braking power, body panels, engine power, and many more things that could make a difference in how interesting the car could be to restore to it's factory condition. And as a side effect, this provides more modularity to the project car of this game. For example, you could choose to have an automatic or an manual gearbox in the car. Maybe put on some cool factory wheels, if the aftermarket rims feel too expensive. Or even give the car different look thanks to the facelift providing a different set of body panels. And there's more of these little things that can help with part variety not just for those looking to complete a restoration project, but for everyone.

[h2]The bad[/h2]

The VIN plate system is great, but it's being bogged down by one major gameplay element. This element is the parts acquisition process. Unlike My Summer Car, the player does not start with a majority of the important parts required to make the car driveable. Instead the parts have to be bought from second-hand dealers. And currently the main method of acquiring parts is through the classifieds magazine. To explain it shortly, you buy the current issue of the magazine, you go through the listings, call the numbers of the listings that you want to buy from, and then wait for the parts to arrive through mail. This system isn't that bad on the surface, but the longer a playthrough lasts, the more flawed it becomes.

The main issue I have with this system is that it makes it so that acquiring car parts is largely luck-based. While there are listings that mention what they are selling, there's also a lot of listings that don't mention what they are selling. At first, this isn't a problem, because you get a dozen parts with the project car at best. Therefore, buying in bulk doesn't seem that bad of an idea, and it could even provide you with what feels like an fairly speedy start to the process. However, the system becomes annoying once you're down to a few specific parts that you need for the car to be operational. This leads to an annoying cycle where you're hoping to either get good guaranteed listings on the magazine, or have to take a risk with uncertain listings that could either be a total waste of money, or provide you with exactly with what you need. Money can also be pretty tight, if you're not taking a risk gambling with the ventti dealer. This leads to the game slowing down, as you try to balance your financial situation, and the rate at which you acquire car parts, so that you don't end up overspending.

[h2]A potential solution[/h2]

The game currently revolves around an largely luck-based system, which slows down the game a lot. For context, it took me around 50 to 60 hours to acquire all of the mandatory parts for the car. At the latter half of that journey, I was feeling frustrated, not because the game was too difficult, but instead because it was too tedious. Compare this to My Summer Car, where a good player can get the car started in around 2 hours, and I think there's a clear issue here. The main difference between these games is that My Summer Car had an almost identical starting position for every save, where as My Winter Car can have a lot more different variables that affect a playthrough. The issue is that My Winter Car relies too much on randomization at the moment, which makes it so that it takes a very long time to get to the main point of the game.

So, how about adding some consistency to the game? As I stated, the main issue is the over-reliance on the classifieds magazine. Despite this, I don't want to modify the classifieds magazine itself. The idea of a cheap source for buying parts in a bulk is good on it's own. What I would like to add to the game is a form of spare parts shop, where you can find a large variety of used car parts at all times. By this, I mean that there should both be parts from various categories, such as suspension, interior, body, and auxiliary parts. And I think that the variety in these categories should be something where you can find a majority of the parts in each category at all times, but not every part. The parts should also be sold at a slight premium compared to the magazine listings. This way it doesn't encourage the player to entirely ignore the classifieds magazine, and instead offers an alternative source for those who want to speed things up a little bit, and are willing to spend some extra money for an guaranteed, and instant access to certain parts.

Overall, the game has a lot of potential to surpass it's predecessor in how enjoyable it could be to revisit, but it's currently being bogged down by an gameplay loop that discourages players from starting a new save file, if they were to somehow lose it. Some users have estimated the gameplay time required for building the project car to range from 20 to 80 hours currently. I think that's too much, if this game is going to have any chance of being remembered as fondly as it's predecessor. An average of 20 to 40 hours would be a good step forward, and 10 to 20 hours, if a player knows what they are doing.

I enjoyed the first game, this one.... It's hard and unforgiving, which is great, but some things just either frustrate for no reason other than to be frustrating or poor design choices. 

1. UX / UI. Game design on assembling the car, has you chasing invisible anchor points for parts. 
2. Physics... Sometimes, you try to push something and nothing happens, another time you walk past a car and it flies into a wall and flips on it's roof inside your garage.... Nothing about skill or being difficult, just frustrating. 
3. No way to get unstuck. In a jank game like this, your character can get stuck in geometry of the game. If that happens you are done. Options are wait to die and respawn miles away and die from cold or die permanently die to permadeath.
4. Part gathering is just frustrating. Feels like you are getting loot boxes through classifieds. Good luck finding the part that you need and hording piles of unused parts. 
5. Sometimes, you would drive at a few mph in snow, run over a hump of snow, same as all the ones you ran over before, and the physics think you had a head on collision and you die. 
6. Biggest issue NO SAVE. You can only save in bathrooms, which can be 15 minutes away IRL. This means you have to commit to certain amount of time at your computer. This is a huge problem for people with jobs, families and commitments, not even taking into consideration that saving a game is a standard UX expirience. Maybe portapotties or some outdoor bathrooms, but no, I only saw 2 in my 16hrs of playing. 
7. Another problem with saving is it resets your car status. It takes a good amount of time to warm up a car in this game and defrost or scrape ice from windows. Now, when you save a game, you are taken back to the main menu and when you get back into the game, the car is cold and frosted over.

Overall I was OK with a single dev releasing my summer car in a state that it was but I was expecting a sequel to have the same idea but more polish and UX / UI improvements. This game, however, makes things difficult and frustrating for all the wrong reasons.

I wanted to like this but I really don't think this is a great game.

The new features sound good on paper but in reality most are just unfun, don't respect the players time or are plain frustrating. Allow me to list some examples:
- The main job expects you to work for 8 in-game hours, for minimal pay. It would take weeks of working just to afford the crankshaft alone. Besides that, what kind of player wants to do the same repetitive clicking boxes for hours on end?
- The taxi job is super frustrating because the taxi gets stuck in the snow every 3 minutes. This is especially common when picking up and dropping off passengers. When you have arrived at the destination, you'd expect the passenger to say something but instead the only thing that changes is the text in the corner. Not knowing this made finding the right dropoff spot really annoying.
- Buying car parts is essentially a lootbox mechanic where you get random parts every time. If you're unlucky it takes a really long time to get everything.
- It gets way too cold in game. I understand that Finnish winters are cold but -50 degrees is insane. Idling cars becomes a game of continuously tapping the accelerator. Realistic? Maybe. Good game mechanic? I don't think so.
- The long nights are way too dark. I don't know when the developer has gone outside at night for the last time but in real life I can see much more than this even on moonless nights. The ambient light level at night is set to zero meaning objects are only lit under direct light which is not realistic. Especially with headlights on at night there should be some ambient light for objects outside the direct beam, as in real life the light gets reflected. My Summer Car had this issue too but you could get around it by staying home or just sleeping through the night, not really feasible in this game.

These in my view are definite signs that the developer didn't (himself and/or let other people) test the game features as much as is needed. In fact I find myself doubting the developer has even tried doing a complete playthrough from start to finish on his own as a lot of these issues are noticed quickly and easily solved by tweaking some game values.

Then there are the other issues:
- Blatant AI usage for assets. Really?
- No way to pause the game. This is a basic feature that should be in every (single-player) game.
- The map is virtually the same as MSC but with some added/changed buildings and some terrain/texture tweaks. I feel like the dev could have at least added another town or something.
- No English translations in parts critical to game play (ATM and gas pumps)
- The same engine, with the same bugs from MSC.
- Grocery store shelves are completely empty aside from the things from MSC.

Overall I can't recommend this game in it's current state. The game needs more content and the frustration level of the game needs to be toned down a little bit. Until then I will keep playing My Summer Car over this.

First off, I adored My Summer Car. One of my favorite games of all time, so as you can imagine I was beyond hyped for this game. Unfortunately in it's current state it has glaring issues, specifically around building the car. 
1) The loot box style car building sucks. You need a specific part and it takes forever to get it. There should be an option to buy all parts as 'factory'.
2) Due to the large amount of used parts you will undoubtably end up with poor condition parts. But here's the thing, you have no idea if the part is in good condition or bad condition. You could assemble the car perfectly, but due to bad parts it won't run. 
3) After all that pain staking work of finally assembling the car, getting it tuned, getting everything working, it running great etc. I save the game and start it back up and the car is broken. This has happened multiple times, so every time you log out and log back in your car might be broken. Who knows, and there is zero feedback. 
I get the game is supposed to be 'hardcore', but in the real world we can look at a car part and see if it's in good condition or not. We can test if fuel is pumping, and if the spark plugs work. We can test compression etc etc. In My Winter Car you can follow a manual, do things perfectly, tighten every bolt, tune every aspect from the timing, to the distributor to the carb etc etc, and the car may or may not fuction seemingly at random. 
Hopefully in the future they fix it and give more feedback to the player.

I had high hopes for the winter version of this game, but unfortunately it feels like little more than a copy-and-paste of My Summer Car. Beyond a few visual tweaks, there’s almost no new content or mechanics that justify it being a standalone winter experience.

The “problem meter” is just plain irritating, constantly nagging without adding meaningful challenge or realism. Handling is another major disappointment. In My Summer Car, you could feel the difference between driving on asphalt and gravel, which made the driving experience immersive. Here, the car doesn’t even attempt to feel like it’s sliding on snow or ice—the physics are flat and unconvincing.

A winter-themed game should offer unique challenges and sensations, but this version fails to deliver on both fronts. With proper snow and ice handling, thoughtful mechanics, and less intrusive UI elements, it could have been enjoyable. As it stands now, it’s frustrating and feels like a half-hearted re-skin rather than a real winter driving experience.

One of those games that is absolutely amazing with it's depth but almost completely unplayable without heavy mod support to fix the jank and generally bad game design.

Most of the gameplay doesn't actually consist of gameplay, but fighting the game to let you play. Items randomly disappear, your car glitches out and falls out of the map or you just die randomly while driving down the road. All while the game enables permadeath by default which is a brave choice for a game where so many deaths aren't your fault.

There isn't even a way to pause the game. Someone ring the doorbell while your desperately trying to get home because you haven't had your sixth beer in an hour? Hope you won't die of thirst or cold before you come back.
The only way to pause is to exit the game and the only way to do that is to reach one of the very few toilets sprinkled throughout the game.

Even a simple inventory system would be such a massive improvement.

I know the game is still in early access so bugs are to be expected, but looking at their last game it's questionable that this one will be much better (jank-wise) out of early access, from a game design standpoint it certainly won't.

If you desperately want to enjoy this game like me, wait for the mods and community to develop further.

THE GOOD:

Incredibly brutal experience. Cold mechanics have you freeze. Cars don't start and need to warm up. Day is short and you have to deal with the dark. More expenses because you pay rent. Car parts are random in loot boxes you pay for and you have to pick up the car up yourself. Massive electricity bills. You now have a drinking problem and fatigue at 1.5x the normal rate. 

The evolution of the story is on point. The happy Summer months are over. Now its the pain of growing up and surviving in the real-world.


All that said. THE BAD:

The Dev is sadly incompetent with running a business and managing his money... kinda like the MC of the game:

He paid 49% taxes on RAW income amounting to over 3 million dollars just last year to the Finnish government instead of reinvesting that money and taking expenses. Essentially an entire small Dev team, wasted that could have:

- Updated the graphics, engine and textures
- written some music
- implemented quality of life features that modders have to add FOR FREE.
- implemented advanced Winter driving simulations and shades (I mean COME ON, your car doesn't even leave tracks in the snow.
- FINISH the game after 6!!! YEARS and not have it sit in early access.
- and finally develop and refine an IP that has grossed almost 90 million in sales. 


Before I hear a *but acccctually* compare this Dev with Kenshi and Lo-Fi studios. They hired a team, they upgraded the engine to Unreal, they are releasing their game when its DONE and working to improve all aspects instead of peddling watered down slop. 

While still engaging, My Winter Car is a VERY lazy sophomore effort by Topless.

So far, it's nearly the same as My Summer Car.  No, pausing still does not work.   No, there are not audio settings.  Somehow the community is ok with this after 10 years of My Summer Car.  It took you ten years to add snow and different ground textures, a different gas station?  What exactly is different?

Just play My Summer Car if you're interested in something like this.

Or play Mon Bazou, which at least was developed with UI and quality of life in mind, and doesn't outright disrespect your time.

I absolutely loved MSC, this one has a long way to the same effect.
1. It's 1999, carburator again, really? (civic EK and e39 are banging the market in 1999)
2. Winters in Finland are not -40 winters
3. Diesel engines don't care about being left in the cold, gasoline engines cool down to the point of stalling just idling. I would say even with all the additives, in -40 the diesel would be so thick you can cut it with a knife. Again, really? Absurd.
4. Those status bars. A little bit too fast. I'm okay with bringing 1 resource with me on, say, sewage run. But I have to carry food, coffee and booze just to make it back and forth on a single job. Annoying.
5. Taxi pickup and drop off points are so confusing it breaks the game for me. To this moment I wasted 2 hours trying to figure out how to drop off those guys to no effect. Annoying x2
So yeah, don't buy it just yet.
