This game is basically a meme quiz, but in Russian. The only thing I'm really learning here are the titles of popular internet culture memes, but in Russian.
Memology has a simple interface where you have 4 different answers to choose from, with only one being correct with the corresponding image for that question. Except those images are in poor resolution, pixelated, and I'm ... pretty sure sure they're the achievement thumbnails stretched out to fit the game screen resolution. Simple 2-bit music plays in the BG, and the visual effects are very minimal, which allowed for a smooth experience playing. I haven't encountered any bugs nor glitches in my time playing. I refunded the DLC though, as it only served to accomplish the 100% achievements. I wouldn't touch it again or the game for this matter. 
I do hope the devs make an English translation. The store page claims that it supports 103 languages, English included but there is no such options in the game. So ...
As a proper quiz game, I wouldn't recommend it.

MEMOLOGY: GOYDA is a meme quiz game.

This game claims to have English language support as well as 103 languages supported in total, but it is only available in Russian. As a non-Russian speaker, this game requires a guide from start to finish and the guides are... in Russian. The art is made with AI, the game is short and I've seen maybe 5 out of the 50+ memes used in this game.

Overall, a terrible experience with NO ENGLISH SUPPORT despite the claims!

Dear lord. For me, this was unplayable. I really LOVE liminal space and atmospheric horror games so this seemed to be exactly what I'd love, but right off the bat, there's zero volume control. There's background volume control, meaning the volume for when the game is minimized? Which that's crazy how there's no in game way to turn it down because the music and ambient noise is LOUD. Also the text in the game at least for the English translation automatically advances FAAAAST. It's impossible to read. I didn't see a setting to adjust this, but it literally just doesn't stop going. Also the English translation isn't available for the first few documents you find in the game. You can power through this game technically, but c'mon. Zero volume control? It's really imbalanced and very distracting. I can't get invested in the story if that text is going to be zoomin' like that. It looks like others reported this has been updated and fixed, but it's not on my end.

game_name: Stone Simulator – Just Be a Rock
review_text:
This game in it's concept really seemed like so much fun. Just a silly fun time being a rock. For what little I played of it, it was goofy and I enjoyed it. However, at the time of purchasing I didn't see the AI declaration and my heart sunk finding out they used it in the game. I understand the pressure that comes with trying to develop a game and make it accessible but I don't think turning to generative AI is the answer. Even with a game as small and simple as this, and especially not with translation. Even if translation was a slow process I think people appreciate the care put into even the smallest of details instead of turning to a machine that may very well get it wrong.
For those that have no issue with the AI, this game is exactly what it says it is and you'll get a decent laugh out of it. As for me, the Ai usage has put me off it entirely and I think I'll be refunding it sadly.

game_name: Stone Simulator – Just Be a Rock
review_text:
Got the game just for the achievements. It did stress the pc just like other said but it was a given. Then recently the game went nuts. Resolution went to 720p Fullscreen which i was originally playing at 4K Fullscreen. The problem is i can no longer change nor the resolution neither any other settings. Why? the save button FFKKing disappeared. Can't use mouse pointer like normally too i have to use keyboard. At this point I'm past the 2hrs so can't even refund. So had to ignore everything and just let it run until it gets 100%.

Graphics?
It's Beautiful

Is it WORTH it?
Yes, If you have a pc with a decent cooling system

Should You try it?
Yes, ONLY if u got spare money to waste, want the achievements and a possible headache

game_name: Stone Simulator – Just Be a Rock
review_text: As far as i can tell i can't get into multiplayer and, if you hate idle games that play themselves this one is not for you, there are no controls only watching and the game practically plays itself aside from rerolling the game countless times for some selective achievements that's all the game is going to offer.

game_name: Stone Simulator – Just Be a Rock
review_text:
The game was running fine until I tried to change the resolution. Immediately afterward, it suddenly changed the resolution again and the save button disappeared off-screen. The Escape key only gives you the choice between "Back to the main menu" (without saving the changes) or back to the options menu (also without saving the changes. W...T...F...). The config file is a .json, which, without more in-depth knowledge, doesn't offer any way to change the resolution from there.

After uninstalling and reinstalling the game, nothing changed. The save button is still off-screen (I deleted the config file after uninstalling).

Wtf guys...

game_name: Stone Simulator – Just Be a Rock
Stone Simulator – Just Be a Rock
review_text:
Tbh the title doesn't lie, you'll only be a stone doing nothing, and I ain't got anything against this.
However, there are two problems:
Firstly, the store's description mentions they used AI to translate the game, which could be fine (bonus point for honesty, ig), but in-game main menu and loading pictures also seem AI-made as hell, which is utterly disgusting.
Secondly, and most importantly, the game goes crazy over ressource usage, it is REALLY DEMANDING. Idk if the poor optimization is linked to some AI vibe coding, which wouldn't be surprising considering the precedent observation, or if it's just some weird cryptominer scam, but this isn't acceptable at all.
It's just some low-effort money farming slop supported by a sort of funny premise and either scam or plagiarism? Not on my account!
[b] Don't fall for the hype gentlemen [/b]

game_name: Stone Simulator – Just Be a Rock
review_text: It's relaxing and nice to look at, so much for the positives. There's no gameplay because this is an idle game, more like a idle software. To be precise: A stupid screensaver. And that's the problem. It would have been nice if it had been hand drawn or somehow lightweight, but I don't need to run my computer at full throttle for 6+ hours for a UE5 screensaver and a few achievements. I understand it's a meme game, but I don't feel it.

game_name: Stone Simulator – Just Be a Rock
review_text: While this game is very pretty and I went to do all the achievements (which was pretty annoying since the existential crisis one was bugged), Im very dissapointed to see that in the loading screen there's AI pictures used. The game is fine if you just want to put a pretty background for on your computer but then you can better just search a youtube video and put that on.

game_name: Stone Simulator – Just Be a Rock
review_text: I have Nvidia RTX 4060. Have a AMD Ryzen 5 7500f. 32 GB data SSD and why i have 50 FPS on low quality graphics or 30 FPS on HIGH quality??? Nvidia DLSS is ON but this game completely unoptimized!!!

game_name: Stone Simulator – Just Be a Rock
review_text: All fun little jokes and memes aside, a game about a rock, which is really nothing more than a glorified screen saver, should not run this poorly on a modern PC.

game_name: Stone Simulator – Just Be a Rock
review_text:
I'm sorry, concept is fun and all but it should not run this poorly on a AMD Ryzen 9 5950X 16-Core Processor. It's constantly at 100% and even forced my PC to shutdown from overheating.

My settings are on the lowest possible, and it still does it.

game_name: Stone Simulator – Just Be a Rock
review_text: Even for $2, it’s a no from me. Generative AI is used for visuals which could be way better if an artist created those. The real red flag, tho is performance, the fans ramp up like it’s running something far heavier than a 5GB game. Its not fun for an idler game either because of the performance.

review_text: Repetitive and not scary. Kept glitching and waste of time.

Not very inspired or interesting game. It is a cheap jumpscare-horror and absolutely everything feels like a stock model or sound. A single developer can still make great horror games, as an example N4ba or ENIGMA STUDIO, also do not get me wrong, a dev can use stock stuff too, but here it doesn't feel like he changed anything really or made something out of it. The jumpscares are cheap and lousy, the story only gets going in the last 2 days and it is very repetitive with only 1 innovative and thought out scare. A bland and generic game, it is.

This game is not fun at all. The game is seriously boring and no gamer experience is needed. The jumpscares are very basic and generic and overdone. The is no game to play, just follow instructions. You might as well just watch someone play it on youtube.

Okay, this game was pretty bad.

I would say the only positives is it kinda scared me a couple times, and some of the other encounters were funny.

It's pretty obvious the game doesn't take itself too seriously, and that can be enjoyable in other games, just not this one.

Even though I jumped a couple times; it wasn't really scary, the gameplay was boring, the story was basic, the ghosts were uninspired, etc. Just so much wrong with a game rated "Very Positive" on here. I personally don't get it.

The story and overall atmosphere was decent but there is a "sanity" meter which forces you consume food/drink to "survive" which is really just bs.  You're forced to replay scenes from this bad mechanic.  It makes the game longer.  It's short and not thought out well.
jumpscare slop. and the whole "disorder" the girl had was just AUTISIM???? THATS it????? Shit writing, boring and steryotypical horror, give me my five bucks back.

Personally I don't think you're missing much fun by not playing this game.  The first half hour is fun as it's new, but it's only soon after you realize the repetition of scares and parts of the game that simply do not work. Let's not even get started on the AI use in the game which is so ridiculous, it takes away from any genuine "scariness" this game might have to offer. 

Solid 2.5/5 for me, but you decide.

If you want to play this game solo, then you won't have any problem doing so. The problems start in multiplayer. My friend and I consistently crash after finishing one round on any map. Once this bug is fixed, I will change my review. When it crashes, there is no way to put in a crash report and this has been going on for months.

Bought this game for me and friends because Insym played it but honestly? Don't buy. It's not worth the current price.

Game has consistent crashing issues. Zero way to turn off motionblur. The most recent update isn't even playable and the dev blames everybody but themselves for the issue. I've tried 2 days in a row to get this game to work, as it has potential and is quite fun, but with the consistent crashes + the fact that at least 1 person can't join the lobby I can't safely say this game is in a playable state where I would recommend anyone to buy it.

Want to love this game but there are some big issues that bother me:

Developer acknowledged that nightmare difficulty randomizes how much evidence you get, at least on double or nothing, but doesn't seem balanced to have 0-1 tasks a doll likes as enough sufficient evidence to make a guess on a high-stakes match.  Also just because you don't see a bug in a small circle of creators doesn't mean it doesn't exist there have been 2 occasions where I lost double or nothing and never lost my money.

Lastly, the bots are not dolls, they are separate entities and it was nice to see that they are optional but they aren't on certain contracts.  They are pretty straight forward and from my personal experience, broken on circus map and don't move so they stand in the post.  It's kinda shocking to see someone put in a report bug option in the game and be told that the bug reported hasn't been reported by anyone else, what's the point of putting in a feature if you are going to discredit someone that uses it.

Don't get this game, it's a confusing clusterfuck of what you can do, and how it works, plus it isn't getting updated except on oculus. I spent my first 30 minutes trying to figure out how to ineract with npcs, then the second 30 minutes trying out the "story" mode.

First half, I tried talking, there's no way to talk. I tried to befriend, befriending is both the most complex wave of hands, yet as simple as walking away, and coming back, and doing the same wave of hands. Then I tried the combat, guns felt so finicky, but the melee weapons were... decent.

Second half when I went into "Story" mode, There was actual dialogue... using ai generated voices... and they only act as quest givers, not any actual dialogue. Let me sum the quests up as not well made. First one I was given was by a woman at the entrance, who wanted me to find her husband, a man with black jeans, a blue shirt, and a handsome face. I thought it wouldn't be hard, found a man that fit the description brought him over, was met with "no that isn't my husband." I thought that I could try again, found someone else that fit the description, "No that isn't my husband." Again, try a third time "No that isn't my husband." (Insert Farcry 3 insanity definition here).
Second quest, guy asks me to get another guy drunk so he can take his girlfriend. Scummy, but okay, it's something to do. This one was the easiest to complete, somehow as the moment I gave the guy a beer, his girlfriend started trying to fight with me, which let me bring her over. and that was it. Third quest I got was to talk to some guy at a table about money he owed to a friend, I chat, he gets mad, we get into a fight, and the quest wouldn't let me complete it anymore... great.
Thats where I ended it, did not enjoy, do not get this game.

This game is obviously just a cheap cash grab for the many people out there that just wants to try new vr games but in every aspect this game is bad.

Takes about 1 hour to play. I'm not sure that the game actually detects what you're saying just that you have microphone input happening regardless of the sound, which is probably for the best as my pronunciation was terrible. It's a little meme-y game that doesn't take itself seriously, it's janky, would likely be better with more bugs. Lots of jumpscares, some are actually scary. Probably wait for a sale if you like weird meme games. It's honestly not worth the full asking price with such little content. Also, pizda staraya, poshla na hui!

I was excited to play this game as the concept sounded hilarious. 

However the very first book that I picked up in the game (book about lucid dreams) contained a homophobic slur that wasn't even spelt correctly.

I have put in a refund request and would also suggest to the creator to brush up on their spelling as well as their moral and ethical integrity.

Voice acting was 10/10.

"F*ck You Witch" offers an intriguing yet fleeting gaming experience. It mixes simple puzzle mechanics with a unique and captivating art style, set against a soundtrack that perfectly complements its mystical theme. [b] The game's major downfall, however, is its steep price tag, which is hard to justify given that the entire game can be completed in under two hours. [/b] While the visuals and atmosphere are highlights, they don't quite compensate for the lack of content or depth. In conclusion, "F*ck You Witch" could have been a spellbinding adventure, but its overpricing and brevity leave it feeling more like a missed opportunity

The main aspect that the game advertised doesn't work on majority of computers to the point that the developer has to put a warning that it most likely will not work and will crash the pc, note that this warning is only in-game not on the store page (so you only find out after buying the game)

The game is generally very laggy and scripts often crash making you stuck on a level when you were supposed to be loaded onto next one.

The concept itself is very interesting and I was very excited for the game, if the devs fix all the issues and make storyline make sense then it would be worth it

Storyline makes Morbius look good <3

I honestly don't even know what the hell was even going on in this thing. First of all the game is very simple to complete... and the story is really bland. The voice actor (although very difficult to understand without subtitles on) has a few lines ever now and then that'll make you crack a smile... but that's not all together worth the $9.99 asking price. I've played cheaper games that were WAY more focused... and had some kind of story to follow along with. I cannot give this game a "thumbs up" based off graphics and Matt Rife jokes.

Feels like it has some potential, but I can't recommend spending money on this at the moment unless it's like your first introduction into the hacking game genre, there are much better entries out there.

The game holds your hand and tells you exactly what to do and type, but then at the same time it doesn't explain why you're doing it. Just "type this command... and this command... and this command". While the emulated OS is pretty well done, there's not even a notepad application, which I feel like sets the entire tone for the game. It doesn't expect you to actually learn what you're doing or need to apply lateral thinking.

There's no AI disclosure on the store page, but you can tell the voice acting is clearly AI and really awful. It's a huge turn-off. Maybe the game gets better, but it lost me when I found out you needed to punch dashes when using the cell phone, and discovered that beneath the facade of an OS and simulated world, most of the stuff was non-functional and there was just a singular solution to things.

Some of the things it has you do is also kinda tasteless and feels like a dated layman fantasy of a 'hackerman'.

A watered down version of Grey Hack with a 'better' operating system feel. If you found Grey Hack difficult but still want to play a game in this genre, this is an option. However, I would still recommend NITE Team 4 in that case. This lacks depth.

The game initially piqued my interest; the interfaces, code implementation, and content appeared satisfactory. However, after spending a few hours in the game, I observed a distinct lack of freedom or space to utilize personal problem-solving methods. The experience is confined to executing assigned tasks within a rigid scenario.

The character is bound to a pre-existing linear storyline, requiring the player to simply follow the provided steps. It is possible to advance the narrative by sequentially executing commands from the objectives panel on the right, without needing to open specific panels or even read the terminal output. For these reasons, the game demonstrates that it is currently operating at the very bottom of its potential.

Has a lot of clear issues.

- Obvious poor quality AI voices. Go on Fiverr and pay real voice actors or something, there's no excuse.
- Complete lack of a Notepad tool is boggling.
- Tab completion, enter key, and QoL navigation is completely missing and makes everything pretty tedious as you have to type and click everything manually which gets exhausting fast.

I am very keen of these kind of games. Have been looking for an interesting entry since Uplink, which to date is still kinda my favorite game out there.

UI of the game feels nice, it's modern, well rounded, and it mimicks an OS pretty well. Didn't really play much with settings, I came here to be a hacker first.

I was happy to see how different applications were done:
* The browser looked fine and websites looked nice and functional (except, please let me use enter when entering passwords or something where that input makes sense :()
* The terminal is nice to see and works mostly okay. 
* The phone can introduce interesting gameplay aspects but maybe it is hidden later down the story, or is just not exploited early on

I can see potential in this game, but I personally feel it lacks more... freedom, which still can be achieved in Early Access IMO.

Don't get me wrong, yes you need to be handheld up to a point, yes nowadays people like having checkboxes that guide them throw a mission, but there is nothing extra if you want to go out of the box. Hopefully they'll change it a bit.

The mission loop felt kind of dull. Sure, I only played for 30 minutes, but the initial missions could be a bit more interesting at least story wise and give it a bit more purpose to what you are doing. Instead it's a very bland loop of go to website, click button, wait for e-mail, follow tutorial instructions, go back to e-mail, either click button or paste the [b]exact[/b] response the condition expects (like, one reply I sent contained more than just the text inside the quotes "" and the mission didn't finish... O.o

Where it lost me was on the voice acting, I would have preferred go start getting e-mails from friends or something like that instead of dull voices on the phone. A little bit of effort on that front and it can really change the way things feel. Just look at Hardspace: Shipbreaker where you do not see any other characters, but you hear emotions in their voices.

Simulation-wise, the game is okay-ish. Output looks kinda accurate and using some of the commands is similar to the actual ones on linux terminal, I understand some things need to be simplified.

I did miss some few basic features on the terminal that I am really used to due to just daily usage: 
* [b]cd[/b] did not bring me home 
* Path resolution wasn't working every time (a wordlist was downloaded in my downloads folder but I couldn't navigate to the folder where it was and just use it as [b]filename[/b] on commands
* Autocomplete proposing command arguments?
* Pretty sure guest users will mostly never have root access to stuff
* I went around to look at the filesystem of the first computer to find absolutely nothing BUT the user that the story provides.
* Some inconsistencies... like why am I breaking the SSH protocol when it's attacking the MySQL default port?

Some of the other tools like ping, whois, nmap do give you a good feeling though.

I don't know. I would steer clear for a bit until it is more polished. it feels like an AI game, it doesn't state it so I am not sure. That being said, I think we sadly have to start getting used to entries like these already.

There were a few instances of the objective marker just straight up giving me info that I have not found yet, or is just unavailable (like the target's email or username).

Feels like it has some potential, but I can't recommend spending money on this at the moment unless it's like your first introduction into the hacking game genre, there are much better entries out there.

The game holds your hand and tells you exactly what to do and type, but then at the same time it doesn't explain why you're doing it. Just "type this command... and this command... and this command". While the emulated OS is pretty well done, there's not even a notepad application, which I feel like sets the entire tone for the game. It doesn't expect you to actually learn what you're doing or need to apply lateral thinking.

There's no AI disclosure on the store page, but you can tell the voice acting is clearly AI and really awful. It's a huge turn-off. Maybe the game gets better, but it lost me when I found out you needed to punch dashes when using the cell phone, and discovered that beneath the facade of an OS and simulated world, most of the stuff was non-functional and there was just a singular solution to things.

Some of the things it has you do is also kinda tasteless and feels like a dated layman fantasy of a 'hackerman'.

A watered down version of Grey Hack with a 'better' operating system feel. If you found Grey Hack difficult but still want to play a game in this genre, this is an option. However, I would still recommend NITE Team 4 in that case. This lacks depth.

The game initially piqued my interest; the interfaces, code implementation, and content appeared satisfactory. However, after spending a few hours in the game, I observed a distinct lack of freedom or space to utilize personal problem-solving methods. The experience is confined to executing assigned tasks within a rigid scenario.

The character is bound to a pre-existing linear storyline, requiring the player to simply follow the provided steps. It is possible to advance the narrative by sequentially executing commands from the objectives panel on the right, without needing to open specific panels or even read the terminal output. For these reasons, the game demonstrates that it is currently operating at the very bottom of its potential.

- Complete lack of a Notepad tool is boggling.

I will start this review by saying this game has LARGE POTENTIAL to become a great and well made game. I love the concept, and the whole premise of it.

While saying that, I would not recommend the game to anyone at the moment especially since it costs $9.99 without sale and $6.49 with current sale. Also, I understand this is the beta version, it clearly states it on the home screen of the game.

But to cut it short, I should not be paying $6, $10 or even $1 for this game at its current state. There  are way too many bugs at the moment to be charging me even a penny.

Bugs I have experienced but not all
- During tutorial, the instructions are blocked out constantly by other things making it hard to read and follow.
- Poor guidance to where to go and click. Even free games that are in beta do it better
- MAJOR: I couldn't get past the tutorial because it stated "Use the grass repair tool to go over damaged grass and fix it. Also, clean up any trash on the field.". To which i cleaned the trash but when i went to go to my inventory to see if i had the grass repair tool, I didn't. I was confused, I checked the computer in both buildings, I checked the inventory again. I restarted my game, I  even restarted my computer. Even after all that, nothing changed. I tried each tool to see if it worked, nothing either. Like what is going on? If I am missing something and not seeing, then that is still the fault of the creators of the game. Why am i paying any money for a game that is confusing and in PRE beta phase ...

I am extremely disappointed honestly, was expecting so much but was let down extremely. I will keep an eye out but currently, will be refunding it.

Played the demo a while back and the game showed a lot of promise. The finished product is just an below average horror game.

I will admit, it does start well (even though it's just the demo), but after that I feel like the Devs reeeeeally dialed it in. Cheap lame jumpscares, bad gameplay, and a boring story.

There was SO much potential, just ruined by this lazy finished product. 

A final note, and I will continue to say this, HOW ARE SO MANY PEOPLE RATING THIS POSITIVE???
Starts out OK. Turns into a trigger hunt. All of the AI generated photos look god awful and stick out like a sore thumb.
Game glitched after the typewriter wrote me a note. It's supposed to cue the next event but it simply doesn't. Typewriter goes off and then the game stops working.

I picked this up after seeing the “Very Positive” reviews and watching another streamer play it. I enjoy psychological horror games, but this one ended up feeling more like a walking simulator than a horror experience.

Most of the gameplay revolves around finding keys and then walking around trying every door until one opens. Another main puzzle requires you to pick up dozens of items until you happen to find the few with writing on them. There’s no way to check your inventory or inspect items, which makes this process feel especially tedious.

I won’t spoil the ending, but it was pretty lackluster. The story didn’t grab me either - it felt underdeveloped and lacking real substance. There are a few cool moments where rooms change around you, and I probably got around six jump scares, but once you experience the first couple, the timing becomes very predictable.

Overall, it has some neat ideas, but the execution left me bored more often than unsettled.
meh

So I've played a good bit of the game so far and as far as simulators go, it's decent. However, due to some glaring issues, I don't think that I can recommend this particular one.

First off, the menus are confusing and some are redundant. The tabs where you purchase your items are not listed in order of which you unlock first, nor in any particular order. There's a tab that you can view what stock you have in store but not order directly from. There isn't an option to purchase all products at once and once you have all of the licenses in a particular store, it becomes difficult to keep track of what you need. The stores only allow you 1 employee per position, leading to a lot of backup. Stockers don't prioritize boxes with the lowest amount of product in them, so that leads to a lot of clutter. There are extremely limited options for customization (Specifically , there is no way to change any of the wallpaper or floors of the stores and they aren't to my taste at all).The mall still has Christmas decor up near the end of January and there isn't an option to take any of it down. For the machines that you can set out in your mall, there's no employees to do the tasks for you so you'll end up with a lot of back and forth action. Once you get the upstairs stores unlocked, you start running into a game-breaking glitch where customers will repeatedly go up the down escalator for about three steps before stopping and riding it down- leading to them being stuck in the mall until the end of the day- when you shut down the mall.

This isn't to mention that most of the products in the mall are almost certainly ai-generated. -_- I can work past that because I know that it's a lot of stuff to design, but still. 

As of the current moment, there's a lot of stuff that annoys me, but with a few updates, it would probably be worth the time spent on it.

The UI does not respond about 1/4 of the time when clicking to add items into your orders.  And the movement feels gross.  Feels like I slide everywhere just a little bit.  Feels like input lag when moving but there isn't any.  It's got a lot of different items and art but the game just feels generic.  Another game to stock items on a shelf and watch people come and go.  Along with that, there DESPERATELY needs to be the ability to hire someone to help with stocking the insane number of products that are available for vending machines.  No one wants to endlessly restock those.  Needs something added to give it some pizazz or worthwhile.  Otherwise it's just another simulator in a sea of very similar games with no identity.

I can’t recommend the game in its current state.

Movement feels extremely slippery, almost like skiing on ice, which makes basic navigation frustrating. The UI clearly needs a major rework, especially the save system. When pressing “Save,” there is no feedback at all, no confirmation or indication that the game has actually been saved. This made me worry that the function might not even be working. To test it, I returned to the main menu and pressed “Continue,” where I was met with a default Windows loading cursor. At first, I genuinely thought the game had crashed.

Customization options are also very limited at the moment. Several stores appear to have no assigned purpose, while some on the first floor and all on the top floor seem to be permanently locked to specific uses, which is disappointing and restricts creativity. On top of that, placing items and retrieving them from storage racks feels unnecessarily complicated and unintuitive.

Overall, I unfortunately can’t recommend the game at this stage. I’d rather wait for the game to bake in the oven and see how it develops before considering sticking with it.

This is a pretty fun game for the price. It's not quite as a deep as you'd think from all the options you have. A lot of the simulation options don't really seem to matter a lot. Paying thousands of dollars to open up new licenses for instance doesn't mean you really make more money, or that people will even buy what your selling. It just means you have more items to keep track of and restock.

I will say the AI shop assistance are great. The Cashier and Stock person generally keep up with the demand, and you can always let the day run past 22:00 to clear things out if they are behind. Problem is you can't buy a re-stocker for the mall machines like Drinks, ATM, Candy, Crane or snacks. Not even the Toilet paper in the bathroom, which seems to be just there to give you mall points. Every customer uses an entire roll of toilet paper, and you have to restock the stalls by hand.

By your third or 4th store however you will soon find yourself doing almost nothing but buying more and more stock for your stores. Sure you can bulk buy stuff to try and keep a day or two supply going if you have the spare cash. But in general there isn't an easy way to deal with stock. You can easily sell 20+ of an item a day, especially shoes in the Sports store. Meaning not only do you need your display shelves but several boxes in reserve because they sell them by 5 each with no easy option to get larger amounts.

There is a mod that works decently at adding items to your shopping cart as they run out of stock in a store but in game there isn't much of anything to deal with this. One the reasons why buying more licenses, and thus more different items becomes even harder to deal with. Especially for things like the Drink or Snack Machine.

Over all it was a very enjoyable game, I spent an easy 10+ hours on this in the first few days. But you quickly go from actively doing stuff, to just waiting for days to end, buying a ton of stock and repeat.

Edit: I would also like to say, the more you play the game the more you realize how fake a lot of the simulation is. Most of your choices barely matter. There has also been a long period of time with almost zero updates from the Developer, and they are also pushing a new project the Laundry washing simulator at the same time. I am not sure this game is actually going to be "completed".

Edit 2: So it's 2/4/2026 and the Dev just sort of came back did a handful of very minor bug fixes. But mostly just plans to put out a roadmap to promise the work in the future... So very slow progress, claims to be a single person working from what I can understand. Worse is the  community of this game though. Tons of people hard defend this "Indie dev" after taking a 2 month vacation with no real communication with the players right after it's release.

If you question the development progress of this game your attacked, threads are shut down and the entire thing is silenced. About the only work the Dev seems to do is make sure no one talks about this game being a scam.
Games not very interesting. Actually very boring. Same thing, buy store, buy product, repeat.
Still no GFN support 👎🏻

Once you get into the gameplay, you'll notice that all shops are practically the same thing. You can only hire 1 stocker and 1 cashier for each store. Any items that are not inside a store such as toiletries, sodas, candy, and such are required to be manually stocked because you can't hire mall workers. Pretty much all products are just AI cloned copies of brands from IRL. Also the pictures are pretty misleading, and were also probably generated with AI. The burger shop picture for example shows you handing a customer a tray of food. That is not what happens at all. Instead, you're pretty much just stocking the burgers on a shelf and the customers help themselves then check out. They will sit down to eat afterwards, and that does help your mall points though. This game does have potential if the developer commits to it, but I see they're already working on releasing another AI slop laundry sim game so I don't have high hopes.

This is a pretty fun game for the price. It's not quite as a deep as you'd think from all the options you have. A lot of the simulation options don't really seem to matter a lot. Paying thousands of dollars to open up new licenses for instance doesn't mean you really make more money, or that people will even buy what your selling. It just means you have more items to keep track of and restock.

I will say the AI shop assistance are great. The Cashier and Stock person generally keep up with the demand, and you can always let the day run past 22:00 to clear things out if they are behind. Problem is you can't buy a re-stocker for the mall machines like Drinks, ATM, Candy, Crane or snacks. Not even the Toilet paper in the bathroom, which seems to be just there to give you mall points. Every customer uses an entire roll of toilet paper, and you have to restock the stalls by hand.

By your third or 4th store however you will soon find yourself doing almost nothing but buying more and more stock for your stores. Sure you can bulk buy stuff to try and keep a day or two supply going if you have the spare cash. But in general there isn't an easy way to deal with stock. You can easily sell 20+ of an item a day, especially shoes in the Sports store. Meaning not only do you need your display shelves but several boxes in reserve because they sell them by 5 each with no easy option to get larger amounts.

There is a mod that works decently at adding items to your shopping cart as they run out of stock in a store but in game there isn't much of anything to deal with this. One the reasons why buying more licenses, and thus more different items becomes even harder to deal with. Especially for things like the Drink or Snack Machine.

Over all it was a very enjoyable game, I spent an easy 10+ hours on this in the first few days. But you quickly go from actively doing stuff, to just waiting for days to end, buying a ton of stock and repeat.

Edit: I would also like to say, the more you play the game the more you realize how fake a lot of the simulation is. Most of your choices barely matter. There has also been a long period of time with almost zero updates from the Developer, and they are also pushing a new project the Laundry washing simulator at the same time. I am not sure this game is actually going to be "completed".

Edit 2: So it's 2/4/2026 and the Dev just sort of came back did a handful of very minor bug fixes. But mostly just plans to put out a roadmap to promise the work in the future... So very slow progress, claims to be a single person working from what I can understand. Worse is the  community of this game though. Tons of people hard defend this "Indie dev" after taking a 2 month vacation with no real communication with the players right after it's release.

If you question the development progress of this game your attacked, threads are shut down and the entire thing is silenced. About the only work the Dev seems to do is make sure no one talks about this game being a scam.

If anyone is on the fence of this one you have to understand a couple things. Heavily made with AI. Down to the menu theme song. Its pretty atrocious. Also kind of funny. You end up with rip offs of everything you know. It's not M&M its MinM. Its not crash bandicoot its Cat Bandicoot. Your mall patrons vary between like 6 models. most times you will have multiple of the same person in the same store. 

On to the gameplay. This is essentially an inventory management game. As you level up you get to open new stores in the mall. Each can be named whatever you want. Each store has different tiers of products hidden behind "licensing" in game. Along with stores you can buy ad space, vending machines, claw game, atms, and a few other things. As soon as you buy a new store hire a shop keep and then the majority of your gameplay is ordering inventory and stocking shelves. As of right now that's kinda it. The game isn't terrible but as of now I cannot recommend it. Most will hate it because of its blatant use of AI. 

I enjoy the game to be honest, but there's only so much you can do in the current state. Every in game day feels the same and the only thing that changes is the products you put on the shelves. Beyond that it feels pretty lifeless. I enjoy streaming simulator games. This one as of right now is just far too shallow.

It eventually just becomes a game about waiting around, the list of actions you can actually take is extremely limited:
- Cashier
- Restocking
- Laying out furniture

That's it, yes, you can buy a new store ever so often, but the unlocking prices grow so absurdly fast that, you will have to sit around for 20min waiting for the money to pile up, ordering things for restockers to pile, until you have enough money for that one upgrade, or that one new shop, then you have fun for 5 minutes, then you have to wait again.

I get that simulators are more and more like this nowadays, but others do a much better job of keeping you entertained after you automate some actions.

I love the concept, I enjoy it for quite some time.

The only thing that is making this one cringe is the lack of auto-order things on the end of the day. Once you have at least 6 shops to manage, it turns into a run-a-check every few minutes to make sure that everything is properly stocked.

Apart from missing this auto-order function I also miss the possibility to sell anything you don't need anymore. My storage is overfilled with things that had been changed for upgraded version and I can't get rid of it in any way. Tried to look everywhere but couldn't find anything in regards of "Sell" or "Discard".

For me it would be much better to kinda "rent" the places instead of manage everything by yourself. Or combine those 2 options and give the player a desired freedom in how things could be potentially run.

In general yes, it is fun and I recommend, but due to the lack of the most important feature at this stage I do not recommend.

In de basis is het een leuk spel en heeft het zeker de potentie om een goed spel te worden. Maar het spel is inmiddels 3 weken uit in early acces mode en kent ook veel problemen. Ik speel dit spel op een vrij zware game pc, maar zodra je meerdere winkels hebt en over de 200 bezoekers per dag gaat heeft het spel last van heel veel lag. Het spel hapert dusdanig dat de lol er snel vanaf is. Verder missen er veel basis functies voor een spel als dit. Zo kan je gekochte inventaris en accessiores niet weg gooien of verkopen waardoor je een magazijn vol ongebruikte planten, schappen, kasten, etc hebt. Het bevoorraden van de winkels duurt heel lang (Ik heb 8 winkels waarvan 4 volledig uitgebreid, een bioscoop en een gamehal, en het duurt pakweg anderhalf uur voordat alles opnieuw bevoorraad is en je een nieuwe dag kan starten) doordat je iedere winkel los opnieuw moet bevoorraden en er totaal geen structuur zit in de opbouw van de magazijnen waardoor je niet eenvoudig je huidige voorraden kan controleren. Ook zitten er veel bugs in waardoor bijvoorbeeld medewerkers of klanten vast lopen of magazijnmedewerkers random ergens heen lopen en hun werk dus niet doen. Dit een kleine greep uit de problemen. Het lijkt erop dat de ontwikkelaar totaal niet omkijkt naar dit spel, hij communiceert niet hier op steam en er zijn tot op heden geen bug fixes oid uitgebracht. Voor nu kan ik dit spel zeker niet aanraden, maar hopelijk gaat er toch snel wat gebeuren in de toekomst. Ik ben voor nu gestopt met het spelen van dit spel.

Currently no I wouldn't recommend this game. I think it has the potential to be really fun with a little more cooking time in the oven, especially reading the roadmap plans, but right now I found it a bit too boring;

1. There is no music. At all. I thought maybe you had to buy a sound-system in game (a nice little diegetic way to add sound?) - nope...nothing. Just boot spotify up I guess

2. When you buy a shop, it comes 'pre-made'. Why? What's the point? They aren't exactly expensive either really. Just let us set it up ourselves.
- This includes the 'front window' displays with things like "50% off!" showing products....I've not even unlocked?! xD )
- Also there are no sales so...why have that in the windows?

3. The game is SOOOOOOOOOOOO easy. The starting balance I think is actually ok. Its not massive amounts, you can buy a few products. But its not enough to stock everything. I like that.
- HOWEVER. $80 for a T-shirt that cost $30 to make? Someone clearly likes Macklemore.
- The profit margins (and what customers will pay) are INSANE! 
- Also the prices never change. May or may not be a good thing (some gmaes in this genre have varying prices...and yeah it can get REAL tedious adjusting the prices)

4. The customers give you a random amount of cash. T-Shirt cost $80? They will literally give you $82 and you have to give them $2 change...um....why did they give me the $2?
- Customers should be smarter about this sort ofthing - usually people wil lround to the nearest dollar or denomination ($5, $10 etc)

5. Customers are limited to buying 3 items max from a store (unlike other titles in this genre, the counters seem to have 'slots' and only 3 per checkout slot so customers can only buy 3 things)
- I never got passed 2 stores, but it seems they carry 1 store-bag per hand...so it looks to me like customers can only visit 2 stores each (max total of 6 items)

In this state I can't quite recommend the game yet. I'm still experiencing too much lag which causes me to just stand around and wait until all the customers have left. The gameplay is fun so far. But it just doesn't seem to run great

Game struggles to find an identity, the console repair sim part of this game is great but it really positions itself as a gambling simulator with a side of console repair shop management, life streaming simulator and just really struggles to do anything that well. My advice to the devs would be remove most of the gambling aspect (crypto buying, case opening, ect) and focus on what the game is suppose to be. 

With that aside, the game is still very buggy and ultimately refunded the game because of the lack of identity and bugginess.

Fun game. 

There are a few bugs that can not be ignored. After starting the game and playing the shelves will bug out and not allow anything to be placed. I left off off on day 2 after loading up game and half of my shelves are un useable. Every time this happens you have to quit and re load.

Another issue I am having is when ordering things they will not be delivered despite the game taking the money.

Will change thumb when shelf issue is fixed.

I REALLY, REALLY, REALLY WANT to recommend this. This game is the closest thing there will ever be to what I actually do for a living IRL.

Saying that, of course I enjoy the prospect of repairing game consoles and running a store (Both Brick and mortar and online.) And regardless of all the issues, I had a bit of fun playing Console Shop Simulator. I also really like this dev. I watched and owned this for a good bit before actually playing and this dev has added quite a few good QOL updates, content updates etc. Dare I say this is probably top three most active dev I've seen on these types of EA Simulator games, and that is also why I would still almost soft recommend this because if the dev keeps it up this will end up being a pretty decent game. But I'm pretty sure I'm a bit biased because of what I do, and even with that I was just increasingly frustrated with this as time went on.

The biggest problem is simply the lack of polish combined with major balancing issues that exist due to a lack of overall polish that just makes this game not really worth it. Even for me.

The progress is oddly slow, like but in reality it's actually not all that slow either. It's hard to explain, you do regularly get new games to put on the shelves and opportunities for new contracts with new game companies. There is an actual working calendar system that contains release dates for new games (Nice touch btw) Trade in's give you extra stock for shelves and that keeps you busy..ish.

However it's all done at such a trickle slow pace, Which is also partially because there is just hardly anything to sell in the first place. It says there are 300 games in this you can collect but that would mean like 275 of them are all old games you can only get from trade-ins and In my 7 hours I saw all of 2 old games get traded in. You start with like 1 game on your shelves and then every few in game days a new game releases and simultaneously you will buy new contracts to offer more games from more devs. So as an example I played for what...7 hours now and I was now offering about 13-15 games? That's it. So even though you do get new stuff regularly to unlock, it takes a while to get it and it's just one thing at a time. So you don't really FEEL like nothing is happening but in reality, nothing has really been happening.

Employees are all jacked up they feel HYPER expensive and it's extremely easy to not have enough money to cover daily pay, which causes them to quit. Because your money is ALWAYS tight. And just as soon as it doesn't feel that way you upgrade a thing or two and you're right back to being broke again. The issue is that you then have to rehire an employee and the initial hire cost continues to go up regardless of how many employees you actually have. So the first employee cost me a little over 100 dollars to sign them on (+ their daily pay) and now I've had about 5..6..7...? employees quit because I didn't have enough money at the exact right second they have to be paid. So now to rehire and have ONE employee costs me almost 800 dollars, so now I can't even afford to replace the employees if they quit.

Now I'm sure that's a bug/oversight and the scale of cost, I'm sure, should only actually scale with how many employees you actually have, not the total tally you've ever hired combined. But this is a great example of the 75 things like this you are going to run into that just don't feel like they had much attention paid to them during their implementation.

This is compounded because it's not like I was over-expanding or anything almost every time an employee quit was because I just had to restock multiple items and it turned out 30 seconds later the employees were to be paid. So it's just insanely easy for that to accidentally happen. ESPECIALLY on a game release day where you're constantly restocking to keep up with the higher demand for the new game. (Also another nice touch BTW)

Almost any non-release day is a nightmare because you will not get enough customers and enough sales in the store to even cover a single employees pay for the day. So again if you look at the bigger picture this is the issue with the game, tons of small unbalanced issues that begin stacking on top of each other. It's almost not even worth it to have the store open and just do online sales only. Online sales are the only way you can actually make a bit of money and slowly expand. So, in Console SHOP simulator it is actually a better business decision to not actually run the shop lol, oops?

It was so dumb and silly that I can run the store all day with two employees stocking and running the register but it was me packaging and shipping the online sales that paid for any of it. And who knows, if this asset pack is the same as the TCG card shop sim game then maybe I was supposed to buy physical shop expansions to increase customers, but I literally unlocked like half the games and items and they were only enough to fill 2 shelves so there was no reason or me to expand much beyond having a storage/warehouse area. Not to mention that the balancing issues made me not want to expand the shop much because I was probably gonna lose employees and not have enough to restock if I did. And I'm just guessing anyways because it's not like there is anything in the game that tells you how to bring in more customers.

Honestly just money in general feels completely unbalanced. Instead of an actual economy existing its like, there is money in the game, and then a ton of systems in the game that run off that money, but none of it is balanced together in an economy it's like everything is just individually attached to the money. 

Console trade-ins are a waste of time because most of them cost as much money as you make in two days. So you never really buy many of those, and even when you do they rarely sell because they are so expensive and most customers don't even carry enough cash to buy something that expensive in the first place! Why do games costs a normal amount of 40-60 dollars but then vintage consoles are like 1000+? It's like it isn't priced to match the in game economy it was just a feature added to the game with a random price tag attached to it. In fact you have to spend several thousand dollars on what are essentially skill points that will increase the RNG roll chance for rich customers to visit the shop.

Why have skill points that you pay for?? You literally level up in the game why do you not unlock skill points for the skill point esque things? Instead it's one more thing attached to the in game money system that makes it feel like you never actually have enough money to do anything. It felt like I had the same amount of money to work with at seven hours as I did after 1, the money doesn't scale with any of the cost, so you don't start making more money as you sell more items, you start making enough money to add the next game to the shelf. It's just...odd. 

So at this point I just don't really feel like booting the game back up again and having to cover every task again until I can squeeze enough cash out of the game to hire another replacement employee again just so I can get backed into a corner again and not be able to pay that employee again and then have to do all that all over..again and I just don't feel like I can recommend the game to anyone when I didn't even want to play it more than 7 hours.

EDIT: I also wanted to add that the steam workshop for this game has me extremely concerned as well. Its literally thousands of copyright problematic items INCLUDING real Nintendo games and that is a recipe to get shut down. We all know what Nintendo will do if they See a "Super Mario sunshine for Gamecube" mod or if Sony sees the "Barbie Sony PlayStation 5". The devs better do something about that QUICK if they don't want this game to get shut down and end up in court.

So you never really buy many of those, and even when you do they rarely sell because they are so expensive and most customers don't even carry enough cash to buy something that expensive in the first place! Why do games costs a normal amount of 40-60 dollars but then vintage consoles are like 1000+? It's like it isn't priced to match the in game economy it was just a feature added to the game with a random price tag attached to it. In fact you have to spend several thousand dollars on what are essentially skill points that will increase the RNG roll chance for rich customers to visit the shop.

Why have skill points that you pay for?? You literally level up in the game why do you not unlock skill points for the skill point esque things? Instead it's one more thing attached to the in game money system that makes it feel like you never actually have enough money to do anything. It felt like I had the same amount of money to work with at seven hours as I did after 1, the money doesn't scale with any of the cost, so you don't start making more money as you sell more items, you start making enough money to add the next game to the shelf. It's just...odd. 

So at this point I just don't really feel like booting the game back up again and having to cover every task again until I can squeeze enough cash out of the game to hire another replacement employee again just so I can get backed into a corner again and not be able to pay that employee again and then have to do all that all over..again and I just don't feel like I can recommend the game to anyone when I didn't even want to play it more than 7 hours.

EDIT: I also wanted to add that the steam workshop for this game has me extremely concerned as well. Its literally thousands of copyright problematic items INCLUDING real Nintendo games and that is a recipe to get shut down. We all know what Nintendo will do if they See a "Super Mario sunshine for Gamecube" mod or if Sony sees the "Barbie Sony PlayStation 5". The devs better do something about that QUICK if they don't want this game to get shut down and end up in court.

"Recycling Center Simulator: Prologue" is  is not a game.  It's an advertisement for a product called "Recycling Center Simulator".  Recycling Center Simulator is yet another samey low effort "job simulator" that seems like it's just a lazy Unity kitbash (Playway has now published literally hundreds of these "games" onto Steam) without any long term interest (or polish, or professional delivery). 

But this isn't completely relevant because this is not Recycling Center Simulator, it's just an advertisment for it, so it's not correct to try to review that completely distinct product.  We must instead review this for what it is... an advertisement.  If you enjoy advertisements, you can consume them for free on the web or on Youtube without needing to add them to your game library, download them, and then launch the advertising.  It's impossible in a general sense to recommend people should do that over playing real, full games on Steam, and that applies here with "Recycling Center Simulator: Prologue" too.

The purpose of this advertisement is to get you to shell out a whopping $32 USD on buying Recycling Center Simulator, and I don't recommend you do that either.

Insultingly, the developer used a bunch of GPT AI slop for the Steam store page and marketing material.  AI slop is an insult to gamers everywhere, it's trained on copyrighted material without permission.  Worse, if they had no qualms about using it here, there's no telling how much of the rest of this product is also vibe coded or made with AI slop.

Now on to pricing and value for gamers.

As an advertisement, this is only free in the same way that adverts on Youtube and banner ads in your browser are.  This is a game demo that's been dumped on Steam to advertise another Steam store page in the hopes of raking in some cash.  Just because advertisements are free, doesn't mean they're worth your time.

It's important to understand there's a formal process to create a demo/page for a game which hasn't been followed here:

https://partner.steamgames.com/doc/store/application/demos

While it might seem like an inocuous mistake, publishing demos and advertisements as if they are full games is misleading and in some ways harmful.  This advertisement is taking up marketing space and visibility that would be better made available to a developer that's done the right thing and created a full game.  Instead, that visibility is given over to a demo/advert that's been published incorrectly and is polluting the marketplace.

It's unclear if this mistake was made through incompetence or a deliberate intent to mislead gamers.  Often developers may choose to incorrectly publish demo/advertisements as separate games as a cheap way to bypass paying Valve for marketing presence on the Steam front page, which may seem clever to them, but it's harmful behaviour and it's not good for gamers.

Advertisements and incorrectly published demos don't add +1 to your game collector count, they aren't full games.  It's impossible for me to recommend this incorrectly published advertising spam to gamers, you deserve better than adverts.

The use of AI slop is a direct insult to gamers and real, ethical content creators and artists everywhere.

Until they figure out how to make a splitter that actually makes sense to use to split items it will be a thumbs down. The dumbness in splitting something to send items to failed then to send those items to split again just to split those to get to a end result is wild.

I'm sorry I've voted this game down. I really do enjoy this game, heck, i've got just over 10 hours into it BUT......

Christ it's got boring really quickly. Every process is the same. You cut, you wash, you dry. The only difference is does it go into a bowl, bottle, jar, pouch...Everything else is the same. 

What Would make this game even better is if a process needed more than one veg, say a veg salad that needed 3 or so vegs and a sauce and you had to merge lines together etc etc. 

You have a merger and splitter etc already in the game but they're useless because you don't have to merge or split anything. 

I....I enjoy this game, I really do and was super excited to play it and I still kinda am but I feel like I've already completed even though I've not unlocked everything because it's just the same process over and over, you slice, you wash, you dry, you package, you label, you box and that's it for every single process. 

Only only 2 jobs at a time is sooooo slow! some jobs take two/three days to complete but I have everything needed by 11am on the first day....Sure I can force end day but I hate doing that. 

Also I think this is my first ever review, this is how much I care! I wanted so much to enjoy this and I do but yeah. Thank you for reading. Happy Food Processing! 

P.S I would recommend this game BUT maybe only when more stuff is added to it, as it is now, you'll probably get bored pretty quickly.

The full game is basically a the demo with more pngs to send down the conveyor belt.

There's a concept here that is okay, but every recipe is linear, identical, and only one machine is ever changed out. As a fan of more complex automation games, in the current state this feels more like a demo or a self-education project than a factory sim.

Until they figure out how to make a splitter that actually makes sense to use to split items it will be a thumbs down. The dumbness in splitting something to send items to failed then to send those items to split again just to split those to get to a end result is wild.

Know what pisses me off? This modern belief that you can slap together a pile of third-party assets, bolt on a "flight model" that feels straight out of Unreal Engine defaults, throw together a couple of sloppy levels... and then call it a UAV simulator.

And the nerve to tack on "Ukrainian". Its not clear whether the author expects that label to guarantee success, or thinks the audience will go easier on it just because its Ukrainian. Either way, its careless - and it reflects badly on the rest of our nation.

Right now, it plays like a hobby project - the sort of thing a bunch of kids would throw together instead of doing their homework.

Im assuming this is largely due to being early access but  too many times trying to fly on realistic i hit what feels like a potential wall that wind and top heavy drones doesnt account for.  Ive flown overloaded drones and while top heavy drones will flip like this, the tendancy to  be so at the mercy to the wind is a little absurd.    Ive watched several times as the blowing dust indicate a wind direction but my drone cannot accelerate not inline with the dust but at a funny angle to the dust which makes no sense.    Other times i get a height altitude and it feels like im caught up on something up high, but if i lower altitude i regain power.    The physics of this are definitely a work in progress.

It took a long while for me to finally get in this game. First off, thanks to the Dev for hitting me up with a Key, I appreciate it.

With that being said, I didn't like this game at all. To be honest, I liked the demo better than the full game.

I thought the gameplay was boring and too repetitive. Then again, there isn't much you can do with a
"Jumpscare Sim". And having the same two or three jumpscares back to back didn't help at all either. I wish there was a bit more variety with the kind of faces that popped up instead of it just being the nun and that naked "man thing".

To me, the story didn't make sense at all. Like I said in my video, it's like you put a bunch of words in a hat, then took 'em out and put them in a random sentence. I understood what you were going for, but the execution was way off. And to be honest, I think you dragged out the experience way longer than it needed to be. With all the dialogue and random doors to go through, it was all over the place for me.

And like I said in the demo, you should've done something else instead of having the flashing green lights between rooms. It didn't bother me, but that kinda stuff is annoying to look at and could irritate somebody else.

There's more to be said about the game, but I'll let my video do the rest. To me, it's not the worth the time nor the asking price.

I have so many complaints it's hard to know where to begin but I'd like to start off saying...I actually do like it. Me and my friends had a lot of laughs. We had a great time. Which is why it's so frustrating that this game is so close to greatness and yet isn't.

Let's address the biggest problem. This game is 17 gigabytes and it takes 5 minutes or more to load a single room. I play GTA V, Red Dead, and Spider-Man on this computer with little to no loading issues. There is zero excuse for the game being this fucking slow. IT'S ONE ROOM, dude. Drying paint watches this game load. Nintendo Online loads faster than this. I finished watching the entire credits to Sonic Colors before this goddamn game loaded. 2001: A Space Odyssey's plot moves faster than this game's loading does. This game is so poorly optimized that I'd be genuinely fucking surprised if the developer playtested this game in the slightest. And speaking of which...once you've FINALLY loaded in after a million years, the game is over within two minutes.

Liar's Deck, no issues honestly. It was fun. Liar's Slots I have a very big problem with. The death spin should be on your slot machine not ONE slot and you HAVE to get a heart. That makes no fucking sense. Not a single time in all the times we played did anyone get a heart so the game is over in like two minutes if not less. Liar's Dice is probably the best one here, if you play on Traditional Mode it has a lot of really good mechanics that keep things interesting. And Liar's Poker is...fine? But it feels lacking. as much as i enjoy the gameplay of this game, there are some glaringly obvious issues that need to be addressed. most of which are game breaking, some of which are ethical.

1. everyone has already brought up the ai voice acting, i'm not gonna bring that up. there's already hundreds of comments on it.
2. the game has an issue of picking up your mic even WHILE it is displayed as muted. this happens most commonly in the loading in screen, but has happened to me in-game as well. not only is this a severe privacy issue, it also effects gameplay if you are a streamer and want to talk to your chat about your hand/game.
3. lastly, the settings are SO incredibly poorly optimized. as far as fixing the voice chat bug i mentioned before, i had to literally turn off my entire voice chat, which can only be done WHILE you are in a game. meaning, your microphone will be picked up in the pre-lobby before you can even turn it off. there are also issues with not being able to rebind keys, and various other issues with the very underdeveloped settings menu.

as far as those glaring issues, there are also just some things that could be added to the game for the sake of fun. why is the max amount of players four people? the game would make SO MUCH MORE MONEY if they just increased the player count, at the very least for privately hosted games. if they want to maintain matchmaking as solely 4 people for rank based purposes, that's totally fine, but a lot of people buy this game with the intention of playing with their pre-existing friendgroups.

It's not that I don't like the game, it's very fun.  My main issue is I can't play it for more than a few minutes without getting nauseous because of the unnecessary and unstoppable head bobbing your character does.  I just gave the game another shot (no pun intended) and two games in and I have to stop.  I saw one steam review comment from the developer saying that the change is impossible because the camera is based on the character's physics.  

I find this to be a poor excuse.  A simple answer would be to create a character that doesn't move like that exclusively for people who experience motion sickness like that.  An additional option would be to make a separate camera that is third person, or first person but stationary, and is a toggleable option in the menu.

This is the only game that I own that does this.  It is a completely separate issue from the game itself, which is why I don't understand why the devs are forcing it.  The games themselves are a great time and I do enjoy playing them, and I do want to play more, but I cannot while this issue persists.  I type this while dizzy from just playing the game.

I respectfully implore the devs to think of their playerbase as I uninstall the game.

2. the game has an issue of picking up your mic even WHILE it is displayed as muted. this happens most commonly in the loading in screen, but has happened to me in-game as well. not only is this a severe privacy issue, it also effects gameplay if you are a streamer and want to talk to your chat about your hand/game.
3. lastly, the settings are SO incredibly poorly optimized. as far as fixing the voice chat bug i mentioned before, i had to literally turn off my entire voice chat, which can only be done WHILE you are in a game. meaning, your microphone will be picked up in the pre-lobby before you can even turn it off. there are also issues with not being able to rebind keys, and various other issues with the very underdeveloped settings menu.

as far as those glaring issues, there are also just some things that could be added to the game for the sake of fun. why is the max amount of players four people? the game would make SO MUCH MORE MONEY if they just increased the player count, at the very least for privately hosted games. if they want to maintain matchmaking as solely 4 people for rank based purposes, that's totally fine, but a lot of people buy this game with the intention of playing with their pre-existing friendgroups.

it's a great friendslop game, but i think they put this out WAY too soon. they needed more game testers, and funds for actual voice actors.

Got through essentially the entire first area;

One of many games in this new era of sloppy 'simulators'.  I will not lie, it is the most polished one (alongside supermarket simulator) on the market today, yet still falls victim to many of the same annoyances and issues that prevent me from fully falling in love and ruin it's playability for me personally.

The car physics is not perfect, but is OK for the purpose of the game. The game world actually has quite good detail for the scope, however it is quite 'dead' and uninteresting (with seemingly only one, randomly generating, wreck to collect at a time). It has a bar with no purpose or ability to socialize with NPCs. Everything interesting happens at the dealership. This is where the majority of the issues lie; the game loop (and complete lack of anything to break out of this boring monotony). There is a vandal (that is only annoying), but this made zero sense (as you're unable to close the security gate to your own dealership at night) so I turned it off.

You will go meet people to haggle over used car prices at their houses, which I think is an interesting concept, but this is quite literally nothing more than a fetch quest with some generic 'haggling' with ZERO randomization (each NPC has the same haggling 'skill'). All of the 'haggling' skill and activity is solely determined by your offer compared to the game's 'value' of the car, and gets incredibly boring and annoying by Week 2.

The bills in the game seemingly aren't tied to anything. The game makes you think they are, but endless testing (days of selling cars/having employees vs. no activity) seems to indicate the game just generates random values within a range based on your dealership upgrades/workers/day for all bills (not explicitly tied to employees). When combined with the generic, uninspiring task-based storyline in-game (seems directly lifted from other simulators) forcing you to progress in these upgrades, you will face a growing financial burden with almost zero benefit. 

You still have to give workers individual orders (you can't just drive a car into the body shop and they automatically start, which would actually be worthwhile), and micromanage every aspect of the (growing and increasingly unmanageable) dealership, while reaping less profit over time. If I deal with an increasing amount of stress in a management game, I expect a higher level of return, which simply isn't the case in this game (although an attempt is made through the weird car pricing formulas and model mixes/unlocks as you level up, but this is a nonfunctional solution in my experience).

For the sake of the length of this review; I'm not going into detail about the lackluster mechanics and repair loop of this game either. If you are BIG into cars and realism, steer clear of this game as it very much aims for simplicity over realism. I'll just say, after replacing ~50 batteries I stopped bothering. If you are more into the mechanical elements; Car Mechanic Simulator is leaps and bounds closer (this game assumes every single car uses the same type of engine, wheels, battery, fuses, etc. which PISSES me off).

I believe the devs HAVE offered the option for a 'sandbox' gameplay where you can turn aspects of the normal gameplay off and simply repair cars, but this mode only possesses the boring repetive repair and hagging loops and offers even less in terms of long-term entertainment. This is not a functional solution to the story criticisms IMHO, and I would prefer adjustments (or even different story 'modes' if the dev wants to keep the current implementation).

All of this creates an incredibly unsatisfying and annoying core gameplay. I don't know if it's due to game engine limitations (various assets thrown together in unity) or rushing this into a release before actual testing of the game loop itself. Considering all the other borderline AI slop simulators that the dev's are tied to, and the DLC coming out; I have zero faith the core issues of this game will be reformed. 

If you buy it though, make sure to enjoy the multiple ads for other games the dev has worked on within this. Probably the most interesting and redeeming part of this game /s

Im absolutely gutted to write a bad review for this game, because it has the world of potential under its feet. I thought it was an early access title, then realised it wasn't and I was playing a final product (Albeit with updates incoming but not the point).

First, why its SO GOOD:

- Addictive.
- Finding a bargain and making money gib dopamine
- Felt like genuine progression for the first few levels with huge progression on the horizon....

....But its at a few levels in you realise, that what you have been doing, is pretty much it for the entire game. Buy a car, do it up (Or have one of your mechanics do it), sell it for profit. Rinse, repeat. There isn't a game beyond a certain point.

Also - Fuel Station at home? a 100+ car park? Who the heck needed those? You sell cars the same day you put them on sale, and you are individually responsible for moving them around. Imagine if you had 100 cars to run around to each and every station!?

You can buy a Tow Truck and its upgrade for $60k which makes picking up cars only 10% easier as you can carry two, but you can't pickup two large ones, so negates the point completely. Furthermore, you can tow large vehicles with the base standard trailer hitch you get.

I found car sales pricing to be extremely patchy. I would buy one "Porsche" and make it perfect, sell for £60k, the next for £80k, even though the mileage and year were extremely similar. Sometimes I would get random beaters that fetched in immense cash, and others that weren't worth the hassle, but figuring it out was actually very difficult.

Automation would be nice here - Create a production line eventually where you can oversee it, what comes in (Quality vs Quantity), oversee pricing vs competition and the market, etc.

I really wish the devs the best because this is a cracking effort, but getting to this point in just a short few days has left me gutted.

Car Dealer Simulator looks gorgeous and starts strong: the one‑man, used‑car hustle feels right and the early restoration loop makes sense. But the game quickly funnels you down a single path and the late game turns hollow. Selling is trivial—lots clear in seconds even above market—and there’s no real demand, negotiation, or sales floor energy. Staff are static and micro‑managed, with teleporting behaviors and no actual salespeople, while customers pop in and out without a buying journey. The experience tilts oddly toward tow‑truck logistics, culminating in the seven‑vehicle hauler as the marquee upgrade instead of land, showrooms, or operational depth—so you never truly feel like you’re managing a dealership.

What’s missing are the pillars of the business: building placement and lot planning; trade‑ins with appraisal/reconditioning; financing and F&I; new‑vehicle ordering delivered to the lot (lower margins, higher volume for late‑game pacing); accessories and service/parts; and role‑based staff with skills in sales, finance, and tech work. Layer in market conditions and customer intent (shoppers seeking specific vehicles), price elasticity, and inventory that doesn’t evaporate instantly, and the sim could blossom. As it stands, it’s a beautiful shell that misses the mark on dealership management.

I will recommend this game based on the gameplay, but I cannot recommend this game for its engine/camera work.

The progression arc is interesting and fun.  Plays like a lot of other "simulator" games and the economy flows/works well.  

However, the use of lumen and Unreal 5 seems to be relatively poor in optimization.  Without DLSS, it suffers from the same "flickering" problem prevalent in other games using UE5's rendering systems.  But moreso than that is the jarring camera angle/work.

It's most noticeable when doing body work when the camera suddenly stops when you click to sand/putty/etc anything.  The sudden stop, for some reason, causes a bit of motion sickness.  I'm not sure what it is, but for me I just couldn't manage it without feeling some amount of nausea.

If the tools simply followed the center cursor as an anchor and allowed us to turn the camera like with all of the other non-body-work tools, it would be drastically improved.

Also there seems to be a significant amount of stutter/jitter that happens as things render in at distance, even with a higher end PC like mine.  if it were able to be better optimized, this game would get high marks from me, but currently I can't seem to weather playing it for more than an hour at a time.

Even still, someone without my boomer issues would likely enjoy this for what it offers.
