I want to like this game, but... it's EARLY early access. I'll concede that I didn't get very far, but given how much of a slog it is in its opening, I'm not inclined to come back unless the early game is reworked to be less tedious.

There's no depth to the combat beyond spamming mouse 1, no indicators when to parry. All the non-combat/rescue objectives are minigames that are pretty easy and not very engaging. Your powers are very situational, even if having the ability to breathe ice to put out fires seems like something you could use in combat.

When not superheroing, you have to make $300 for rent every 5 days or else you're evicted, which wouldn't be so bad... if you had more than just the one unfun taxi driving minigame where you sorta just have to figure out a route to drop of your passenger on the fly within a very short time limit and no GPS to help you get along, with a very poor payout. From the Discussions page, apparently you don't unlock different jobs until you are at least LV23, which is insane because leveling feels like a slog.

Furthermore, the customization is so limited I honestly feel like it doesn't deserve the "Character Customization" tag yet. You can't play as a female character, you can't change anything about your character except his eye and skin color, and you start out without hair. If it IS in the game, it's not available from the get-go, and you're stuck as a bald guy for the start. You CAN get clothing, but clothing costs LOTS of money and you aren't gonna be getting any drip any time soon.

Maybe I'll give this another go in a few years. What's here is interesting, but the beginning is too much of a slog to be worth forcing myself through.

I hope the developers reconsider their game concept. I have rarely played such a boring and repetitive game. So far, it's just loveless garbage. It almost seems as if the developers reached into a box full of mini-games and randomly sprinkled them over the game like sugar sprinkles. Everything you do as a hero feels meaningless and rarely makes sense. The entire city is sinking into chaos while you try to be in too many places at once and help citizens - who are too stupid to go for a walk - out of self-inflicted emergencies.

Examples: A mother loses her child on an open, straight road with no crowds and all she does is crying instead of searching for it. For some inexplicable reason, a citizen's boat breaks down in the middle of the water with no shoals, and they have obviously forgotten their life jacket. A guy has a sprained ankle and needs to be flown to the hospital urgently. This raises questions—especially since every task you don't take on automatically fails. Doesn't this city have a police force, fire department, emergency doctors, or coast guard?

We are superheroes—we should be there for crises where even the National Guard fails and needs our support. I also seem to resemble a hero from the DC universe in a blue and red costume—why does he fall over when he gets hit by a bullet? Why does flying exhaust him? Why does he have to start a mini-game to extinguish a fire in a skyscraper with his freezing breath? Why can't he do it on the fly (pun intended)?

What kind of villains are we supposed to be fighting there, and why are they standing at an intersection with no motivation, just waiting for us? They're obviously not even robbing a bank. You wouldn't even know what their motivation is for waiting for us. I don't even want to talk about the “supervillains.” Why is the A-hole on the streets much faster than us when we chase him, and I, as the hero, have to anticipate where he happens to turn so I can cut him off in midair—and what supervillain calls himself A-hole? It's just ridiculous.

By the way, what is this useless brother doing in my apartment? Why am I living in a rat hole? Why do I have to drive a taxi (which is also super boring, by the way—does anyone else remember SEGA's Taxidriver? That had more pizzazz) to earn a living? Didn't I go to high school? Didn't I go to college? Didn't I have any other plans in my life?

As a game developer myself (or an aspiring one, really) I cannot support the usage of AI in games. Originally , I purchased the game because I had seen it online, and the AI disclaimer said that minimal AI was used, only to speed up texturing for the game's creators. Upon actually playing "Rainbow Gate", every single asset appears to have been made without human interference. Every wall, box, or poster art is made with AI. It is blatantly obvious. Every piece of audio used, hodge-podged by a computer. And all of the game's core systems are either built into Unreal Engine, or easily purchasable on the store, and are identical enough to other titles that use the purchased gameplay packages that I can say without much doubt that they are purchased and non-original assets. So, if a title is made by a combination of purchased assets off of a marketplace, and made in conjunction with an AI creating all the cosmetics of the experience, where was the original developer even involved? Stitching everything together neatly, sure, but that is so small and pales in comparison to all the work done by other sources that I just don't even want to play the game anymore. Now, I want to say that this game isn't all bad. The animations are clean (from what i've seen so far) and I don't know of an AI that can make those. The animators did phenomenal. Along-side it, the game's 3-D models seem very good as well, they don't explode my computer, and despite how the textures are (by their own admission) AI, they do feel very well made. I wish that this title actually hired a 3D designer to work on the project, instead of an LLM subscription. If the main visuals of the game were all original, this would be STUNNING, and a real threat to some of the mascot horror games out there. Such a shame.

When it comes to horror games, I don’t want the enemies to be too similar, or it feels repetitive. I felt that the Wolf and Alligator segments were fine, but then when I got to the Rabbit? Seriously guys, get some actual artists and voice actors because the AI usage in this game is bullshit.

I am a small streamer/youtuber who makes horror game content and enjoys mascot horror games. Never have I ever felt such a level of frustration in a game before. This game not only wasted my time, but my money, and the time of those that watch me live on stream. There are so many things that need to be fixed, and I gave the devs the benefit of a doubt since they are a small team. I was aware that AI was used in the game, however I just assumed it was only used for the first vhs tape that we watch in the game since that's the most obvious thing made with AI. am not the best at recognizing AI at first glace, but as I continued to play the game I noticed things.
1. All the voices sounded really robotic and similar to each other
2. The voices broke and sounded choppy in a way that made me question if it was really a voice actor
3. The characters stamina was extremely poorly done and we did not have enough. He sprints for two second and the characters run much faster than him. The sprint replenishment also took way too long resulting in unnecessary deaths and a long gameplay time.
4. The puzzles were too vague (although some were well done) and there wasn't much direction given to the player to even nudge them in the right direction
5. The sound affects were repetitive. I heard the same weird scuffing and hard breathing from the player that wasn't necessarily bad, but they sounded like some low quality Youtube video sound effect from 10 years ago. Same can be said about that random creak sound you hear every 5 minutes. 
6. None of the characters were rememberable at all, or made any significant impact on the player. Their designs were plain, boring, and not charming
7. Some of the animatronics (like moonbear and the bee) don't even have audio when it comes to their footsteps. It made those parts of the game longer than they needed to be because you get killed and have 5 seconds of stamina to get away
8. There should have been a hot key to press to heal yourself while running, it would have been helpful. Opening your inventory and scrolling takes too much time
9. The zoom in camera affect literally had zero use in the game
10. Through the game you forget that you're there to investigate and look for a missing person. The player doesn't conduct themselves like a private detective would. Who would go here alone and without protection?
11. There is no significant lore that keeps you connected besides "animatronics killed people at the 20th anniversary"
12. The layout of the amusement park makes no sense. No parents in their right might would take their child to a concrete bunker with barely any fresh air to have fun. 
13. I found out through research that most the textures and everything in the game was made with AI. This infuriated me. I'm not opposed to AI if its a simple picture that you're not claiming to be your own or something innocent, but to make an entire game with so much Ai then have the audacity to charge 15 dollars for it is ridiculous. It feels like a scam (Poppy playtime only charged 5 dollars its first release as a comparison)
14. It feels as though the game was made purposefully long so people would go past that 2 hour steam mark and not be able to get a refund
15. The ending however was really well done and I loved it a lot. 
16. There is no reason that this game should take up so much space and have to run at 30 fps. My pc is more than strong enough to handle that yet somehow the game didn't run smoothly higher than 30 fps. I looked at others gameplay and they have specs on their pc similar to mine yet had the same issue. 

Overall this game and team behind it have a lot of work to do. I think it can be an amazing game but right now it feels like a poppy playtime ripoff scam. Work on the story, do the work yourselves with all your heart and I think this game could really be something special in the future.

I was genuinely enjoying this. The demo was great, the puzzles are so intricate and well done, really creative. But the game execution makes this game impossible to actually enjoy. I don't think there was no UX testing at all, the game doesn't have a pause button, the camera feels floaty and just off, you don't have checkpoints within games which is completely unhinged. You gotta do so many things and within each game, stuff doesn't save up. I'll avoid saying any spoilers but there are sections where you need to grab many objects to put them in an order but you're in constant danger, it is completely ridiculous that there is no checkpoints in between, so you have to start all over again, and it's not even like you can actually memorize where things were. This game had a LOT of potential, the story is good, the lore building was nice, the animatronics and the puzzles and how things unfolded were good and really original but the execution and the lack of attention to what real players would face made this feel just stressful and no fun at all.

8. There should have been a hot key to press to heal yourself while running, it would have been helpful. Opening your inventory and scrolling takes too much time
9. The zoom in camera affect literally had zero use in the game
10. Through the game you forget that you're there to investigate and look for a missing person. The player doesn't conduct themselves like a private detective would. Who would go here alone and without protection?
11. There is no significant lore that keeps you connected besides "animatronics killed people at the 20th anniversary"
12. The layout of the amusement park makes no sense. No parents in their right might would take their child to a concrete bunker with barely any fresh air to have fun. 
13. I found out through research that most the textures and everything in the game was made with AI. This infuriated me. I'm not opposed to AI if its a simple picture that you're not claiming to be your own or something innocent, but to make an entire game with so much Ai then have the audacity to charge 15 dollars for it is ridiculous. It feels like a scam (Poppy playtime only charged 5 dollars its first release as a comparison)
14. It feels as though the game was made purposefully long so people would go past that 2 hour steam mark and not be able to get a refund
15. The ending however was really well done and I loved it a lot. 
16. There is no reason that this game should take up so much space and have to run at 30 fps. My pc is more than strong enough to handle that yet somehow the game didn't run smoothly higher than 30 fps. I looked at others gameplay and they have specs on their pc similar to mine yet had the same issue. 

Overall this game and team behind it have a lot of work to do. I think it can be an amazing game but right now it feels like a poppy playtime ripoff scam. Work on the story, do the work yourselves with all your heart and I think this game could really be something special in the future.

I was genuinely enjoying this. The demo was great, the puzzles are so intricate and well done, really creative. But the game execution makes this game impossible to actually enjoy. I don't think there was no UX testing at all, the game doesn't have a pause button, the camera feels floaty and just off, you don't have checkpoints within games which is completely unhinged. You gotta do so many things and within each game, stuff doesn't save up. I'll avoid saying any spoilers but there are sections where you need to grab many objects to put them in an order but you're in constant danger, it is completely ridiculous that there is no checkpoints in between, so you have to start all over again, and it's not even like you can actually memorize where things were. This game had a LOT of potential, the story is good, the lore building was nice, the animatronics and the puzzles and how things unfolded were good and really original but the execution and the lack of attention to what real players would face made this feel just stressful and no fun at all.

I was so excited for this game, until I played it. Nothing made sense, its all AI.. unless you have a super PC you will not be able to play this. My frames were 27 fps, and in the chase scenes it was worse! 15 dollars for this game and its not even good! I am very sad because I liked the demo but this was just not a good overall game.

Designed in a way to force a gamer to spend more than 2 hours before deleting:

1) No speed control, so a gamer has to wait a lot for the game time to pass;
2) No saving of progress, so, if a gamer wants to take a break, they must leave the game opened, hence, increasing the "time played".
3) Because the content is revealed slowly, it creates a feeling: "Okay, now it is boring, but maybe later it will become complex and interesting, let's wait a bit." This never happens, though. 

The whole game can be finished in 20 minutes, if we could increase the speed. No replayability.

Юлик сделал игру гораздо более насыщенную контентом, в десятки раз более артовым визуалом, такого же интеллектуального уровня, но один, а не в восьмером, и за несколько месяцев, а не несколько лет.

Russian friend recommended me this game, saying it wipes the floor with all the Paradox games and is peak court intrigue simulator. What sold me the game, though, is the bizarre development story - apparently one of the developers was criminally convicted for glorifying terrorism and was coerced into inhaling inmate farts in prison. At that point I knew I couldn't miss this one.

Unfortunately, this feels like an attempt at making a functional game, instead of a fun and engaging one. It works, it has an ending and it imitates gameplay quite well. It is indeed a game. Not a fun or interesting, though. Maybe if it was one of the no-name Miniclip dot com flash games from early 2000s it could become a classic.

Obviously it cant compare to actual strategy games. Outside of okay visual style and alright soundtrack, I fail to understand who can find this engaging enough to justify even the tiny price it costs.

You're supposed to find three people who plot against you in seven days, or be killed. To find out who they are, you have the help of four agents who travel to collect clues. The problem with clues is that they don't work, meaning, the game is unfinished and when you click on a suspect with a green dot--that is when a new clue has been found--the only thing in the clue are two attributes that describe the character. There is also a subtitle in the character bio called "Claims that", and I expected this to be filled in as part of the clue. It hasn't, I don't know if it's supposed to fill in at a later stage in the game, and I received no replies to my question on the game's forum.

There are other game features, such as building, recruiting troops with gold, and repulse invasions, but they don't matter since you can't work out the clues. So how come the game received so many positive reviews then? Well, it's a bit of a mystery, but it seems that these reviews compliment the game without addressing the game mechanics, and what makes it a good game, so I have a funny feeling about them.

After playing this masterpiece i lost both legs and my friends started calling me "Chert". The druids from the prison gave me a hint about the traitor and trained me how to smell gases. 12 refunds out of 10.

[quote][b]Note: Dead PvP game[/b]
This game contains no meaningful single player content.  There's no viably populated servers/active player population.  This game cannot be played as intended.[/quote]

[quote][url=https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/AI_slop][b]Warning: AI Slop[/b][/url]
This game is partially or completely made with talentless, soulless AI generated content.[/quote]

[quote][b][url=https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Microtransaction]Warning: Microtransaction infested mobile-tier game![/url][/b]

This game is infested with a toxic mobile app style cash shop.  You will be prompted to and expected to give money to the developers through this cash shop to buy in-game goods so you can play the game.[/quote]

"ScreenPlay CCG" looks exactly like one of thousands of nasty mobile apps infesting the "free" section of Steam, but it's not really free.  While I couldn't find this on the app stores, for all intents and purposes this is a mobile app... it has all the same qualities, from the mobile app tier graphics to the shallow gameplay you expect from mobile app cash harvesters.  We can't say why this wasn't accepted on the mobile app stores, maybe it's because Google and Apple have QA departments, and Valve has "Steam Direct".

This is your basic Hearthstone ripoff only made out of disgusting AI slop, deep in development hell, and is a horribly monetized cash grab as the developers desperately seek to get YOUR money for AI slop. 

There's only a superficial game here.   The game visuals and the game itself are incredibly shallow... think of them as the cheap, bright, colourful plastic cabinet dress theme on a poker machine at the casino. That's basically what this is.  So really, the mobile app "game" is just a veneer, a thin skin wrapped around a nasty, predatory mobile app cash shop.

Putting that toxic mobile app monetisation aside... this is basically a mobile app on the wrong platform.  I didn't spend thousands on a gaming PC so I could pretend it's an iPhone, and I don't think you did either... this kind of mobile app has no place in your Steam library.

The developers committed to finishing the game last year but didn't.  This is deep in development hell and is unlikely to be completed as the developers subscription to ChatGPT ran out (kidding... probably).  And whether the developer finishes this AI slop or not is irrelevant, because the tiny handful of players who got duped into playing an AI slop game already left when they saw it was full of AI slop.

This unfinished cash grab is just polluting Steam and making it harder for gamers to find genuinely, completely made games.

Now on to pricing and value for gamers.

Despite not being able to finish the game, the developers apparently had all the time in the world to build that fully functional cash shop, and financial transaction back-end system into the game.  That shows the developers primary goal was just to set out to gouge cash from Steam gamers just like mobile devs do with mobile peasants.  They want your money for AI slop.  Disgusting doesn't do a good enough job of describing this atrocious behaviour. 

In terms of public reception, this failed to appeal to the 130 million+ gamers on Steam.  SteamDB showed the game peaked at just a dozen or so concurrent players, and now there's basically nobody playing it.  Even though it's free.  This is a dismal public reception, but it's easy to see why gamers rejected it.

It's important to note that, despite this being such a low quality product, there's a number of positive reviews that all appeared immediately after the game launched, from suspiciously inactive Steam accounts that have reviewed almost no other games apart from this.  Take the review score of this game with a grain of salt.

All other issues aside (any of which would disqualify this from being worth your time), this is a game that relies on other players for it to be viable, and because there's not enough players, it's not viable.  I can't recommend anyone downloads this, it's basically dead, and AI slop is an insult to everyone.

I cannot believe that this has good reviews. I am betting that it's from the friends of the developer.

I played this on my channel about a month ago - or whenever the demo came out. It had a lot of AI in it, but I was willing to give it a chance. Not now.

Nothing besides the english translations have been fixed, whatsoever. This has tons of visual bugs and ugly textures on it that constantly change - there's a wall that has a new texture every day. The clothes on the guy walking past you aren't even fitted to the body. If you look up in the alley, there's an ugly lighting/wall glitch. 

It doesn't matter if you win or lose, because after a certain amount of days of playing you will go to the next sequence whether you spotting the anomalies or not. Then you have to go into a building and guess which person is the serial killer. There are no real hints to who it is - and in the book they give you, the portraits don't even match the people in the units. When you talk to people, they don't really give any indication if they're normal or not besides the GLARINGLY obvious guy in room 2 who has a big mess as a room. They keep calling him "Fatty" which is kinda messed up anyway.

This game has no real atmosphere or scares, not even sure why it's called a horror game. The use of AI isn't just casual - it's lazy, sloppy, and not well thought out. 

The sad thing is that the premise of going into a building and finding who's the killer/who's an anomaly is actually interesting, but the execution is so bad I stopped playing half hour in. And got a refund.

Another note, it says "uses AI to generate parts of music and videos" when it uses ai
- To do any animations, including the delivery animations, the driving away animations, and the portrait videos
- Advertisement video and news video are both AI

If you want some genuine feedback, the game would be better with: 
- No ai
- Make your own animations, videos and assets 
- Get rid of the alley way, or at least make it so it matters if you find anomalies
- Go more in depth with the apartment story, it's interesting! 
- Make the portraits in the book match the models, and make the book useful in general 
- Make sure you meet every character before deciding who's the killer
- Polish and fix the games visual bugs and glitches

I purchased Banker Simulator with high expectations, but unfortunately the game suffers from serious and persistent technical issues: frequent crashes every 15–20 minutes, constant lag, and loss of progress during gameplay. These problems only appeared after several hours of play, making the game practically unplayable.
The game has interesting graphics and a promising concept, but until these technical issues are fixed, I cannot recommend it. I hope the developers release significant patches so that other players can finally enjoy the game without problems.

I tried to request a refund on Steam, but it was denied because my playtime exceeded 2 hours. I understand Steam’s policies, but it’s frustrating when a product is technically defective and no compensation is possible.

Customers also come to your bank to pay their enormous electricity bill, you forward the money to the electricity company, for a hefty fee. And you give out loans which are incredibly buggy. Sometimes, the game tells you to go repossess property of a customer who paid their debt, and sometimes it seems customers don't pay their loans back at all and you can't go send the bailiff (aka. yourself) out, and even if you can, the value of the items in the house seems to be the same all the time.
Also, you can print fake money, up to a limit. But if you exceed it, a cop car shows up, you bribe them with a couple hundred bucks and they're gone. Upgrade your machines to make larger bills and increase speed. But the tray, which is essential for moving money around the machines never gets upgraded. WTF is this?
I want to run a real bank, where we earn our money by having us and the employees bring in gullible people in our fancy ass bank and sell them stocks, money market accounts, fixed-term deposits, insurances and all kinds of loans and of course issue our own credit card brand that is free, but has a lot of hidden fees and charges 20 % APV. I purchased Banker Simulator with high expectations, but unfortunately the game suffers from serious and persistent technical issues: frequent crashes every 15–20 minutes, constant lag, and loss of progress during gameplay. These problems only appeared after several hours of play, making the game practically unplayable.
The game has interesting graphics and a promising concept, but until these technical issues are fixed, I cannot recommend it. I hope the developers release significant patches so that other players can finally enjoy the game without problems.

I tried to request a refund on Steam, but it was denied because my playtime exceeded 2 hours. I understand Steam’s policies, but it’s frustrating when a product is technically defective and no compensation is possible.

Its fun in concept, but even for early access it falls short. It took me 11.6 hours to fully complete and that was long into the "end game" content and I spent a lot of that time pursuing other goals like laying out the bank to maximise income.

Bugs I've noticed:
- Employees for money printing sometimes just stand around in a specific spot.
- Bank notes will often seemingly turn into different denominations or into washed or unwashed notes randomly.
- The UI objects that tell you how much of each note denomination you're holding is often wrong and will say you're holding notes you aren't, or not show notes you are holding.
- Honestly, they money tray is the most buggy part of this game which is annoying because it is like half of the game.
- My first money printer just wasn't using any fluid to print, although subsequent ones did.
- Visual glitches with the money.
- The safe doesn't always render the cash in it.

I can forgive having minimal content in an early access game, but I think we should strive for a little more polish... Even in early access. There are plenty of games that enter early access with more to show than this. Assuming the dev continues to work on the game and add the listed "upcoming features", I could see myself coming back to this. But if I'm honest, this was released into early access too soon.

This is the "We have Quarantine Zone at home" game (right down to the handheld xray device to look through clothing). Despite this, I would like to recommend it if it wasn't for a number of problems:

-Too easy to farm cash once you buy an automatic turret. Just stand near the checkpoint's entrance and leave your PC for a bit (especially if there are refugees waiting to be processed) and wait for your turret to eventually kill things.
-Related to the previous, zombies spawn infinitely near the entrance. It makes trying to clear the area out completely pointless, aside from trying to get a little money.
-Turrets are not capable of leading shots, and the bullets they fire travel ridiculously slow. A slow, shambling zombie near the entrance that's moving left or right will result in a turret trying to shoot at it and miss until it decides to stop moving or change direction. Why they aren't just hitscan weapons is beyond me. We don't need ballistics in a game like this.
-Turrets deal very low damage, even with the max upgrade. It can take multiple shots from a T3 turret to kill a bog-standard zombie.
-No long range weapon despite how far out you can see.
-Headshots are inconsistent with large zombies (often counting as regular body shots).
-In rare cases, refugees showing symptoms of confirmed infection are counted as non-infected when sending them to liquidation (killing them politely).
-In very rare cases, refugees can show zero symptoms and be considered infected (I doubt it's me missing something. Sometimes textures for some symptoms do weird things).
-As hinted in the previous, textures for things like bruises, bites, and other things can be "deformed" or "bleed" into other areas. Like if it's on an arm it'll wrap all the way around and also occasionally show on the body itself.
-Performance does odd things from time to time. Without any changes I've experienced it going from smooth 60 FPS to bouncing around 40-60. It does not happen often, though. My specs greatly exceed what's listed for the game, and runs comparative games just fine.
-The flashlight is so bright that you can't see symptoms with it.
-It can be too dark to tell if there are any symptoms in some spots. Of course, a flashlight would help if it wasn't like you're turning on the sun.
-Survivors in quarantine sometimes don't change at all (i.e. symptoms improve or worsen).
-No endless mode.

theres more problems, but honestly, its a friday, and im lazy on fridays. despite all of these problems, the way the game is set up is honestly pretty neat, and is absolutely worth putting on your wishlist and keeping an eye on to see if the problems with the game is sorted out

I'll be keeping an eye on the game as well. If the aforementioned issues are fixed I'll change this review to positive.

Go play Quarantine Zone instead.
This one is just a crappy, buggy clone with bad AI voice acting.
A rip off game with AI voices. Glitchy and annoying to deal with the bugs and raids that never end.

Even for free I do not want to play this game. 
Straight rip off from QZ: The Last Check, maybe a few different elements added in but not in a good way.
Trying to explore outside of the safe zone is near impossible with unlimited spawning zombies from every direction.
The entire checkpoint is manned by YOU, no one else is there. 
Performance is alright, sounds are extremely loud, the gear isn't that great, and the story isn't all that interesting.

the last part of the game is lagging so hard that it is barely playable

the rest of the game is fine even tho it is absurdly simple and has plenty of bugs the bugs are not game breaking and actually kinda funny

But yeah very obvious an unfinished game

I'm probably one of the few that played this INSTEAD of Quarantine Zone, so without the influence of comparison I can say that this game is extremely lacking in its current state.

The game feels very clunky and unfinished, and the inspections are sadly the worst culprit of this. The NPC refugees feel extremely robotic and lifeless, which isn't helped by their distractingly AI-generated voices. They constantly repeat the same lines, explain/make excuses for injuries they don't have, and just generally have no 'life' to them. For one of the main gameplay loops, this really needed to be better.

The shooting and base-defense aspects are fine, but the zombie movement could use some work. If you're going to heavily incentivize headshots, you can't have enemy animations that are this jittery and unpolished. The crawlers go from skittering around like a 5fps cocaine spider to flying through the air like a statue shot out of a cannon. 

Game is getting regular updates and fixes, but I just can't see it reaching a point where I would recommend spending your money on it. It's disappointing and half-baked.
Badly optimized, screen looks like a smudge of poo. Clearly trying to rip off of Quarantine Zone, badly at that. Asset flip.

Quarantine Zombies has a solid idea for a checkpoint simulator, and for about two-thirds of the game I was genuinely enjoying myself.
The problem starts in the last ~10 days on duty, where the game suddenly begins to break its own rules.

You check a person: no symptoms or barely suspicious ones → you let them through → infected.
Next person: heavy symptoms, everything points to infection → you stop them → healthy.
This doesn’t feel like rising difficulty, but rather randomness pretending to be a mechanic.

Because of that, player decisions stop mattering, since logic no longer applies.
The game is also very short, underdeveloped, and feels more like early access than a finished product.
It used to be a good simulator, but then it took an arrow to the knee.

With a heavy heart, I do not recommend it, even though the potential was clearly there.

Boring, no map to figure out where to go,

This was alright to begin with, it started off as this creepy and atmospheric walking simulator, as the streamer & the police officer to begin with, was nice! Especially in the tunnels and the jumpscares. I also enjoyed that section in that library bit, with that glitchy entity, was kinda funny, and it was almost like a stealth section. Some of the puzzles were simple. But then it suddenly turned into the a survival horror, which was like Resident Evil/Silent Hill, which it suddenly became gross and gloomy with those skeleton like creatures running around and chasing you, unless you hid haha. 

Then at the end, it suddenly became Exit 8.. which didn't seem to work. As was I looking for anormalies? I didn't know until i found that note, but even when I thought I spotted some, and I tried to go back, I couldn't and had no choice but to go forward, and then starts the cycle again. The combat, especially with the boss fights, I wish some of the chests had more fixed items, like shells or whatever I needed, like medicines. And that section after the tunnels with those spinny things, was a bit too long. 

Overall, I did have fun, I screamed and I liked the start of the game, however, some things did need working on.

Amazing concept but poor execution. I picked up the game cause it looked really similar to fears to fathom games and it was on sale for $3.49. The game has a very short playtime, about an hour and it is too pricy for the amount of content. It really is a fantastic concept that I wish Rayll from fears to fathom got a hold of first. The story line makes sense for the tasks you have to do but a lot of the lines don't make logical sense. Like having to pick up the original box of soda to clean up cans. It could also really use something to do instead of just going straight from the video games to the next day. I think it is a full copy cat of fears to fathom using the same type of text and tasks, as well as the sound bar and sound the killer makes when he spots you. It does have some original content that is not exactly modeled like fears to fathom. The protagonist being a pervert to the girls in his window is a strange addition of content but its the only original thing I can find in this game. Overall the game is all right but is a copy of fears to fathom done worse and is not worth the money. It is much cheaper to get fears to fathom game and they have more content. The first game of fears to fathom is similar in quality and free but the games get progressively better as they keep going. Without any sales this game is more expensive than fears to fathom 2 and 3 and it will only cost four dollars more to get Fears to Fathom 4 and 5.

game_name: STAY ALIVE MY SON (VR): A True Story About A Father's Relentless Search For His Son
review_text:
Interesting but very bad technical/VR implementation:
No seated VR option (for no obviou reason).
No subtitles or language options.
Very hardware demanding (for no reason, graphics are mediocre or just ok at best), had graphics issues all the time. 
Killing bugs (abandoned game due to leaving a necessary object at an inaccessible spot).
Not tested on popular controllers (have Pico4 but I read of issues on Meta also), had to find walk-around solution...
There are many interesting free experiences (its really not a game) with no such issues, so price is unacceptable.

This was the first time I enjoyed reading customer reviews at the end of a game day! I'm impressed. 
It's a solid system that could for sure make a fun game. It's almost there. 

They forgot to make it into a game though.
- There are no rewards to pursue or penalties to avoid. 
- There is nothing to build, level up, or buy - so a 1 star review is effectively the same as 5 star review.
- It feels like a demo.

I love the focus on multiplayer!
+ Specializing in a trade gives you unique creative abilities which is fantastic for needing each other.
- I wish it was easier to keep track of what my ally was doing while I'm doing my thing. If I didn't keep demanding to know who they were helping and what they needed every 30 seconds in voice chat to avoid overlapping efforts again it would have felt like single player.
They made a tech demo with AI and charge money for it.

[b]TL;DR:[/b]
I can’t recommend this game at its current price for what you get, Maybe in the future when more stuff is added. 
If I had purchased this myself, I would have refunded it within the first hour.

[b]Review:[/b]
I received this game for free from the developer and because of that I want to be completely honest.
Other reviews are right, this game does have potential. 
However, in its current state it is severely lacking and because of that I can’t in my right mind recommend it to anyone right now.

I played for 5+ hours and unfortunately, what’s here is extremely basic.
There is no real sense of progression, no meaningful consequences for your actions and no lasting interactions.

At the end of each client session, you tell them “see you next week” but you never actually see that client again. 
You meet them once and that’s it. Because of this, there’s no payoff for trying to be a good therapist, no evolving relationships and no feeling that your choices actually matter long term. 
While insulting clients and making them angry can be amusing at first, that novelty wears off very quickly.

Another thing worth mentioning is the use of AI. 
The game already uses AI in some areas, which I don’t mind at all it’s just surprising that AI voice acting hasn’t been implemented.

Given the game’s built-in focus on streaming, including letting chat vote on dialogue choices, having to constantly read out client dialogue and every response option quickly becomes repetitive and dull during longer sessions. Even basic AI voice work for clients would go a long way toward improving immersion, pacing, and overall watchability for streamers.

I understand this is an Early Access title that only released on December 10th. That said, I personally feel the game was released too early, before enough of its core mechanics and roadmap features were implemented.

Once some of those systems are added, I could absolutely see myself recommending this game but right now, it simply isn’t there.

I’ll happily revisit and update this review if and when those systems are added. 
Until then, I can’t recommend this game in its current state.

If you're wondering why this game has such positive reviews, but you cannot see any recent reviews that's bc 90% of English reviews are Chinese-Based or Botted accounts that are promoting this game for a suspicious reason.

And yes I brought receipts:

https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3432552216

[h3]Steam Deck[/h3]

It does not work on Linux, even with Proton[GE]. It tries to install Microsoft Edge components which are essential for the game to work at all as this appears to be a web-like video game.

[h3]Conclusion[/h3]

This is more of a warning, the game is being review botted, it costs $2.99 & I can't help but be suspicious of the motive. The Game's directory may be up to no good in the background & I highly encourage you to NOT install this game.

There are plenty of other games in Steams 100K+ Games Library for you to play, this one is suspicious & I don't reccomend you install this game to even try it out.

I am running Linux, so testing this game is of nearly no risk to me as I never actually ran in it the first place & I can wipe all of the Proton Data after uninstalling & leave nothing behind; [b]YOU[/b] on Windows might not be so lucky...

DON"T BUY THIS. Steam should recall this game, or force it to have another title. They are piggybacking off of another more famous game with the same title. IMO this is a scam and they should be booted off the platform.

The best "review" I can give for this game is a mini-guide I wrote here:  https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3613532473  

But other than that... 
This game has a cheeky sense of humor and I like that.  A soulless corporate CEO forces you into slavery by dumping your grandfather's debt on you, and so you have to mine minerals from asteroids for his company... forever.  Unless you find something super shiny and pay off your grandfather's debt first.  Meanwhile, you have to solve the mystery of what really happened to your grandfather.  So you take his old RV (I mean spaceship) and travel around the galaxy mining asteroids to pay the bills.  
It's FUN, shows PROMISE to be a good game.  
But it suffers from some MAJOR BUGS that make it impossible to play.  These are currently (as of Dec 1 2025) being looked into, and if these bugs get fixed then I would say this is a good game.  But until they get fixed, I have to say DO NOT BUY THIS GAME right now.  WAIT for the bugs to get fixed first.  Here is a list of the bugs: 

1.  With the way 3-d movement works in this game, it's too easy to lose track of where you parked your ship.  Then you take so long to find it that you run out of oxygen before you can get back to it.  When your oxygen runs out, you get teleported into the ship but you lose ALL of the ores and gems that you mined, which means you earn NO MONEY for that run.  Fortunately the developer has already said he will be adding an arrow to the scanner tool that will point towards your ship to make it easier to find!  So now we just wait for that to get implemented. 
2.  The vacuum collector is SUPPOSED to suck up any nearby ores so that you don't have to grab them directly by hand anymore.  But it only collects about 1/3 of what it targets; the rest of the ore just gets flung behind you at nearly light speed, getting scattered across deep space.  Then you have to fly around and try to FIND them all again and pick them up one at a time by hand, which SUCKS.  Especially since as you fly you're running out of oxygen. 
3.  On the fourth map I think (it's called "BIG ASTEROID" and has a couple of small asteroids and one big one with its own gravity) you can't actually dig the asteroid.  If you try, then all the ore will sink to the center of the asteroid - which SOUNDS like a good thing since you can pick it all up in one place.  But actually it is a BAD thing because there are a THOUSAND ores in that asteroid and it KILLS your graphics and physics engines, which makes the fps drop to around 0.5 fps and then freezes the game so you need to hit Alt-F4 to quit before your graphics card catches on fire.  I'm not exaggerating.  It really is THAT BAD.  (I'm running an Intel i9-14900KF with a GeForce 4070 Ti-Super and 32 GB of RAM, so it should NOT be dropping to 0.5fps.  I even tried lowering the graphics settings AND shrinking the resolution below HD standards and it is still a huge problem.)
4.  Combine bugs 2 and 3 on the BIG ASTEROID by trying to use the vacuum collector tool on the asteroid after digging some ore... you end up freezing your game AND all of the ore from the asteroid will get thrown at WARP FACTOR NINE behind you, up and off of the asteroid, scattered across half the galaxy and then you'll never be able to reach it all - even if you do find it all.  

Because the collector and fps glitches are so bad, I had to stop playing the game halfway through that fourth asteroid field.  So I'll never know what really happened to grandpa and I'll never become a space mining millionaire.  Hopefully they get these FIXED soon so I can come back later and finish the game.  But sadly, for now the game is unplayable due to these bugs.
Lots of fun, but is so poorly optamised for the tools it gives you. Set off multiple bombs, try and suck up all the resources, instant lag and then game crash...

Ai generated characters, a bug where it just teleports you to a random location for no reason, I got teleported into a locked room on the ship and had to reload the game, losing all the resources I had on me. The big planets feel like a chore to mine, and the translation is terrible. Overall, pretty awful game. Barely worth 5 dollars.
This game is far too easy, and uses ai for characters and probably update logs. Honestly, this game is shit. Play something else.

Okay, from the top. My review picked up traction and honestly I think it's best if I go back and address some things. The original review is still at the bottom of this massive wall of text and I want to make a few things clear. 

I am not active in the brickadia community, I am very much an outsider and wish to keep things that way. I do not wish to debate or talk about allegations against the devs further because i value my free time and have no skin in the game whatsoever.

For one, I stand by the fact I do not reccommend this game. Not only was the initial price tag of 30 euro a total shock, with a massively inflated starting price for an incomplete game, but this was only recently remedied by reducing the price down to 20 euro. That's a reasonable price. 

 The fact is the original review by phobos i based this review on got deleted for whatever reason and commenters have made it abundantly clear that the exposal documents were made under dubious circumstances, with the controversy being covered almost exclusively by accounts spewing right-wing and blatantly transphobic arguments. I find the whole situation shady and I don't want to touch it with a 9 foot pole frankly. 

I find the devs shady, I find the pundits covering the situation shady.  To put it plainly and simply, I just don't want to touch this game after this whole situation. I think the devs NEED to address this because when I first learned of the whole port controversy I immediately took to making the original review.
 


Will I buy it again? Probably not. Will this make the game more active? Oh, 100%, the high asking price was the initial thing that scared me off buying this game before I refunded it. Do I care? Not really anymore. This whole drama, poor handling of the community and general vagueness of everything make me not want to touch this game anymore.

TLDR: The devs suck at community management and honestly they need to address this stuff, the game suffers from being incomplete and previously suffered from an exorbitant asking price which was lowered on the fly, without actually giving anything to players that paid full price for an incomplete game.
Frankly I'd give this one a miss.

i cannot for the life of me reccomend this game, sadly. I do not think you should buy this game or support the developers in ANY WAY WHATSOEVER. For why i believe so, please read the full review.

To preface, I'm someone who grew up on Roblox since mid-2017, who has become completely disillusioned with Roblox's current state. I knew about Blockland (this game's original version) since around somewhere in 2019 through youtube videos as "a funny little roblox clone!". I also remember hearing something vague about one of the developers being off, but I kind of just pushed everything related to it to the back of my head.

My main praise is with the art direction. I love this game's artstyle! It feels like a mix of roblox, action figures and lego figures, with pretty cool and crazy customization options, all free of charge. Literally spent the first 30 minutes of me playing this game cosplaying as the source missing texture lmao.

 I first played this game with friends during next fest and found the prospect and artstyle of this game really cool! "the simple building mechanics of Roblox combined with the sandbox mechanics of Gmod? it's a little clunky, but sign me up!" I eagerly kept my eyes on the game, playing the hell out of the next fest demo, and being like "this game is great, I cannot wait to buy this game for 10-20 euro!"

Thirty godamn euro. That's ridiculous, especially for an unfinished game in early access. The game is incomplete, full of bugs and you're telling me the final price is gonna be more than that? What the hell? Gmod is 10 dollars and roblox is free (heavy quotations). While I understand games becoming more expensive, after all developers need to eat (trust me, as someone who is actively studying game design, i REALLY get this), but a base cost of 30 euro is downright stupid.

The game is also just dead. Barely anyone plays it, and honestly I wonder why (maybe don't make the ticket to entry 30 dollars). The game runs awfully on my new-ish gaming laptop, servers are horrifically laggy and right now the game worlds just feel empty, which the opposite of what you want in a sandbox-social game. 

Oh yeah, there's also the AI generated code, which the developers claim is just for the repetitive tasks, where if true, is a very tame use of AI in the art of making games in the grand scheme of things, but if you are wholely against the use of AI in game creation that is a strong dealbreaker, especially if you are a strong believer in the AI slippery slope argument.

All the previous issues are negliable to what i'm about to say. I would happily have bitten the silver bullet and called the game a 6 or 7/10, fun to play with friends, less so with randoms if what I'm about to say wasn't a problem (i swear this isn't a 67 joke this is actually what i think about the game 😭)

What made me refund the game was the fact that the developers actively support pedophiles. not in like "oh one guy made an offcolor joke in a chatroom once 12 years ago kinda thing", like the GODAMN FORMER LEAD CODER LIKED KIDS, CALLS THEMSELVES A SHOTACON, HAS MADE WEIRD COMMENTS TO KIDS SINCE 2012 AND CONTINUES TO ACT THIS WAY TO THIS DAY. While they should have instantly fired this guy, removed as much of their influence as possible and soldiered on, they continued to keep people like this around, even welcoming them back in the community after being disgraced. Other developers showcase odd behaviours such as reposting furry porn on their work account right next to Brickadia concept art, which is extremely odd considering Brickadia is a game intended for all ages with a toylike artstyle.

For more info, check this google doc link posted by a prominent community member: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1phwpWHgOoskt4lQdPBgThMbBcbZhf0Db0qYy8iYThts/

Special thanks for Phobos and their amazing steam review for actually making me aware of this, you can read it here for the full details: 
https://steamcommunity.com/id/phobosbbq/recommended/2199420/

Please refund the game if you can and cease support of the developers, since this is a recurring pattern and I do not believe this game or the developers will change anytime soon, they have been engaging in this since 2012.

All i'm gonna say is, to the devs of brickadia, what a letdown. I expected better and honestly I shouldn't be surprised by stuff like this anymore. How hard is it to not hire and keep around people who are literal pedophiles. I would say do better, but my expectations are currently lower than the Mariana Trench.
[/quote]

Played during Next Fest and right before the game launched on Steam. This game had so much promise and was shaping up to be quite the sandbox, and I quite enjoyed my playtime during the Demo- building cars was something I wish was doable in Blockland without the need to model and script. 

Not long after the Demo, I was granted a code to play just before the game would release, although I hardly played it all due to waning interest on my end. Then I learned pretty much everything that'd happened behind the scenes and now I absolutely do not want to touch this game at all. I was already not fond of the price but that cemented my choice to not purchase it.

Pros:
- Great building system
- Ability to build your own vehicles
- Neat physics system
- Hosting a game is simple
- Building and editing tools are fun to use
- Avatar editor has good options
- (mostly) well optimized

Cons:
- Price tag
- No modding support (as of right now)
- Smaller vehicles have a tendency to flip over
- Horrifically low player count

Difficult to really recommend this game. While fun, the lack of a playerbase and any UGC/mods are going to destroy its longevity. If you're dying to build with bricks in a LEGO like game, Blockland is significantly cheaper, has plenty of mods for just about everything, and despite its age, gets more players than Brickadia.

(This game is genuinely my FAVOURITE and i've only been playing for ~2 days. I know its totally early access, but the things you can do it in right now is crazy. I'm someone who went from playing BABFT on ROBLOX, and even though that game let you be more specific and detailed with how you build, this game is literally better than that & every other game i know in every single other way.

First: Originally I thought the character costomizing was bad, but then i discovered everything and now im a changed man. The character costomizing is SO good, and even though you have to get a little bit creative with how you may make the character you wanna make, that actually is a plus in my eyes. It reminds me of the good old days of roblox where you had to use the mad scientist hair to make sonic quils and it was iconic and respected the style of the game. It's limited but in the best, most unlimiting way I suppose. Roblox's UGC sorta ruined that for me so im glad i can experience a similar system again.

Second: THE GAME IS SO SMOOTH, and perhaps that should be expected, but when I tell you that its possible to spawn like 2 million houses and then throw them into a giant washing machine, or spam the map with your creation, flinging it every whichway, where other games would literally implode and manifest a black hole inside your computer, i think thats enough for you to say, "perhaps this game could handle my magnum opus..?"

Third: The building system is PRETTY GOOD. I've only been playing for ~2 days, but I've been able to make a pretty alright version of the Death Egg Robot with my limited experience. I've seen other people make these pretty houses and amazing cars and to know whats possible if you try hard enough is actually really inspiring. I will say, there is a slight learning curve but if you can just get lost in a build and youre solving problems while your encountering them, i think youll learn pretty fast. Imo, get to know your inventory and how various parts can fit toghether.

Fourth: The mechanical/computery stuff.. I really like it so far. I've only done one thing, but I can already smell the potential... I don't really wanna say so much on this zone, but just know, that.. I like it and I want to learn everything about it, and some of you smart people who are good at math and mechanics and whatevs will DEFINITELY like it and probably know all of it.

Fifth: The game is really pretty.. Its got nice water, and when you drop into the water it actually ploops, and splashes.. Theres nice lighting effects which too, its costomizable so its definitely possible for someone to come along and make a really well-lit city or zone to vibe and chill B)... My pc is pretty old and stuff, but it was still able to look really gud, while spawning all those shadow-casting objects.. and! THE CLOUDS MOVE.. The sun was literally going behind the clouds, causing the maps lighting to change realistically.. That blew my flipping mind... Very pretty game.

Sixth: My 1 tiny tiny teeny weeny concern.. Theres not really that many players.. Like 50 people in 70 servers, and that doesn't seem right to me. This game is AMAZING.. I feel like i struck gold, lapis, emerald,  and diamond when i found it.. Yeah, the game is 30 somethin dollars, but it's just so worth it, and.. I think you would be happy with what it is, cuz, I know I sure am. You could literally BE ANYONE.. DO ANYTHING. To the devs, please dont abandon this game cuz its literally my dream come true, and when you add the wicked destruction and whatevs.. It would just blow my mind.. Anyway.. thanks for reading if you read all this.. its just how i feel, true and honestly..

The game has an interesting prestige system with very differing options that actually make your runs feel different. You can pretty easily reach the end within 15-20h and there are no particularly difficult mechanics in the game. The ones that are there are not explained insanely well and the overall level of information is pretty meh - to optimize your late game setup you'd need to have an overview over which sources of certain things produce how much for example and that is nowhere to be found.

Additionally there is a shitton of bugs all throughout the game, which will either leave you confused or annoyed regulary, especially towards longer runs. These longer runs themselves are less and less well balanced the longer you go, which is a shame and shows that a bunch of the mechanics are only really meant to work for a certain timeframe. One example is that you need maybe 1M/s of one resource during a long run, which can be produced at a rate of 1:1000 of another resource. Your refinery for the first one can easily be upgraded to reach those 1M/s, but the refinery for the 2nd resource can also only be upgraded to reach 1M/s. That you'd need a thousand times that amount to even feed the other refinery is not balanced around at all and you need to resort to manually buying the resource (which is arguably easy enough). Another issue is that your profit from sales is directly proportional to the amount you can sell with one click, which is capped. Towards the end you can easily get 1e15 products / s and only sell 1e10 per click, which is obviously never going to make even a dent in the amount of produced products.

One clear positive point is that the devs are very actively working on it, patching the roughest edges of the game and being very communicative about it.

It was interesting at the start, but two things put me off this game:

2.  The stock market.  After 2 prestiges it seems likely I could more or less complete the game with those stocks - not really any point to do anything else.
Some other issues such as the numbers not matching - eg. shows I have 2 trillion but in fact I have 20 trillion.
If the game was half the price I might have given a reluctant thumbs up - but as it stands I need to give a thumbs down to the experience.

TLDR: After 10-ish hours, I really liked this game to begin with, but after a few hours when you get into the mid game some things just feel lacking, slow, and unbalanced.

First of all, once I got into the mid game (small spoiler ahead), [spoiler]Dividens from stock accounted for about 98% of my income boosting me into the quadrillions.[/spoiler] 

Secondly, although it is fun hand-crafting at first, later on it can get tedious when you've already done it literally a hundred times. 

Thirdly, production for materials feels... interesting. I cannot quite tell if I like it yet or not. When I got to [spoiler]Missiles[/spoiler] a lot of my material production became unable to support it's production, even when I spent all my money on upgrading it even though I was able to afford the automatic crafting upgrades. And it wasn't even close.

Fourthly and lastly, the prestige system is a bit unconventional, but not bad. They have very unique boosts for specific things, special unlocks, etc. My issue with it is for example that when prestiging some things feel really slow, like you don't get to keep anything that you have unlocked (other than stock, from what I've noticed). 

I think this game has a promising start overall, even though I've listed a lot of negative things. I think if the developers rebalance prestiging a bit and add some more conventional and permanent boosts/automation, this game would [i]feel[/i] a lot better. For now I will keep playing a bit, and see if I can add something else to my review.

It isn't actually an idle game; it's an incremental game with idle aspects. However, it requires a lot of attention until you can automate a lot of the production aspects later on.

Pros:
-It's a cute incremental game made with attention to detail.
-The models are nice.
-Progression feels earned and is mostly not sluggish.

Cons:
 
-Maximum playtime is 10 hours unless you grind the last two or three achievements.
-Full price is too high (10 Euros)
-Requires too much manual clicking (assembling weapons), which becomes very repetitive for an 'idle' game. The options to boost this are minuscule until very late in the game and at maximum prestige.
- The use of ChatGPT for descriptions is declared, but at least remove the em-dashes.... However, I know that as a solo developer it's a lot of work and AI helps cut on the work time here, so this is just my personal grievance.

Edit: Since the developer fixed the constant crashes that made the game unplayable, I have to rewrite my review.
I would still give it 60/100, but for the price it's more like a 45/100, making it not recommendet (for the price).

I enjoyed this game at first but it's totally worn me out. And I say this as someone who plays too many idle games.

Each run, you generate Silver and Gold. Silver is the temporary currency you use to buy fish, skill, and stall upgrades, anything tied to that specific run. These skills are arranged in tiers, and each time you complete a tier, a new one unlocks. The new tier usually has some permanent unlock that goes along with it, so not only does completing tiers help future runs go smoother, but you gain something new which gives you a tiny dopamine hit. Silver always resets when you Ascend so there's no reason to hold onto it.

Gold is bankable. You generate it during the run but you can't touch it until you Ascend. When you Ascend, the game finally gives you the Gold you earned so you can spend it on long term improvements in the Ascension Tree. These include permanent bonuses that are supposed to make future runs stronger, as well as new fishing locations and upgrades to your fishing boat so you can hire more crew members to auto fish for you.

You also earn medals from repeating fishing contests. In theory they're supposed to give you extra bonuses, but in practice they barely make a dent.

The problem is that by Tier 14 of the skills you buy with Silver every run, you get stuck. No matter what you've bought with medals and Gold, you just don't seem to get any closer to finishing Tier 14. I think I've been stuck on Tier 14 for several weeks now. I don't even know how many times I've Ascended. And by this point, when I do Ascend, it doesn't feel like I'm banking enough Gold to get through the absolutely [i]huge[/i] Ascension Tree so I have to save up and do more runs. Each upgrade I buy just feels like a drop in the ocean now. The game becomes sluggish and heavy.

[strike]The dev added an excavation minigame recently that's fully manual, which hasn't been popular since it requires active play in a formerly 100% idle game. I'm a relatively newer player so I'm not as upset about this change as older players are, although I understand their irritation, but I still don't understand what excavating fossils has to do with fishing. So I'm just like, "Mmmkay..."[/strike] (Update: excavation now has an auto feature. I'm sill not sure what excavation has to do with fishing though, and I'm still stuck on tier 14. Lol)

What makes all of this even stranger is how late the game waits to give you a real prestige option. In most idle or incremental games, you get some form of prestige early so you can wipe everything for a stronger permanent boost. In IF, this appears to be the Rebirth feature, which you don't unlock until you finish the entire Ascension Tree. Once you Rebirth, you lose all your Ascension upgrades and start again with the Amber Tree instead, or so I've read. I'm nowhere near Rebirth with almost 60 hours logged. I don't think I'm even halfway there. I can't imagine doing all of this again. I don't trust that the dev has his math right.

[b]TLDR[/b]: Tier 14 kills the fun, I'm not even the only person to bring it up on the forums (dev said it's operating as expected). Progress stalls, Gold upgrades feel useless, and the loop gets stale. I can't help but feel like the developer put some decimals in the wrong place.

It's half fun. I don't like the graphics but I like video game fishing, so all good so far, as the fishing system is well done and fairly interesting even if on a idle manner. My big problem with this game is that I installed it to score all the achievements but the developer just keeps adding more, and I don't understand why you'd do so so far after release. Some of these take a LOT of time and offline time won't count. Sure, integrate new systems, but does everything need 100 achievements on top? And Excavation - did the game really needed that as is?

Yeah cant bring myself to finish this game. its completely unpolished. the fighting is always the same. exiting a boat or boarding a boad is a nightmare. even one of the swords its not the most expensive yet its double the damage of the most expensive one. shops that dont accept equipment you want to sell so you get stuck with that equipment forever. and then when you need to upgrade your ship its asking for 3x the shops maximum supply. so you need to come back over 3 days to get enough equipment for an upgrade. all basic issues that need to be fixed. I cant fathom why this game is currently over 80% positive. 6/10 at best.

This game is not Full Release quality by any reasonable standard. This game shouldn't be listed as anything past Alpha grade. There are game breaking bugs, main storyline quests to locate the relic of light (or whatever its called), its no where in the game, you can only get it by a glitch, I found youtube videos of this game from over a year ago, and they had the same problem. The quest intros are written in broken english and some are downright useless. The UI breaks and points all over the map some times. I spent 2 hours stuck "in combat" with a ship I boarded, looted and killed the whole crew, then sank it and yet somehow its still attacking me. I built a cook station on my ship as required in a quest and its floating in the air off the island where my ship was parked when i completed the quest. The combat is simple and for a laid back game like this I understand, but looting your spoils to get rich as a pirate...nope. I found 1 cloth merchant who you do one of maybe 2 sidequests in the game for and once you give him his 10 cloth for that he doesn't buy any more for the rest of the game cause his stock is full. I looted over a half dozen short pirate pistols worth a pretty penny, and no vendor in the game will buy them. There are several other items in game where you are just letting them stockpile for no reason other then the fact you can't mass drop a stack of 99 rocks so if you want to clear them out you gotta do the drag and drop outside your inventory menu 99 times.

My biggest regret is that it took me 6ish hours to hit the major bugs in the game and Steam doesn't want to do a refund.

- The boat is not much better. You're not having fun, you're just waiting. (Looking at you Hidden Island)
- The game slows down a LOT when you look horizon level. I have a beast of a PC, I shudder to think how the average player is affected.
- There is not a lot of gameplay in the game. Would be ok if the game was nice to walk around in, but again, the game itself gets in the way.

I like this game, I love Fallen Enchantress and I have played Elemental War of Magic and Sorcerer King.  Elemental Reforged suffers from many of the same issues as those games. -Item crafting is cool, but very limited...sort of useless after awhile.
-Unsure why you can produce some of the materials you need in crafting...I've killed every creature in the world and the crafting material is lacking.
-City unrest is just dumb...need more building to reduce this.
-The epic quests are just at some point.  Im rocking fully upgraded everything, all the spells, all the gear, all the XP...and my entire party gets one shotted (technically 2) by a dragon.
-The spells you can cast in the world are awesome...but then in battle, my level 15 mage with 500 mana, and all the stuff cant do more than a 40 damage fireball.
-No matter how good you make the units, how much you kit them out, how much XP they have...they just die.

I did enjoy early game, but this feels like a much cheaper version of endless legend...but with crafting that needs work.

To the designers:
-Better combat spells
-Unrest needs to chill
-expand crafting ALOT and make it so we can produce materials...or at least buy them.
-More scaling.  Seems like the game ends early, but the epic bosses are comically OP.

If you've already played Fallen Enchantress, this is an identical game with a better graphics engine. There are some very minor additions: you collect crafting elements and recipes and individual units have their own inventories like in Sorcerer King (which contradicts the unit design function of Fallen Enchantress) and there's a new dynasty system where you marry and have kids and can choose to either breed or play the offspring. Do not waste your money buying the same game.

If you haven't played Fallen Enchantress before and like the idea of a fantasy 4x with a focus on exploring and tactical combat (like Heroes of Might and Magic), then this might be a worthwhile purchase. I've played this series more than I played Civ VI. I was so excited to see that they are revisiting the series because it feel full of unexplored potential. Honestly, I prefer Fallen Enchantress and Sorcerer King as separate games because they play quite differently, but if you only want to buy one, I hope you enjoy it as much as I have.

"Frostrain" is an uninventive game that rips off the thematic design of "Snowpiercer" (train in endless snow... so creative) and combines it with gameplay ripped from Teamfight Tactics and a bunch of AI slop... the creative bankruptcy here is an insult to all gamers.  So, you may ask, how is the game presented?  Of course, like a pixelcrap mobile app.  Ugh.  Let's get into the rest.

What do you do in the game?  The game loop is short, repetitive, and tiring after the first couple of loops.  You get a random train full of passengers and you keep them happy by playing randomly drawn cards from your deck... this might even work well but it seems like more of a rushed demo for a fully paid (and also AI slop infested) second game.  So this is more of an AI slop advertisement, advertising more AI slop.

While I couldn't find this on the mobile app stores, everything about it looks and feels like it's a mobile app designed for mobile phones... for PC gaming enthusiasts, it's not going to give you a cutting edge PC gaming experience.  That alone is enough to disqualify this from any recommendation.  We don't spend thousands building gaming rigs so that we can pretend they're iPhones and play iPhone games and mobile apps.

This features really godawful pixelcrap "art" instead of real graphics.  It's hard to say if this was because the developer didn't have the skill to do the job of creating 3D visuals, or couldn't afford to hire someone who does, but it also doesn't matter.  What matters is that this looks much worse than most other games because of that decision, and that's a major deterrent to gamers.  Games like Terraria and Stardew Valley succeeded [i]despite[/i] the bad graphics, not because of them.

As mentioned, this features really godawful, "wrong" looking AI slop instead of genuinely and sincerely created game assets.  It's hard to say if this was because the developer didn't have the skill to do the job of creating real game assets or couldn't afford to hire someone who does, but it also doesn't matter.  What matters is that this looks bad because of that decision.  AI slop sticks out like a sore thumb... and it comes across as insulting.

There's also the old adage that those who lack the budget and talent to make their own game assets usually lack the budget and talent to do the rest of the job of game development properly, too, and that's glaringly obvious here.

The developer chose not to do the job of a real game developer and create meaningful bespoke levels, narrative based gameplay progression and so on.  Instead, they decided that "Procgen IS content!" and lazily tried to use procgen algorithms to create infinite, samey, repetitive, soulless levels, because that's a lot less work.

As a result of this poor decision, the game gets tired and repetitive almost immediately.  Developers must learn that procgen is a terrible substitute for creating real content.  It should not be used as "ez-mode" for content creation, as the developer tried to do here.

Now on to pricing and value for gamers.

This is "free" enough (not every "free" game in the "free" section is truly free, there's usually some hidden price tag), at least you won't be expected to spend any more money after you download this, but just because something is free doesn't mean it's good, and the cost of a game is more than just financial, your time is valuable too.

Bearing in mind that free games don't add +1 to your game collector count, should you bother adding this to your library and downloading it?  What's the verdict?

This is a hard pass. There's no immediate financial commitment, but there are thousands of more compelling games on the platform that will give you a lot more enjoyment for your time.

The use of AI slop is a direct insult to gamers and real, ethical content creators and artists everywhere.

Fossilized Wonders is a game that I like a lot. It holds one of the most interesting and fun mechanics in the series that makes every single run worth it. Music's great, both new and old characters in the game are awesome, and generally a lot of things that one loves about Touhou can be found in this game.

Despite that, the usage of AI is a very big problem the game possess, wether it is the backgrounds or the story and its concepts. Which for a series that for years has had breathtaking stages and while silly, still pretty interesting plots, is a big shame. 

Hopefully the AI doesn't become a mainstay and reviews like this can be heard

Unfortunately, this is probably one of the worst touhou game.

- There are some serious visibility issues, notably stage 4 and activated hyper.
Hell the stage 4 background looks like vomit, and even worse it rotates and makes me extremely dizzy.
The hyper... usually shouldn't be a issue but the non-existent i-frame after breaking hyper makes it really annoying as the breaking effect obstructed the view next to the player. Cherry borders in PCB and hyper in WBaWC work fine, but now it is really terrible.

- I don't find much creativity in the patterns. Ariya's ultimate is just spam (similar to Keiki in WBaWC) and many spellcards feel reused. A spellcard of Yuiman looks like Parsee (SA)'s last spell. Toyohime's nonspells are so obnoxious. Some designs are terrible like stage 4/5 midboss and random "death bullets".

- Difficulty - holy this game on normal is basically on steroids. I hold some lunatic/hard clears in other game and normal in this game still feels unreal. Stage 2 is already comparable to lunatic in PCB. Easy is still easy. And normal is much worse than normal in difficult games (SA/UFO/LoLK).

-The music doesn't really clicked for me - Although I think this has been an issue that has been plaquing the later entries.

- Lags when there are too many bullets being cancelled (This is extremely frustrating). The fullscreen crash is still here. No keybinds change for movement key... still.

If you are looking to get into touhou games, I would recommend Unconnected Marketeers (cool mechanics with cards gambling)/Legacy of Lunatic Kingdom (basic but extremely solid and comes with pointdevice - checkpoint mode) and definitely not this game.
Touhou has been pretty samey since DDC. This game continues that. Most mid one yet. Zun needs to get his shit together for the next one. The AI stuff doesn't help either.

This game was satisfying, relaxing, and seemed to be well-made. There are a few things that are a little rough, like having to click on the letters rather than the entire owl or the wax stamp, but the rest of the game is actually very forgiving. There's no requirement of how to align the spell symbols, very efficient hints (later spells take two smaller spells to make, and one hint reveals one of those spells), and easy-to-learn mechanics. There were some requests that acted as puzzles, and I felt engaged the entire time.

The one problem, which I learned after I played it, is that most of the letter art is ai-generated. This is unfortunate, especially because the rest of the game seems to have heart. I enjoyed the game largely because I did not know this while playing.
