So incredibly dissapointing to see the use of AI art. It's especially disheartening because it's used in places where there doesnt even need to be any art or images, and it would be completely fine with just the text. If the devs just removed the AI art then this game would be fine.

While the game itself was reasonably fun, I am ultimately going to put a thumbs down for the rampant use of AI 'art'. Yes, it's a free game, but the principle remains the same: the more AI art is used in games without anyone fighting back, the more normalised the idea becomes and the more acceptable devs and publishers on the other end of the scale (looking at you, triple A industry) will think it is to screw artists over. While AI has its uses, it should be used responsibly - and definitely not in a way that devalues the work of real living human beings.

Especially because the game doesn't really *need* the art to work? It's all done through letters, which can be understood just fine without pictures, and the few diagram puzzles that actually *do* require visual aids can all be generated in-game without AI. People typically do not spend hours painstakingly drawing pictures of what they've seen in letters. I suppose they would if they had magic scrolls that could draw the art for them, but then I imagine the artists in that fantasy world might get rather upset about it.

For a game with a story that harps on about how important memory, emotion and creativity are, it's rather ironic that the devs chose to use so liberally a tool that stomps all over precisely those virtues.

AI slop. That's the best I can call it. It has some interesting ideas, it almost reminds me of that one alchemy game where you make potions. But the awful ai images and more importantly the ai letters you receive are thoughtless, souless and so vague that most of the times you just guessing what the character wants, and you bet you will be getting them all incorrectly. 

I wouldn't recommend this to anyone until they fix their laziness and hire real artists to work on it. If they couldn't be bothered to fully make their game, why would I be bothred to play it for more than ten minutes?

While the game concept is creative and the gameplay is fun, ai slop is used for some visiuals when it didnt need to be used. Because of these uses I wouldnt be suprised if the whole game was written with ai which really spoiled the whole immersion in the game for me.

AI slop. I was intrigued by the premise, but the spell creation system lacks any engaging depth, the writing is poor, and it uses AI-generated slop assets (that are extremely obvious and low quality, I might add). 

Overall, in my personal opinion, it's just not worth the time of anyone in the target demographic of this sort of game, nor is it long, polished, or varied enough to be the sort of cosy timesink one might expect from games like Potion Craft: Alchemist Simulator. There are only 14 levels in total (in-game days, the game takes place over the course of a fortnight) with 30 spell combinations derived from combining different variations of the 4 cardinal elements of fire, earth, water, and air and augmenting them with higher strength or longer duration to meet the needs of your customers.
The premise is very interesting and I would very much like to see it executed more effectively by a developer with ethics and integrity. Requests you recieve are very simple but increase in complexity and specificity over the course of the game. They seem to be scripted and linear, or at least they feel that way compared to other similar games that I have played, making Enscroll more of a puzzle game cosplaying as a more complex game than it actually is by enrobing itself in the flavour of an intriguing concept that I personally think it fails to deliver on. 
It definitely has potential, but I would not recommend Enscroll to any prospective players as the developer's lack of integrity suggests that it is unlikely that the game will be improved or expanded upon sufficiently enough to be worth recommending.

Don't get me wrong, personally I would love to give this game a positive review. Purely out of respect for the CoasterGalaxyWorld group, who really know how to put their hearts into creating a good project. But I also have to be honest: considering the price I paid for this game, this is disappointing.
The attractions look quite nice. You can really see that they put a lot of effort into them. However, it's the game mechanics that are unfortunately unstable. 
I encountered a few bugs here and there. In Mega Dance, for example, the sound was completely distorted, with the motor revving like crazy while the ride was stationary. Also the NPC's are sometimes floating on the ground, which looks a little weird.
Then there's the fact that the game crashed several times at random moments. When I want to control something, when I load an audio track, when I'm just walking around, and even when I close the game. 
I play MSFS2024 in my spare time, purely to give an example of what my PC can handle.
And personally, I find the GUI design disappointing. It's a bit messy. You could say that I should expect that from a smaller game, but considering the price I paid for it, I expected something of higher quality. 
Suppose I had paid around 15 euros for this, then that would have been somewhat acceptable given the current state of the game. I understand that a first game is mainly intended to try things out, but if you ask people to pay a fairly high price, they expect a little more quality. But I hope the developers will look into this.
For now, I don't recommend the game. At least not at the current price point. However, I will keep an eye on the updates and adjust the review if I think it's necessary.

Unfortunately, I can't recommend the game right now. I believe the price is way too high for what you are getting. I do hope CGW will add some more rides and maybe offer a bit better quality and then I'm sure this will be the best fair ride sim we'll have available.

If I had known that they would release almost the exact same game again in 2025 (TM25), I wouldn’t have bought this one. TM24 isn’t a bad game, but it already feels outdated the moment you start, because TM25 is right around the corner.

Some of the features you miss from TM25 make TM24 feel incomplete, and the achievements they added to TM24 are somewhat unrealistic – requiring an estimated 1000 hours of playtime. That makes the game feel more like a grind than an enjoyable career journey.

Overall, the game itself works fine, but it’s not designed for a single, satisfying playthrough. My advice: don’t buy TM24 or TM25. Wait until TM26, when (hopefully) they’ll bring real improvements.

game_name: What is in the Black Box?
review_text:
"What is in the Black Box?" is a set of short simple puzzles that allows you to interact with a Steam inventory item... and the reward for finishing this short tech demo?  You get advertised to.  Yes, the Russian asset flippers at SQRT are pushing an advertisement on Steam, for money, with fake/paid reviews on it, so they can then sell another product to try to get your money for a bunch of Unity asset store assets. 

Let's put the disgusting advertising aspect of this aside and consider what you get for buying this simplistic minimalist "puzzle" advert.

From a technical perspective, the game doesn't meet basic minimum requirements that most PC gamers expect as standard.

There's no option to change the resolution and no useful graphics tweaks.  There's no way to ensure this is running at the native resolution of your display.  There's no guarantee this game will look right on any PC as a result of this hamfisted design decision.

The game features lazy minimalist/untextured low-polygon "retro" assets and visuals, making this look like a barely functional 3D game from the early 1990s.  The lack of textures is a method that lazy devs often use to disguise their lack of talent/interest in doing the graphics properly and trying to disguise it under the name of "art", or "We made it look bad on purpose", which really isn't something gamers should have to put up with.  It's unclear why the developers weren't willing to arrange high quality, high polygon count contemporary assets and high resolution textures for the game.  It looks bad as a result of their decisions, and that's just another reason to avoid it.

The controls can't be customised because the game has such a dumbed down, simplified interface that it's just iPhone screen tapping stuff.  The fact that the interface is this dumbed down might be seen as a problem in itself, however... this is a fairly shallow experience if you're the kind of gamer that likes to play games with deep, rich control schemes and interaction.  You'll get none of that here.

This looks and feels like a mobile app, but I wasn't able to find it on the app stores.  Maybe it was removed, maybe it was rejected by Apple and Google (they do have more rigorous quality standards than Valve does for Steam, after all).

Regardless, for all intents and purposes "What is in the Black Box?" might as well be a mobile app, it has the same limitations and dumbed down qualities.  It's impossible to recommend such a game to PC gamers.  We don't spend all this money building gaming rigs so we can pretend they're iPhones and play games that might as well be mobile apps.

These technical defects push this game below acceptable standards for any modern PC game.

"What is in the Black Box?" didn't appeal much to the people who own a copy of the game, either.  It has achievements, and they show us a very clear picture that the game didn't really capture any interest from gamers.  The most commonly and easily attained achievement is "Get a Black Box", starting the advertising, trivial to get, but less than 3 percent of players bothered to get that far before uninstalling the game.  Hardly a success story, even the people who own this game weren't interested in it.

The poor quality of this game is also reflected by how many people spent time with it.  At the time of this review, SteamDB shows the all-time peak player number was [b]only 4 players[/b].  This is a remarkably low number, and now, the only player activity occurs once or twice a month, presumably someone loading it up to see what it is then quickly uninstalling it.  Considering there's over 120 million gamers on Steam and well over 130,000 games for gamers to choose from, the overwhelming lack of interest in this low quality game is to be expected.

So, should you buy this advertisement for another game?  Is this one of the best of the 130,000+ games on Steam?

"What is in the Black Box?" is relatively cheap at $2 USD, but it's not worth it. Given the defects and quality issues with the game, coupled with the unrealistic price, this is impossible to recommend.  Because this is the kind of game you can just play for free on mobile phones, it's impossible to recommend anyone should pay money for the same experience on Steam.  This is also competing with over 14,000 free games available on Steam, many of them far better than this paid product.

It's important to note that, despite this being such a low quality product, there's a number of positive reviews that all appeared immediately after the game launched, from suspiciously inactive Steam accounts that have reviewed almost no other games apart from this.  On a purely unrelated note, recent US FTC rulings mean that review manipulation and fake reviews are now federal crimes.  Interesting, isn't it?  Regardless... take the review score of this game with a grain of salt.

Valve have marked this game as "Profile Features Limited" at the time of this review.  This is usually caused by poor sales figures and low community acceptance for the game (to date).  Until this status changes, this game will not give you +1 to your Game Collector badge count, appear in profile achievements or any other Steam meta-accomplishments, nor can it be displayed in some profile showcases.  If these factors are important to you, it may be worth holding off before buying this game.[/quote]

game_name: What is in the Black Box?
review_text:
"What is in the Black Box?" is a set of short simple puzzles that allows you to interact with a Steam inventory item... and the reward for finishing this short tech demo?  You get advertised to.  Yes, the Russian asset flippers at SQRT are pushing an advertisement on Steam, for money, with fake/paid reviews on it, so they can then sell another product to try to get your money for a bunch of Unity asset store assets. 

Let's put the disgusting advertising aspect of this aside and consider what you get for buying this simplistic minimalist "puzzle" advert.

From a technical perspective, the game doesn't meet basic minimum requirements that most PC gamers expect as standard.

There's no option to change the resolution and no useful graphics tweaks.  There's no way to ensure this is running at the native resolution of your display.  There's no guarantee this game will look right on any PC as a result of this hamfisted design decision.

The game features lazy minimalist/untextured low-polygon "retro" assets and visuals, making this look like a barely functional 3D game from the early 1990s.  The lack of textures is a method that lazy devs often use to disguise their lack of talent/interest in doing the graphics properly and trying to disguise it under the name of "art", or "We made it look bad on purpose", which really isn't something gamers should have to put up with.  It's unclear why the developers weren't willing to arrange high quality, high polygon count contemporary assets and high resolution textures for the game.  It looks bad as a result of their decisions, and that's just another reason to avoid it.

There's no real reason to switch from the hand drawn kits to AI generated kits. If I noticed that when I was buying this game, I wouldn't have bought this. 

Besides that, the painting mechanic is boring, there's no depth too it, just click and boom you're done. The process of building the models is janky and mildly aggravating. The audio is satisfying but if that's all I wanted I'd watch somebody else play this game. 

TLDR: Buy a different model building game, this one lacks depth and crutches on AI to create content.

I bought this game a while ago under the impression that all of the art/models for the game were hand-crafted by artist and fans of the model-building community. That is not the case as now revealed by the developer on the store page. 

If I had known that AI-generated content was in the game, I wouldn't have wasted my time/money on it. At the very least, the developers should update the store page description and remove the part about being "hand crafted". Personally, I'm going to try and refund the game.

I had put many hours into this game when it was first made, but after the dlcs came out, they were lazier than the original content and some even being AI, I will not pay for AI anything and if I ever support someone using AI to make money, it was a mistake, so just know, this is scummy, don't buy the game unless the AI is removed.

Very disappointed to discover AI was used in this game's assets. This game is relaxing and I have enjoyed my time with it, but I would not have purchased or supported the game had I known AI was involved. I suggest trying another kit building game or just building some cheap and easy HGs irl.

While the developer has stated they only apply AI-generated graphic art for the game, AI images are known to be messy and the detailing is often just a kind of noise pattern it tries to follow, whereas it would be much more coherent if all the designs were created by hand. I bought the game and its DLC under the impression they were all hand-crafted as advertised, but that was only a half-truth in that some were drawn and the rest are generated.

Needless to say, I am thoroughly disappointed by what I've found in this game and I unfortunately cannot recommend paying money to play this game or continue supporting it to others I know. Were it true to its word, this would've been a much different story.

The gameplay itself is fun, however considering the use of generative AI I cannot recommend it. Being "given" generated designs by contributors by mistake is one thing, letting them stay in the game after finding it out is a different can of worms - especially when using a video of someone actually drawing as promotion material.

played this game in demo, it was fun. straight to the point gunplay builder. it wasn't that deep but was cute and relaxing. the controls weren't so janky. i hadn't played it since then because of life stuff. i came back to it to not only find it full of ai kits, but the game crashed my pc twice. a memory leak of some sort. i'll start building and then it'll piss memory until my screen is completely unsuable but i can still hear things.

Initially when I bought this game I would have recommended it as a relaxing, fun lil kit builder, great for quick builds to destress. However, I honestly can't recommend this game anymore since the updates/new DLC. Stating on their page that  "All models and pieces have been carefully crafted for a relaxing experience." along side a timelapse of one being drawn, while accepting and implementing  generative Ai kits from the community is two-faced. 

The generative AI kits themselves are terrible and provide nothing entertaining or joyful to the game itself. It is disappointing to see devs favour Ai slop over creating their own assets (or community artists who do). This is a game that will sadly sit shelved as it no longer provides the charm and joy it originally did.

i mean for a dollars i guess its ok but not really worth your time tbh game quality is pretty poor overall even if i think the graphic are good for something indie but yea overall 5.5 out of 10

This is barely a game. You go from Point A to B only to return to A and back to B, this back tracking serves no purpose other than prolonging the “game play” time. This is a glorified visual novel at best.

The artwork is beautiful. Although others have said it looks like AI... I don't know enough to know. 

However this story.... is very one note.. even playing through a few of the choices. Very much Madonna/whore complex, man the center of the universe stuff. Nothing really new to it. 

If you're into that.... go for it, it's short and has a few different options. 

If that trope is a bit tired to you, skip this one.

The game initially was fun, entertaining and interesting to play, it used to be everywhere because of the humour and content you could get out of the game. But I don't know what happened, but the game dissolved into shit. AI got stupider, became harder to play, not out of choice, purely because bugs RIDDLES the entire game, Unless they do a massive ass bug fix, I have an unchanged opinion. Quite simply. 

Edit: After a little longer period of time playing this game, I can provide a slightly more helpful review, both for developers, consumers, and people wishing to play the game.


The premise couldn't get any better, a custom ai that speaks to you with a unique story and narrative, making every playthrough funny, amusing and unique? Sign me up! Is what I thought when I was buying the game. And it was fun, for the first 2 minutes. The Creativity of the AI? None, almost as if It's following a script, Realism? Christ, I'm scared to tell you the full story, inaccurate, bland, miserable, It makes the idea of a fun unique game every time seem like fantasy land when you can't even handle a single conversation (which also takes around 5 years to actually finish, because the AI cannot comprehend when to accept/deny invite in unless you say some extremely clear words, like you're speaking to a little toddler. It took me a bombardment of vulgar insults to the AI to close the door. Try it yourself, if you have the game. Start it up, walk to a door, start giving it insults, vulgar or non vulgar, I'm honestly convinced it doesn't matter, it'll take a whole eon for the AI to do the bare minimum. Oh, and forget beating them, I spent 23 minutes, 32 seconds (yes I'm a nerd, I timed it) speaking to Grandma Maude (One of the first people you talk to, and I think the easiest one to beat, according to this 'Code' that the developers scrawled into whatever software they used. And it took 23 minutes, of non stop back and forth to get this dumbass AI to finally figure out 'oh, I gotta end the cutscene now don't I?' even when it says 'come on in' or 'Take a step in' or whatever the hell, it doesn't end the convo, So I'm sitting there like 'So uhh... can I come in...?' For about half a decade too.

Oh, and the AI is just the start. Don't you worry, developers themselves made some major errors while programming too... Let's start with the main culprit, Love Bites, the revolutionized mode to make everyone have a 'great' time. I'm sure you know where this is going. While also carrying the same issues as the classical mode, Love bites is unique in the sense that it just doesn't work. I have 5 hours on the game, about 2 of that is testing Love Bites for this edit/review, and i can assure you, friends. It doesn't work, even when it states it will, the convo ends abruptly, and walk back to them and they act like you just pissed them off so hard you're now mortal enemies for this random ass ChatGPT AI with no ability to have a virtual conversation, oh, and this rap thing, Mic Drop, or something. Oh wow, assuming it's 3 modes for 4 pound, I wouldn't play it if you gave me money, AI system? Non-existent, Rating? Bias, fun? No, just no. It's like trying to rap to a wall, but that wall is backed by an entire AI system, because it doesn't understand 2 simple rules. FAIR PLAY. Talk about AI getting better, the only thing this game did well was make me have utter faith that AI won't take over our planet. 
So, for some that's something.

 Oh, and yeah, weird add on, Why the fuck is Kim-Jong-Un, Stalin, and other, yknow, Bad people for history teachers just in the game, no questions asked? Just, throwin' out there that they just rap about nukes, Real world atrocities and the Russia that was lead by the moustache man That just feels wrong to be in a game, no less on the biggest gaming platform arguably in the world. How desperate are you for the bag? Or is it just attention? Well, whichever it is, I find it really funny that nobody honestly cares. Look at people like, That Vegan Teacher, face it, she gets fame off of controversy. And usually, that strategy just, works. But this one just... didn't, because there's either too many things about this game to hate about, or nobody plays it enough to give a damn. I literally looked through dozens of reviews before editing mine, and not a single guy (or girl, or non-binary! Which is totally okay, js gotta put that out there, be you, respect.) mentioned that they just have dictators in the game. Which either means your fanbase is insane or my prior theories are just right. 


So for those people that are lazy and don't wanna read my rant, here's a recap. Classic Mode: Horrid, AI bland, stupid and always like they're staring at a wall. Love bites, same 'luxuries' as Classic AI, but is just unworkable, and even if it does it sometimes just doesn't play, Mic Drop, boring, bland and blah blah blah, you get the drill, but bias AI and can't rap for life. Oh, and there's real world dictators, for no reason at an attempt of a sad hatewatch revival, spoiler, just didnt work.





I made my point clear, bland, horrible, severe need of changing and AI editing to improve the AI to make this playable. Yes it's blunt, Yes it's harsh, Some might even call it Rude. But my opinion is my opinion lad, deal with it.

The game is infested with bugs, love bites it still broken and unplayable, and we haven't received an update in 3 months. It's starting to look like the developer has abandoned the game. Can't recommend in the current state.

I loved ur singing in the third grade" and it will say something like "Hey Mister Sony Toprano! I loved your Linging inn the turd grade!" Although it seems exciting at the start it just gets boring.

I really wanted to enjoy this game because the concept is great, but after about 2 hours of playtime I ran into too many issues to justify the current price.

1. Audio bugs
The main menu music randomly disappears, and sometimes there’s no in-game audio at all. It only comes back if I start messing with the audio settings, and even then it occasionally drops out again.

2. Character customization issues
Several customization icons are completely blank/purple, as if the textures are missing. It makes the character creator feel unfinished.

3. Gameplay-breaking bugs
When I entered Rusty’s house, my character froze completely — WASD didn’t work, none of the buttons responded, and after a few seconds my character just fell to the floor like a ragdoll. Restarted the game twice and it happened again. Only on the third restart did it stop.

All of this happened within the first two hours. The idea behind the game is fun seriously, but in its current state it's extremely buggy and not worth 15€ until the devs fix these issues. Refunded for now  I’ll gladly buy it again and  change my review to positive once the game gets some needed updates for the issues i mentioned.

This game has SUCH great potential, but it lacks the AI intelligence to make it great. I only got this game because greenskull played it and the interactions felt so much more realistic. Now it seems like what you say to the characters only matters if it fills in blanks within a script. I would pay for AI tokens to have a realistic interaction with characters, no problem. But the way it is now, you are talking to a character who has predetermined goals and simplistic AI that only responds when you speak the exact way you're intended to.

I think this game should have two tiers in the settings: 1 (simplistic free AI that is just realistic enough) 2: (paid tokens and realistic interactions that make sense.) 

Let's look at Love Bites.
You talk to a character, convince them to break up, and you get no letter. The AI response feels like a break up letter is about to be written, but none is given. The game seems to choose keywords instead of actual AI interpretation. 

The standard game mode:
You fully convince someone to the point where they say "slide in" or "door's cracked," but because it was worded in a way where the game doesn't understand the character is letting the player in, it's not recognized and you have to keep repeating yourself "ok let's head inside" a thousand times until it's recognized.

I feel like this could be fixed by adding a prefix to the prompt like "add <allowed-inside/> to the end of the response to grant access to the building." Same with love bytes, similar response but "to the end of the response to grant break-up."

Also, the breakup responses in Love Bites are super disappointed. The characters ask what they should say to the partner but instead of actually saying what you say, they seem to say some predefined script. This is different from the alpha builds that youtube creator's have played, where they actually respond with the points you told the character to

Overall, this game has huge potential, but it has these huge flaws that I know me and other players would be willing to pay for the AI tokens to be fixed, but there's no option to.

Again, a free tier (obviously not considering the base price of the game) and an advanced AI tier would really improve this game.

"蒸汽麻將社" is quite simply a mobile app-tier trashware "game" with gambling, intended to prey on gamers.  While it's advertised as "Free" to play, it isn't, you must buy chips to continue playing after you inevitably lose... after all, odds are in the houses favour (and the houses bots).  The developer claims it's not gambling because you can't cash out, so all the money they try to coerce you into buying "chips" with is gone for good.  I don't see how that's better or worse than just going to a casino. 

I'm at a loss for words at how such an evil, unethical system as organised online gambling could jam its foot into the door here on Steam, at a time when loot boxes and other anti-gamer, predatory exploitation systems are being investigated by governments around the world. 

There's plenty of accusations of unethical fixing of the odds here, and while they're not substantiated, I find it difficult to believe these people peddling gambling and trying to inflict this on the innocent population of Steam gamers on PC are bastions of honesty and integrity.  So if you think sending thousands of dollars to these people for virtual chips you can gamble away is a good idea, maybe give that some introspection before buying any of the chips they constantly spam you with offers to buy in this advertising riddled mobile app like casino. 

There's no English translation for the game, but that's okay.  The language of bad games and scams is universal.

As mentioned, this features really godawful, "wrong" looking AI slop instead of genuinely and sincerely created game assets.  It's hard to say if this was because the developer didn't have the skill to do the job of creating real game assets or couldn't afford to hire someone who does, but it also doesn't matter.  What matters is that this looks bad because of that decision.  AI slop sticks out like a sore thumb... and it comes across as insulting.

There's also the old adage that those who lack the budget and talent to make their own game assets usually lack the budget and talent to do the rest of the job of game development properly, too, and that's glaringly obvious here.

On pricing and value for gamers, this isn't really free as the game is infested with mobile app store like microtransactions and online gambling.  This is a consumer hostile model that we don't need to see on PC.  It's deceptively priced to gouge gamers, and that's a reason in itself to avoid this.

Even though this is "free", it failed to appeal to the 120 million+ gamers on Steam.  SteamDB showed the game peaked at just a dozen or so concurrent players, and now there's basically nobody playing it.  Even though it's free.  This is a dismal public reception, but it's easy to see why gamers rejected it.

Bearing in mind that free games don't add +1 to your game collector count, should you bother adding this to your library and downloading it?

Nope.  This isn't worth downloading.  While this didn't cost me any money up front, I still feel ripped off.

This game had the shell of a great COD game but fumbled the bag with everything else. The gunplay is good, they feel good to shoot, not too realistic that it feels annoying like MWII but they dont feel like plastic like BOCW. The gunsmith is good, prestige being back is good, the new perk specialities are good, etc. There are some parts of the gameplay that are bad. I didn't play post launch much but the map pool sucks ass, the scorestreaks are underwhelming and cost way too much, etc. 
But the main problem are the surrounding aspects. The main is SBMM. This matchmaking system makes the game not fun to play at all and sweaty as hell. If ur average-above average its almost impossible to play casually. It also makes your connection horrible. The game is filled with dumbass skins. A 90's gulf war game has fucking beavis and butthead in it ffs. The game is monetized to high hell, it's full price and monetized like a f2p game. A 40$ battle pass every season, a 10$ event pass for each event, 20$ bundles, etc. The servers are horrible, when I use my ethernet the internet crashes a few times every hour, and that instantly kicks you out of the game. When I use my wifi, the game has insane rubber banding, which has been an issue for me since COD Vanguard in 2021 that they haven't fixed. The menus are extremely laggy and its the worst optimized AAA game I've seen on PC. The Steam version is even worse optimized than other versions like battle.net and Xbox app.
Overall I def would not recommend buying it, especially if you are on Steam.

I'm convinced at this point a large part of sales represents a market of nostalgic players. It makes sense to want money, it's a business, but this has become absolutely distasteful and honestly disrespectful at this point. My game shouldn't feel like I'm a tourist surrounded by scammers and street vendors shoving product in my face while I try to admire the architecture.  Not to mention the new BO7 feels like a DLC to BO6 but its a whole ass new game with a price tag to reflect that. The lack of respect for players because they makes sales regardless will only last so long. It's a short term game plan if something doesn't change.  Old habits (buying new COD games in this case) may die hard for some, but they will eventually die. Devs need to wake up.

I only play multiplayer so this review is just a review of that and not the entire product. Black Ops 6 was fun on launch. The omni-movement is pretty fun, the new set of guns and the graphics are stellar. There are some really good maps like Skyline but as a whole I found most of the maps in this game to be somewhat basic. Black Ops 6 is not bad per se and was decently fun while it lasted, but I don't see myself playing it for years to come and it is largely overshadowed by the much bigger and better Black Ops 7. My biggest problem with BO6 was its lackluster live service. While the content released throughout its life cycle was consistently of high quality, coming off of MW3 there just wasn't enough of it to keep me invested. It is clear in retrospect that Treyarch was saving much of their content and effort for BO7 which is a much bigger and better game; and what I would recommend to anyone looking for the best modern COD multiplayer.
